          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=Despicable+Me+2+%282013%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Ftitle%2Ftt1690953%2F&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-lion_king-93">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-lion_king_93-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-lion_king_93-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <i>
           The Lion King
          </i>
          <ul>
           <li>
            Total:
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation web cs1">
             <a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/movies/?id=lionking.htm" rel="nofollow">
              "The Lion King (1994)"
             </a>
             .
             <i>
              <a href="/wiki/Box_Office_Mojo" title="Box Office Mojo">
               Box Office Mojo
              </a>
             </i>
             <span class="reference-accessdate">
              . Retrieved
              <span class="nowrap">
               May 29,
              </span>
              2016
             </span>
             .
             <q>
              Worldwide: $968,483,777; Production Budget: $45 million
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=The+Lion+King+%281994%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Fmovies%2F%3Fid%3Dlionking.htm&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
           <li>
            Lifetime grosses (US &amp; Canada):
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation web cs1">
             <a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/movies/?page=releases&amp;id=lionking.htm" rel="nofollow">
              "The Lion King (1994) – Release Summary"
             </a>
             .
             <i>
              Box Office Mojo
             </i>
             <span class="reference-accessdate">
              . Retrieved
              <span class="nowrap">
               May 29,
              </span>
              2016
             </span>
             .
             <q>
              Initial: $312,855,561; IMAX: $15,686,215; 3D: $94,242,001
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=The+Lion+King+%281994%29+%E2%80%93+Release+Summary&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Fmovies%2F%3Fpage%3Dreleases%26id%3Dlionking.htm&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
           <li>
            Lifetime grosses (outside U.S. &amp; Canada):
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation web cs1" id="CITEREFBrevert2016">
             Brevert, Brad (May 29, 2016).
             <a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/news/?id=4191" rel="nofollow">
              "
              <span class="cs1-kern-left">
               '
              </span>
              X-Men' &amp; 'Alice' Lead Soft Memorial Day Weekend; Disney Tops $4 Billion Worldwide"
             </a>
             .
             <i>
              Box Office Mojo
             </i>
             <span class="reference-accessdate">
              . Retrieved
              <span class="nowrap">
               May 29,
              </span>
              2016
             </span>
             .
             <q>
              ...the original release of The Lion King made $450.6 million internationally, an additional $3.8 million with the 2002 IMAX reissue, and another $91.3 million from 2011's 3D reissue for an international total of $545.7 million.
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=%27X-Men%27+%26+%27Alice%27+Lead+Soft+Memorial+Day+Weekend%3B+Disney+Tops+%244+Billion+Worldwide&amp;rft.date=2016-05-29&amp;rft.aulast=Brevert&amp;rft.aufirst=Brad&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Fnews%2F%3Fid%3D4191&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
           <li>
            As of 2010:
            <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
             Block &amp; Wilson 2010
            </a>
            , p.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA764" rel="nofollow">
             764
            </a>
            . "Production Cost: $79.3 (Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-125">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-125">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFMonaco2009">
           Monaco, James (2009).
           <i>
            How to Read a Film:Movies, Media, and Beyond
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Oxford_University_Press" title="Oxford University Press">
            Oxford University Press
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=bgbOsjnppAcC&amp;pg=PA262" rel="nofollow">
            262
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-19-975579-0" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-19-975579-0">
            <bdi>
             978-0-19-975579-0
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            The Birth of a Nation, costing an unprecedented and, many believed, thoroughly foolhardy $110,000, eventually returned $20 million and more. The actual figure is hard to calculate because the film was distributed on a "states' rights" basis in which licenses to show the film were sold outright. The actual cash generated by The Birth of a Nation may have been as much as $50 million to $100 million, an almost inconceivable amount for such an early film.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=How+to+Read+a+Film%3AMovies%2C+Media%2C+and+Beyond&amp;rft.pages=262&amp;rft.pub=Oxford+University+Press&amp;rft.date=2009&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-19-975579-0&amp;rft.aulast=Monaco&amp;rft.aufirst=James&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Wasko-126">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-Wasko_126-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-Wasko_126-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFWasko1986">
           Wasko, Janet (1986). "D.W. Griffiths and the banks: a case study in film financing".  In Kerr, Paul (ed.).
           <i>
            The Hollywood Film Industry: A Reader
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Routledge" title="Routledge">
            Routledge
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jMINAAAAQAAJ&amp;pg=PA34" rel="nofollow">
            34
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-7100-9730-9" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-7100-9730-9">
            <bdi>
             978-0-7100-9730-9
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            Various accounts have cited $15 to $18 million profits during the first few years of release, while in a letter to a potential investor in the proposed sound version, Aitken noted that a $15 to $18 million box-office gross was a 'conservative estimate'. For years
            <i>
             Variety
            </i>
            has listed
            <i>
             The Birth of a Nation'
            </i>
            s total rental at $50 million. (This reflects the total amount paid to the distributor, not box-office gross.) This 'trade legend' has finally been acknowledged by
            <i>
             Variety
            </i>
            as a 'whopper myth', and the amount has been revised to $5 million. That figure seems far more feasible, as reports of earnings in the Griffith collection list gross receipts for 1915–1919 at slightly more than $5.2 million (including foreign distribution) and total earnings after deducting general office expenses, but not royalties, at about $2 million.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=bookitem&amp;rft.atitle=D.W.+Griffiths+and+the+banks%3A+a+case+study+in+film+financing&amp;rft.btitle=The+Hollywood+Film+Industry%3A+A+Reader&amp;rft.pages=34&amp;rft.pub=Routledge&amp;rft.date=1986&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-7100-9730-9&amp;rft.aulast=Wasko&amp;rft.aufirst=Janet&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-127">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-127">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFLang1994">
           Lang, Robert, ed. (1994).
           <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/isbn_9780813520278/page/30" rel="nofollow">
            <i>
             The Birth of a nation: D.W. Griffith, director
            </i>
           </a>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Rutgers_University_Press" title="Rutgers University Press">
            Rutgers University Press
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=rKOgVk13vawC&amp;pg=PA30" rel="nofollow">
            30
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-8135-2027-8" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-8135-2027-8">
            <bdi>
             978-0-8135-2027-8
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            The film eventually cost $110,000 and was twelve reels long.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+Birth+of+a+nation%3A+D.W.+Griffith%2C+director&amp;rft.pages=30&amp;rft.pub=Rutgers+University+Press&amp;rft.date=1994&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-8135-2027-8&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Farchive.org%2Fdetails%2Fisbn_9780813520278%2Fpage%2F30&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-BlockWilson26-128">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson26_128-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson26_128-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA26" rel="nofollow">
           26
          </a>
          .
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             Intolerance
            </i>
            : "Domestic Rentals: $1.0 (Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Cleopatra
            </i>
            : "Domestic Rentals: $0.5; Production Cost: $0.3 (Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-129">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-129">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFBirchard2010">
           Birchard, Robert S. (2010),
           <i>
            Intolerance
           </i>
           , p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA45" rel="nofollow">
            45
           </a>
           ,
           <q>
            Intolerance was the most expensive American film made up until that point, costing a total of $489,653, and its performance at the box ... but it did recoup its cost and end with respectable overall numbers.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Intolerance&amp;rft.pages=45&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Birchard&amp;rft.aufirst=Robert+S.&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
          In:
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          .
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-mickey-130">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-mickey_130-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFCoons1939">
           Coons, Robin (June 30, 1939). "Hollywood Chatter".
           <i>
            <a href="/wiki/The_Daytona_Beach_News-Journal" title="The Daytona Beach News-Journal">
             The Daytona Beach News-Journal
            </a>
           </i>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://news.google.com/newspapers?id=eHIoAAAAIBAJ&amp;sjid=C8cEAAAAIBAJ&amp;pg=4926%2C5479066" rel="nofollow">
            6
           </a>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Daytona+Beach+News-Journal&amp;rft.atitle=Hollywood+Chatter&amp;rft.pages=6&amp;rft.date=1939-06-30&amp;rft.aulast=Coons&amp;rft.aufirst=Robin&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-131">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-131">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFShipman1970">
           Shipman, David (1970).
           <i>
            The great movie stars: the golden years
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Crown_Publishing_Group" title="Crown Publishing Group">
            Crown Publishing Group
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/greatmoviestarst00ship" rel="nofollow">
            98
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            It was a low budgeter—$120,000—but it grossed world-wide over $3 million and made stars of Chaney and his fellow-players, Betty Compson and Thomas Meighan.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+great+movie+stars%3A+the+golden+years&amp;rft.pages=98&amp;rft.pub=Crown+Publishing+Group&amp;rft.date=1970&amp;rft.aulast=Shipman&amp;rft.aufirst=David&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>

         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">

           . June 21, 1932. p. 1.
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Variety&amp;rft.atitle=Biggest+Money+Pictures&amp;rft.pages=1&amp;rft.date=1932-06-21&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">

          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=Biggest+Money+Pictures&amp;rft.pub=Cinemaweb&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.cinemaweb.com%2Fsilentfilm%2Fbookshelf%2F7_v_32_4.htm&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">

        <li id="cite_note-Solomon_(2011)-133">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-Solomon_(2011)_133-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-Solomon_(2011)_133-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFSolomon2011">
           Solomon, Aubrey (2011).
           <i>
            The Fox Film Corporation, 1915–1935: A History and Filmography
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/McFarland_%26_Company" title="McFarland &amp; Company">
            McFarland &amp; Company
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-7864-6286-5" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-7864-6286-5">
            <bdi>
             978-0-7864-6286-5
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+Fox+Film+Corporation%2C+1915%E2%80%931935%3A+A+History+and+Filmography&amp;rft.pub=McFarland+%26+Company&amp;rft.date=2011&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-7864-6286-5&amp;rft.aulast=Solomon&amp;rft.aufirst=Aubrey&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             Way Down East
            </i>
            : p.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=zospQ7o5u0oC&amp;pg=PA52" rel="nofollow">
             52
            </a>
            . "D.W. Griffith's
            <i>
             Way Down East
            </i>
            (1920) was projected to return rentals of $4,000,000 on an $800,000 negative. This figure was based on the amounts earned from its roadshow run, coupled with its playoff in the rest of the country's theaters. Griffith had originally placed the potential film rental at $3,000,000 but, because of the success of the various roadshows that were running the $4,000,000 total was expected. The film showed a profit of $615,736 after just 23 weeks of release on a gross of $2,179,613."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             What Price Glory?
            </i>
            : p.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=zospQ7o5u0oC&amp;pg=PA122&amp;q=What%20Price%20Glory%20hit%20the%20jackpot%20with%20massive%20world%20rentals%20of%20%242%2C429%2C000%2C%20the%20highest%20figure%20in%20the%20history%20of%20the%20company.%20Since%20it%20was%20also%20the%20most%20expensive%20production%20of%20the%20year%20at%20%24817%2C000%20the%20profit%20was%20still%20a%20healthy%20%24796%2C000" rel="nofollow">
             112
            </a>
            . "What Price Glory hit the jackpot with massive world rentals of $2,429,000, the highest figure in the history of the company. Since it was also the most expensive production of the year at $817,000 the profit was still a healthy $796,000..."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Cavalcade
            </i>
            : p.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=zospQ7o5u0oC&amp;pg=PA170" rel="nofollow">
             170
            </a>
            . "The actual cost of
            <i>
             Cavalcade
            </i>
            was $1,116,000 and it was most definitely not guaranteed a success. In fact, if its foreign grosses followed the usual 40 percent of domestic returns, the film would have lost money. In a turnaround, the foreign gross was almost double the $1,000,000 domestic take to reach total world rentals of $3,000,000 and Fox's largest profit of the year at $664,000."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             State Fair
            </i>
            : p.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=zospQ7o5u0oC&amp;pg=PA170" rel="nofollow">
             170
            </a>
            . "
            <i>
             State Fair
            </i>
            did turn out to be a substantial hit with the help of Janet Gaynor boosting Will Rogers back to the level of money-making star. Its prestige engagements helped raked in a total $1,208,000 in domestic rentals. Surprisingly, in foreign countries unfamiliar with state fairs, it still earned a respectable $429,000. With its total rentals, the film ended up showing a $398,000 profit."
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-134">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-134">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">
           Hall &amp; Neale 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=Ro0hASPfC68C&amp;pg=PA53" rel="nofollow">
           53
          </a>
          . "
          <i>
           The Four Forsemen of the Apocalypse
          </i>
          was to become Metro's most expensive production and one of the decade's biggest box-office hits. Its production costs have been estimated at "something between $600,000 and $800,000."
          <i>
           Variety
          </i>
          estimated its worldwide gross at $4 million in 1925 and at $5 million in 1944; in 1991, it estimated its cumulative domestic rentals at $3,800,000."
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-135">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-135">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFBrownlow1968">
           Brownlow, Kevin (1968).
           <i>
            The parade's gone by
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/University_of_California_Press" title="University of California Press">
            University of California Press
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=wCD5EH64Qw8C&amp;pg=PA255" rel="nofollow">
            255
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-520-03068-8" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-520-03068-8">
            <bdi>
             978-0-520-03068-8
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            The negative cost was about $986,000, which did not include Fairbanks' own salary. Once the exploitation and release prints were taken into account,
            <i>
             Robin Hood
            </i>
            cost about $1,400,000—exceeding both
            <i>
             Intolerance
            </i>
            ($700,000) and the celebrated "million dollar movie"
            <i>
             Foolish Wives
            </i>
            . But it earned $2,500,000.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+parade%27s+gone+by+..&amp;rft.pages=255&amp;rft.pub=University+of+California+Press&amp;rft.date=1968&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-520-03068-8&amp;rft.aulast=Brownlow&amp;rft.aufirst=Kevin&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-136">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-136">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFVance2008">
           Vance, Jeffrey (2008).
           <i>
            Douglas Fairbanks
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/University_of_California_Press" title="University of California Press">
            University of California Press
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=e3DVBxGQ95YC&amp;pg=PA146" rel="nofollow">
            146
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-520-25667-5" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-520-25667-5">
            <bdi>
             978-0-520-25667-5
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            The film had a production cost of $930,042.78—more than the cost of D.W. Griffith's
            <i>
             Intolerance
            </i>
            and nearly as much as Erich von Stroheim's
            <i>
             Foolish Wives
            </i>
            (1922).
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Douglas+Fairbanks&amp;rft.pages=146&amp;rft.pub=University+of+California+Press&amp;rft.date=2008&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-520-25667-5&amp;rft.aulast=Vance&amp;rft.aufirst=Jeffrey&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Time_(1925)-137">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-Time_(1925)_137-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-Time_(1925)_137-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>

         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation journal cs1">
           <span class="cs1-lock-subscription" title="Paid subscription required">
            <a class="external text" href="http://www.time.com/time/magazine/article/0,9171,728553,00.html" rel="nofollow">
             "Business: Film Exports"
            </a>

           .
           <i>
            <a href="/wiki/Time_(magazine)" title="Time (magazine)">
             Time
            </a>

           . July 6, 1925.

         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Birchard_(2009)-138">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-Birchard_(2009)_138-0">
           <sup>

             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>

           <sup>
            <i>

           <sup>

              c

           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              d

          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-Birchard_(2009)_138-4">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              e
             </b>

         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFBirchard2009">
           Birchard, Robert S. (2009).
           <i>
            Cecil B. DeMille's Hollywood
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/University_Press_of_Kentucky" title="University Press of Kentucky">

           </a>

          </span>
          <ul>
           <li>
            ch.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=h8I1dEf7GqIC&amp;pg=PT104" rel="nofollow">
             45. The Ten Commandments
            </a>
            (1923). "Cost: $1,475,836.93; Gross: $4,169,798.38"
           </li>
           <li>
            ch.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=h8I1dEf7GqIC&amp;pg=PT138" rel="nofollow">
             56. The Sign of the Cross
            </a>
            . "Cost: $694,064.67; Gross: $2,738,993.35 (to 1937)"
           </li>
           <li>
            ch.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=h8I1dEf7GqIC&amp;pg=PT177" rel="nofollow">
             68. Samson and Delilah
            </a>
            . "Cost: $3,097,563.05"
           </li>
           <li>
            ch.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=h8I1dEf7GqIC&amp;pg=PT181" rel="nofollow">
             69. The Greatest Show on Earth
            </a>
            . "Cost: $3,873,946.50; Gross receipts: $15,797,396.36 (to December 29, 1962)"
           </li>
           <li>
            ch.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=h8I1dEf7GqIC&amp;pg=PT185" rel="nofollow">
             70. The Ten Commandments
            </a>
            (1956). "Cost: $13,272,381.87; Gross receipts: $90,066,230.00 (to June 23, 1979)"
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-May_(2005)-139">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-May_(2005)_139-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFMay2005">
           May, Richard P. (Fall 2005), "Restoring The Big Parade",
           <i>
            The Moving Image
           </i>
           ,
           <b>
            5
           </b>
           (2): 140–146,
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Doi_(identifier)" title="Doi (identifier)">
            doi
           </a>
           :
           <a class="external text" href="https://doi.org/10.1353%2Fmov.2005.0033" rel="nofollow">
            10.1353/mov.2005.0033
           </a>
           ,
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISSN_(identifier)" title="ISSN (identifier)">
            ISSN
           </a>
           <a class="external text" href="//www.worldcat.org/issn/1532-3978" rel="nofollow">
            1532-3978
           </a>
           ,
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/S2CID_(identifier)" title="S2CID (identifier)">
            S2CID
           </a>
           <a class="external text" href="https://api.semanticscholar.org/CorpusID:192076406" rel="nofollow">
            192076406
           </a>
           ,
           <q>
            ...earning somewhere between $18 and $22 million, depending on the figures consulted
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Moving+Image&amp;rft.atitle=Restoring+The+Big+Parade&amp;rft.ssn=fall&amp;rft.volume=5&amp;rft.issue=2&amp;rft.pages=140-146&amp;rft.date=2005&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fapi.semanticscholar.org%2FCorpusID%3A192076406%23id-name%3DS2CID&amp;rft.issn=1532-3978&amp;rft_id=info%3Adoi%2F10.1353%2Fmov.2005.0033&amp;rft.aulast=May&amp;rft.aufirst=Richard+P.&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-140">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-140">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFRobertson1991">
           Robertson, Patrick (1991).
           <i>
            Guinness Book of Movie Facts and Feats
           </i>
           (4 ed.).
           <a href="/wiki/Abbeville_Publishing_Group" title="Abbeville Publishing Group">
            Abbeville Publishing Group
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jdjsQY5qkbEC&amp;q=%22samson+and+delilah%22+worldwide+million+rentals" rel="nofollow">
            30
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-55859-236-0" title="Special:BookSources/978-1-55859-236-0">
            <bdi>
             978-1-55859-236-0
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            The top grossing silent film was King Vidor's
            <i>
             The Big Parade
            </i>
            (US 25), with worldwide rentals of $22 million.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Guinness+Book+of+Movie+Facts+and+Feats&amp;rft.pages=30&amp;rft.edition=4&amp;rft.pub=Abbeville+Publishing+Group&amp;rft.date=1991&amp;rft.isbn=978-1-55859-236-0&amp;rft.aulast=Robertson&amp;rft.aufirst=Patrick&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-141">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-141">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">
           Hall &amp; Neale 2010
          </a>
          , pp.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA58#v=twopage" rel="nofollow">
           58–59
          </a>
          . "Even then, at a time when the budget for a feature averaged at around $300,000, no more than $382,000 was spent on production...According to the Eddie Mannix Ledger at MGM, it grossed $4,990,000 domestically and $1,141,000 abroad."
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Ben-Hur_(1925)-142">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-Ben-Hur_(1925)_142-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1">
           <a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/35/Ben-Hur/notes.html" rel="nofollow">
            "Ben-Hur (1925) – Notes"
           </a>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">
            Turner Classic Movies
           </a>
           <span class="reference-accessdate">
            . Retrieved
            <span class="nowrap">
             December 19,
            </span>
            2017
           </span>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=Ben-Hur+%281925%29+%E2%80%93+Notes&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F35%2FBen-Hur%2Fnotes.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-HallNeale163-143">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale163_143-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">
           Hall &amp; Neale 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA163" rel="nofollow">
           163
          </a>
          . "MGM's silent
          <i>
           Ben-Hur
          </i>
          , which opened at the end of 1925, had out-grossed all the other pictures released by the company in 1926 combined. With worldwide rentals of $9,386,000 on first release it was, with the sole possible exception of
          <i>
           The Birth of a Nation
          </i>
          , the highest-earning film of the entire silent era. (At a negative cost of $3,967,000, it was also the most expensive.)"
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-144">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-144">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1" id="CITEREFMiller">
           Miller, Frank.
           <a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/410794/For-Heaven-s-Sake/articles.html" rel="nofollow">
            "For Heaven's Sake (1926) – Articles"
           </a>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">
            Turner Classic Movies
           </a>
           <span class="reference-accessdate">
            . Retrieved
            <span class="nowrap">
             January 15,
            </span>
            2012
           </span>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=For+Heaven%27s+Sake+%281926%29+%E2%80%93+Articles&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft.aulast=Miller&amp;rft.aufirst=Frank&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F410794%2FFor-Heaven-s-Sake%2Farticles.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-145">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-145">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFFinler2003">
           Finler 2003
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=rvVhEJmbfrsC&amp;pg=PA188#v=onepage&amp;q=wings%20%242%20million" rel="nofollow">
           188
          </a>
          . "At a cost of $2 million
          <i>
           Wings
          </i>
          was the studio's most expensive movie of the decade, and though it did well it was not good enough to earn a profit."
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Jolson-146">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-Jolson_146-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-Jolson_146-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <i>
           The Jazz Singer
          </i>
          and
          <i>
           The Singing Fool
          </i>
          <ul>
           <li>
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFBlock2010">
             Block, Hayley Taylor (2010),
             <i>
              The Jazz Singer
             </i>
             , p.
             <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA113" rel="nofollow">
              113
             </a>
             ,
             <q>
              The film brought in $2.6 million in worldwide rentals and made a net profit of $1,196,750. Jolson's follow-up Warner Bros. film,
              <i>
               The Singing Fool
              </i>
              (1928), brought in over two times as much, with $5.9 in worldwide rentals and a profit of $3,649,000, making them two of the most profitable films in the 1920s.
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+Jazz+Singer&amp;rft.pages=113&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Block&amp;rft.aufirst=Hayley+Taylor&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
            In:
            <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
             Block &amp; Wilson 2010
            </a>
            .
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Crafton-147">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-Crafton_147-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFCrafton1999">
           Crafton, Donald (1999).
           <i>
            The Talkies: American Cinema's Transition to Sound, 1926–1931
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/University_of_California_Press" title="University of California Press">
            University of California Press
           </a>
           . pp.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=KFB_oT-jupQC&amp;pg=PA549" rel="nofollow">
            549
           </a>
           –552.
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-520-22128-4" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-520-22128-4">
            <bdi>
             978-0-520-22128-4
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            <i>
             The Singing Fool
            </i>
            : Negative Cost ($1000s): 388
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+Talkies%3A+American+Cinema%27s+Transition+to+Sound%2C+1926%E2%80%931931&amp;rft.pages=549-552&amp;rft.pub=University+of+California+Press&amp;rft.date=1999&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-520-22128-4&amp;rft.aulast=Crafton&amp;rft.aufirst=Donald&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-148">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-148">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFBirchard2010">
           Birchard, Robert S. (2010),
           <i>
            The Broadway Melody
           </i>
           , p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA121" rel="nofollow">
            121
           </a>
           ,
           <q>
            It earned $4.4 million in worldwide rentals and was the first movie to spawn sequels (there were several until 1940).
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+Broadway+Melody&amp;rft.pages=121&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Birchard&amp;rft.aufirst=Robert+S.&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
          In:
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          .
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-bradley-149">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-bradley_149-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFBradley2004">
           Bradley, Edwin M. (2004) [1st. pub. 1996].
           <i>
            The First Hollywood Musicals: A Critical Filmography of 171 Features, 1927 Through 1932
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/McFarland_%26_Company" title="McFarland &amp; Company">
            McFarland &amp; Company
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-7864-2029-2" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-7864-2029-2">
            <bdi>
             978-0-7864-2029-2
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+First+Hollywood+Musicals%3A+A+Critical+Filmography+of+171+Features%2C+1927+Through+1932&amp;rft.pub=McFarland+%26+Company&amp;rft.date=2004&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-7864-2029-2&amp;rft.aulast=Bradley&amp;rft.aufirst=Edwin+M.&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             The Singing Fool
            </i>
            : p.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=Rg-UBJaPD-sC&amp;pg=PA12" rel="nofollow">
             12
            </a>
            . "Ego aside, Jolson was at the top of his powers in
            <i>
             The Singing Fool
            </i>
            . The $150,000 Warner Bros. paid him to make it, and the $388,000 it took to produce the film, were drops in the hat next to the film's world gross of $5.9 million. Its $3.8-million gross in this country set a box-office record that would not be surpassed until Walt Disney's
            <i>
             Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs
            </i>
            (1937)."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             The Broadway Melody
            </i>
            : p.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=Rg-UBJaPD-sC&amp;pg=PA24" rel="nofollow">
             24
            </a>
            . "
            <i>
             The Broadway Melody
            </i>
            with a negative cost of $379,000, grossed $2.8 million in the United States, $4.8 million worldwide, and made a recorded profit of $1.6 million for MGM."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Gold Diggers of Broadway
            </i>
            : p.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=Rg-UBJaPD-sC&amp;q=%22gold%20diggers%20of%20broadway%22%20worldwide" rel="nofollow">
             58
            </a>
            . "It grossed an impressive $2.5 million domestically and nearly $4 million worldwide."
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Solomon_(1988)-150">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-Solomon_(1988)_150-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-Solomon_(1988)_150-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-Solomon_(1988)_150-2">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              c
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFSolomon2002">
           Solomon, Aubrey (2002) [First published 1988].
           <i>
            Twentieth Century-Fox: a corporate and financial history
           </i>
           . Filmmakers series.
           <b>
            20
           </b>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Rowman_%26_Littlefield" title="Rowman &amp; Littlefield">
            Rowman &amp; Littlefield
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-8108-4244-1" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-8108-4244-1">
            <bdi>
             978-0-8108-4244-1
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Twentieth+Century-Fox%3A+a+corporate+and+financial+history&amp;rft.series=Filmmakers+series&amp;rft.pub=Rowman+%26+Littlefield&amp;rft.date=2002&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-8108-4244-1&amp;rft.aulast=Solomon&amp;rft.aufirst=Aubrey&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             Sunny Side Up
            </i>
            : p.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=WIZwZOz8LHsC&amp;pg=PA10" rel="nofollow">
             10
            </a>
            . "
            <i>
             Sunny Side Up
            </i>
            , a musical starring Janet Gaynor and Charles Farrell, showed domestic rentals of $3.5 million, a record for the company."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Forever Amber
            </i>
            : p.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=WIZwZOz8LHsC&amp;q=%22on+the+surface%22+%22world+rentals%22+%248" rel="nofollow">
             66
            </a>
            . "On the surface, with world rentals of $8 million,
            <i>
             Forever Amber
            </i>
            was considered a hit at distribution level."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             The French Connection
            </i>
           </li>
          </ul>
          <dl>
           <dd>
            <dl>
             <dd>
              p.
              <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=WIZwZOz8LHsC&amp;pg=PA167" rel="nofollow">
               167
              </a>
              . "The
              <i>
               Planet of the Apes
              </i>
              motion pictures were all moneymakers and Zanuck's record would have immediately improved had he stayed through the release of
              <i>
               The French Connection
              </i>
              , which took in rentals of approximately $75 million worldwide."
             </dd>
             <dd>
              p.
              <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=w70iAAAAMAAJ&amp;q=%22french+connection%22#search_anchor" rel="nofollow">
               256
              </a>
              . "$3,300,00".
             </dd>
            </dl>
           </dd>
          </dl>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-151">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-151">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA46" rel="nofollow">
           46
          </a>
          . "Production Cost: $0.6 (Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-152">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-152">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFCormack1993">
           Cormack, Mike (1993).
           <i>
            Ideology and Cinematography in Hollywood, 1930–1939
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Palgrave_Macmillan" title="Palgrave Macmillan">
            Palgrave Macmillan
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=w0JSFWpr2gAC&amp;pg=PA28" rel="nofollow">
            28
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-312-10067-4" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-312-10067-4">
            <bdi>
             978-0-312-10067-4
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            Although costing $1250000—a huge sum for any studio in 1929—the film was a financial success. Karl Thiede gives the domestic box-office at $1500000, and the same figure for the foreign gross.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Ideology+and+Cinematography+in+Hollywood%2C+1930%E2%80%931939&amp;rft.pages=28&amp;rft.pub=Palgrave+Macmillan&amp;rft.date=1993&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-312-10067-4&amp;rft.aulast=Cormack&amp;rft.aufirst=Mike&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Balio_(1996)-153">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-Balio_(1996)_153-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-Balio_(1996)_153-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFBalio1996">
           Balio, Tino (1996).
           <i>
            Grand Design: Hollywood as a Modern Business Enterprise, 1930–1939
           </i>
           . Volume 5 of History of the American Cinema.
           <a href="/wiki/University_of_California_Press" title="University of California Press">
            University of California Press
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-520-20334-1" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-520-20334-1">
            <bdi>
             978-0-520-20334-1
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Grand+Design%3A+Hollywood+as+a+Modern+Business+Enterprise%2C+1930%E2%80%931939&amp;rft.pub=University+of+California+Press&amp;rft.date=1996&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-520-20334-1&amp;rft.aulast=Balio&amp;rft.aufirst=Tino&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             Cavalcade
            </i>
            : p.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=_J9HTLOI08wC&amp;pg=PA182" rel="nofollow">
             182
            </a>
            . "Produced by Winfield Sheehan at a cost of $1.25 million,
            <i>
             Cavalcade
            </i>
            won Academy Awards for best picture, director, art direction and grossed close to $4 million during its first release, much of which came from Great Britain and the Empire."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Whoopee
            </i>
            : p.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=_J9HTLOI08wC&amp;pg=PA212" rel="nofollow">
             212
            </a>
            . "Produced by Sam Goldwyn at a cost of $1 million, the picture was an adaptation of a smash musical comedy built around Eddie Cantor...A personality-centered musical,
            <i>
             Whoopee!
            </i>
            made little attempt to integrate the comedy routines, songs, and story. Nonetheless, Cantor's feature-film debut grossed over $2.6 million worldwide and started a popular series that included
            <i>
             Palmy Days
            </i>
            (1931),
            <i>
             The Kid from Spain
            </i>
            (1932), and
            <i>
             Roman Scandals
            </i>
            (1933)."
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Balio_(1976)-154">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-Balio_(1976)_154-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <i>
           Hell's Angels
          </i>
          <ul>
           <li>
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFBalio1976">
             Balio, Tino (1976).
             <i>
              United Artists: The Company Built by the Stars
             </i>
             .
             <a href="/wiki/University_of_Wisconsin_Press" title="University of Wisconsin Press">
              University of Wisconsin Press
             </a>
             . p.
             <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=QljKdIYzncoC&amp;pg=PA110" rel="nofollow">
              110
             </a>
             .
             <q>
              Hughes did not have the "Midas touch" the trade press so often attributed to him.
              <i>
               Variety
              </i>
              , for example, reported that
              <i>
               Hell's Angels
              </i>
              cost $3.2 million to make, and by July, 1931, eight months after its release, the production cost had nearly been paid off. Keats claimed the picture cost $4 million to make and that it earned twice that much within twenty years. The production cost estimate is probably correct. Hughes worked on the picture for over two years, shooting it first as a silent and then as a talkie. Lewis Milestone said that in between Hughes experimented with shooting it in color as well. But
              <i>
               Variety'
              </i>
              s earnings report must be the fabrication of a delirious publicity agent, and Keats' the working of a myth maker. During the seven years it was in United Artists distribution,
              <i>
               Hell's Angels
              </i>
              grossed $1.6 million in the domestic market, of which Hughes' share was $1.2 million. Whatever the foreign gross was, it seems unlikely that it was great enough to earn a profit for the picture.
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=United+Artists%3A+The+Company+Built+by+the+Stars&amp;rft.pages=110&amp;rft.pub=University+of+Wisconsin+Press&amp;rft.date=1976&amp;rft.aulast=Balio&amp;rft.aufirst=Tino&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-155">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-155">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1" id="CITEREFFeaster">
           Feaster, Felicia.
           <a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/this-month/article/373967%7C18617/Frankenstein.html" rel="nofollow">
            "Frankenstein (1931)"
           </a>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">
            Turner Classic Movies
           </a>
           <span class="reference-accessdate">
            . Retrieved
            <span class="nowrap">
             July 4,
            </span>
            2011
           </span>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=Frankenstein+%281931%29&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft.aulast=Feaster&amp;rft.aufirst=Felicia&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Fthis-month%2Farticle%2F373967%257C18617%2FFrankenstein.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-156">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-156">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA163&amp;q=%22worldwide+rentals%22" rel="nofollow">
           163
          </a>
          . "It drew $1.4 million in worldwide rentals in its first run versus $1.2 million for
          <i>
           Dracula
          </i>
          , which had opened in February 1931."
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-157">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-157">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFVance2003">
           Vance, Jeffrey (2003).
           <i>
            Chaplin: genius of the cinema
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Abrams_Books" title="Abrams Books">
            Abrams Books
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/chaplingeniusofc00vanc" rel="nofollow">
            208
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            Chaplin's negative cost for City Lights was $1,607,351. The film eventually earned him a worldwide profit of $5 million ($2 million domestically and $3 million in foreign distribution), an enormous sum of money for the time.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Chaplin%3A+genius+of+the+cinema&amp;rft.pages=208&amp;rft.pub=Abrams+Books&amp;rft.date=2003&amp;rft.aulast=Vance&amp;rft.aufirst=Jeffrey&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-IMPA_(1937)-158">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-IMPA_(1937)_158-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>

           </i>
           :
           <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/stream/international193738quig#page/942/mode/2up" rel="nofollow">
            942–943
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            <i>
             Kid from Spain
            </i>
            : $2,621,000 (data supplied by Eddie Cantor)
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=International+Motion+Picture+Almanac+1937%E2%80%9338&amp;rft.atitle=The+All-Time+Best+Sellers+%E2%80%93+Motion+Pictures&amp;rft.pages=942-943&amp;rft.date=1937&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Sedgwick-159">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-Sedgwick_159-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>

           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
