           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-Sedgwick_159-2">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              c
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-Sedgwick_159-3">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              d
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFSedgwick2000">
           Sedgwick, John (2000).
           <i>
            Popular Filmgoing In 1930s Britain: A Choice of Pleasures
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/University_of_Exeter_Press" title="University of Exeter Press">
            University of Exeter Press
           </a>
           . pp.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=YsUfc8Ijb-wC&amp;pg=PA146" rel="nofollow">
            146
           </a>
           –148.
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-85989-660-3" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-85989-660-3">
            <bdi>
             978-0-85989-660-3
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            Sources: Eddie Mannix Ledger, made available to the author by Mark Glancy...
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Popular+Filmgoing+In+1930s+Britain%3A+A+Choice+of+Pleasures&amp;rft.pages=146-148&amp;rft.pub=University+of+Exeter+Press&amp;rft.date=2000&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-85989-660-3&amp;rft.aulast=Sedgwick&amp;rft.aufirst=John&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             Grand Hotel
            </i>
            : Production Cost $000s: 700; Distribution Cost $000s: 947; U.S. box-office $000s: 1,235; Foreign box-office $000s: 1,359; Total box-office $000s: 2,594; Profit $000s: 947.
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             The Merry Widow
            </i>
            : Production Cost $000s: 1,605; Distribution Cost $000s: 1,116; U.S. box-office $000s: 861; Foreign box-office $000s: 1,747; Total box-office $000s: 2,608; Profit $000s: -113.
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Viva Villa
            </i>
            : Production Cost $000s: 1,022; Distribution Cost $000s: 766; U.S. box-office $000s: 941; Foreign box-office $000s: 934; Total box-office $000s: 1,875; Profit $000s: 87.
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Mutiny on the Bounty
            </i>
            : Production Cost $000s: 1,905; Distribution Cost $000s: 1,646; U.S. box-office $000s: 2,250; Foreign box-office $000s: 2,210; Total box-office $000s: 4,460; Profit $000s: 909.
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             San Francisco
            </i>
            : Production Cost $000s: 1,300; Distribution Cost $000s: 1,736; U.S. box-office $000s: 2,868; Foreign box-office $000s: 2,405; Total box-office $000s: 5,273; Profit $000s: 2,237.
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-shanghai-160">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-shanghai_160-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <i>
           Shanghai Express
          </i>
          <ul>
           <li>
            <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
             Block &amp; Wilson 2010
            </a>
            , p.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA165" rel="nofollow">
             165
            </a>
            . "
            <i>
             Shanghai Express
            </i>
            was Dietrich's biggest hit in America, bringing in $1.5 million in worldwide rentals."
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-161">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-161">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <i>
           King Kong
          </i>
          <ul>
           <li>
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation journal cs1" id="CITEREFJewel1994">
             Jewel, Richard (1994). "RKO Film Grosses: 1931–1951".
             <i>
              Historical Journal of Film Radio and Television
             </i>
             .
             <b>
              14
             </b>
             (1): 39.
             <q>
              1933 release: $1,856,000; 1938 release: $306,000; 1944 release: $685,000
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Historical+Journal+of+Film+Radio+and+Television&amp;rft.atitle=RKO+Film+Grosses%3A+1931%E2%80%931951&amp;rft.volume=14&amp;rft.issue=1&amp;rft.pages=39&amp;rft.date=1994&amp;rft.aulast=Jewel&amp;rft.aufirst=Richard&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
           <li>
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation web cs1">
             <a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/2690/King-Kong/notes.html" rel="nofollow">
              "King Kong (1933) – Notes"
             </a>
             .
             <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">
              Turner Classic Movies
             </a>
             <span class="reference-accessdate">
              . Retrieved
              <span class="nowrap">
               January 7,
              </span>
              2012
             </span>
             .
             <q>
              1952 release: $2,500,000; budget: $672,254.75
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=King+Kong+%281933%29+%E2%80%93+Notes&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F2690%2FKing-Kong%2Fnotes.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-162">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-162">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1">
           <a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/79021/I-m-No-Angel/notes.html" rel="nofollow">
            "I'm No Angel (1933) – Notes"
           </a>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">
            Turner Classic Movies
           </a>
           <span class="reference-accessdate">
            . Retrieved
            <span class="nowrap">
             January 7,
            </span>
            2012
           </span>
           .
           <q>
            According to a modern source, it had a gross earning of $2,250,000 on the North American continent, with over a million more earned internationally.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=I%27m+No+Angel+%281933%29+%E2%80%93+Notes&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F79021%2FI-m-No-Angel%2Fnotes.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-163">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-163">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFFinler2003">
           Finler 2003
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=rvVhEJmbfrsC&amp;pg=PA188&amp;q=angel%20rock-bottom%20cost" rel="nofollow">
           188
          </a>
          . "The studio released its most profitable pictures of the decade in 1933,
          <i>
           She Done Him Wrong
          </i>
          and
          <i>
           I'm No Angel
          </i>
          , written by and starring Mae West. Produced at a rock-bottom cost of $200,000 each, they undoubtedly helped Paramount through the worst patch in its history..."
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-BlockWilson173-164">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson173_164-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFBlock2010">
           Block, Alex Ben (2010),
           <i>
            She Done Him Wrong
           </i>
           , p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA173" rel="nofollow">
            173
           </a>
           ,
           <q>
            The worldwide rentals of over $3 million keep the lights on at Paramount, which did not shy away from selling the movie's sex appeal.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=She+Done+Him+Wrong&amp;rft.pages=173&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Block&amp;rft.aufirst=Alex+Ben&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
          In:
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          .
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Phillips_(2008)-165">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-Phillips_(2008)_165-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFPhillips2008">
           Phillips, Kendall R. (2008).
           <i>
            Controversial Cinema: The Films That Outraged America
           </i>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ABC-CLIO" title="ABC-CLIO">
            ABC-CLIO
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=ViQzDunkm9QC&amp;pg=PA26" rel="nofollow">
            26
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-56720-724-8" title="Special:BookSources/978-1-56720-724-8">
            <bdi>
             978-1-56720-724-8
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            The reaction to West's first major film, however, was not exclusively negative. Made for a mere $200,000, the film would rake in a healthy $2 million in the United States and an additional million in overseas markets.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Controversial+Cinema%3A+The+Films+That+Outraged+America&amp;rft.pages=26&amp;rft.pub=ABC-CLIO&amp;rft.date=2008&amp;rft.isbn=978-1-56720-724-8&amp;rft.aulast=Phillips&amp;rft.aufirst=Kendall+R.&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-166">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-166">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA135" rel="nofollow">
           135
          </a>
          . "Total production cost: $274,076 (Unadjusted $s)."
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Turk-167">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-Turk_167-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-Turk_167-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFTurk2000">
           Turk, Edward Baron (2000) [1st. pub. 1998].
           <i>
            Hollywood Diva: A Biography of Jeanette MacDonald
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/University_of_California_Press" title="University of California Press">
            University of California Press
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-520-22253-3" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-520-22253-3">
            <bdi>
             978-0-520-22253-3
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Hollywood+Diva%3A+A+Biography+of+Jeanette+MacDonald&amp;rft.pub=University+of+California+Press&amp;rft.date=2000&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-520-22253-3&amp;rft.aulast=Turk&amp;rft.aufirst=Edward+Baron&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             The Merry Widow
            </i>
            : p.
            <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/hollywooddivabio0000turk/page/361" rel="nofollow">
             361
            </a>
            Cost: $1,605,000. Earnings: domestic $861,000; foreign $1,747,000; total $2,608,000. Loss: $113,000.
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             San Francisco
            </i>
            : p.
            <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/hollywooddivabio0000turk/page/364" rel="nofollow">
             364
            </a>
            Cost: $1,300,000. Earnings: domestic $2,868,000; foreign $2,405,000; total $5,273,000. Profit: $2,237,000. [Reissues in 1938–39 and 1948–49 brought profits of $124,000 and $647,000 respectively.]
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-168">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-168">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFMcBride2011">
           McBride, Joseph (2011).
           <i>
            Frank Capra: The Catastrophe of Success
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/University_Press_of_Mississippi" title="University Press of Mississippi">
            University Press of Mississippi
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=DMkLpTFBEtUC&amp;pg=PA309" rel="nofollow">
            309
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-60473-838-4" title="Special:BookSources/978-1-60473-838-4">
            <bdi>
             978-1-60473-838-4
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            According to the studio's books
            <i>
             It Happened One Night
            </i>
            brought in $1 million in film rentals during its initial release, but as Joe Walker pointed out, the figure would have been much larger if the film had not been sold to theaters on a block-booking basis in a package with more than two dozen lesser Columbia films, and the total rentals of the package spread among them all, as was customary in that era, since it minimized the risk and allowed the major studios to dominate the marketplace.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Frank+Capra%3A+The+Catastrophe+of+Success&amp;rft.pages=309&amp;rft.pub=University+Press+of+Mississippi&amp;rft.date=2011&amp;rft.isbn=978-1-60473-838-4&amp;rft.aulast=McBride&amp;rft.aufirst=Joseph&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-169">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-169">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFDick2008">
           Dick, Bernard F. (2008).
           <i>
            Claudette Colbert: She Walked in Beauty
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/University_Press_of_Mississippi" title="University Press of Mississippi">
            University Press of Mississippi
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=P3P9efYabOQC&amp;pg=PA79" rel="nofollow">
            79
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-60473-087-6" title="Special:BookSources/978-1-60473-087-6">
            <bdi>
             978-1-60473-087-6
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            Although Columbia's president, Harry Cohn, had strong reservations about
            <i>
             It Happened One Night
            </i>
            , he also knew that it would not bankrupt the studio; the rights were only $5,000, and the budget was set at $325,000, including the performers' salaries.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Claudette+Colbert%3A+She+Walked+in+Beauty&amp;rft.pages=79&amp;rft.pub=University+Press+of+Mississippi&amp;rft.date=2008&amp;rft.isbn=978-1-60473-087-6&amp;rft.aulast=Dick&amp;rft.aufirst=Bernard+F.&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-170">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-170">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <i>
           Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs
          </i>
          <ul>
           <li>
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFMonaco2010">
             Monaco, Paul (2010).
             <i>
              A History of American Movies: A Film-By-Film Look at the Art, Craft, and Business of Cinema
             </i>
             .
             <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Scarecrow_Press" title="Scarecrow Press">
              Scarecrow Press
             </a>
             . p.
             <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=tgnKY6k5tHYC&amp;pg=PA54" rel="nofollow">
              54
             </a>
             .
             <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
              ISBN
             </a>
             <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-8108-7434-3" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-8108-7434-3">
              <bdi>
               978-0-8108-7434-3
              </bdi>
             </a>
             .
             <q>
              Considered a highly risky gamble when the movie was in production in the mid-1930s, by the fiftieth anniversary of its 1937 premiere
              <i>
               Snow White'
              </i>
              s earnings exceeded $330 million.
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=A+History+of+American+Movies%3A+A+Film-By-Film+Look+at+the+Art%2C+Craft%2C+and+Business+of+Cinema&amp;rft.pages=54&amp;rft.pub=Scarecrow+Press&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-8108-7434-3&amp;rft.aulast=Monaco&amp;rft.aufirst=Paul&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
           <li>
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFWilhelmBrower1993">
             Wilhelm, Henry Gilmer; Brower, Carol (1993).
             <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/permanencecareof00henr/page/359" rel="nofollow">
              <i>
               The Permanence and Care of Color Photographs: Traditional and Digital Color Prints, Color Negatives, Slides, and Motion Pictures
              </i>
             </a>
             . Preservation Pub. p.
             <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=0OtTAAAAMAAJ&amp;dq=%22snow+white%22+million+worldwide+grosses" rel="nofollow">
              359
             </a>
             .
             <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
              ISBN
             </a>
             <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-911515-00-8" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-911515-00-8">
              <bdi>
               978-0-911515-00-8
              </bdi>
             </a>
             .
             <q>
              In only 2 months after the 1987 re-release, the film grossed another $45 million—giving it a total gross to date of about $375 million!
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+Permanence+and+Care+of+Color+Photographs%3A+Traditional+and+Digital+Color+Prints%2C+Color+Negatives%2C+Slides%2C+and+Motion+Pictures&amp;rft.pages=359&amp;rft.pub=Preservation+Pub&amp;rft.date=1993&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-911515-00-8&amp;rft.aulast=Wilhelm&amp;rft.aufirst=Henry+Gilmer&amp;rft.au=Brower%2C+Carol&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Farchive.org%2Fdetails%2Fpermanencecareof00henr%2Fpage%2F359&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
           <li>
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation web cs1">
             <a class="external text" href="http://pro.boxoffice.com/movie/9400/snow-white-and-the-seven-dwarfs-1987-re-issue" rel="nofollow">
              "Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs (1987 Re-issue)"
             </a>
             .
             <i>
              <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Boxoffice_(magazine)" title="Boxoffice (magazine)">
               Boxoffice
              </a>
             </i>
             <span class="reference-accessdate">
              . Retrieved
              <span class="nowrap">
               May 29,
              </span>
              2016
             </span>
             .
             <q>
              North American box-office: $46,594,719
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Boxoffice&amp;rft.atitle=Snow+White+and+the+Seven+Dwarfs+%281987+Re-issue%29&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fpro.boxoffice.com%2Fmovie%2F9400%2Fsnow-white-and-the-seven-dwarfs-1987-re-issue&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
           <li>
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation web cs1">
             <a class="external text" href="http://pro.boxoffice.com/movie/9401/snow-white-and-the-seven-dwarfs-1993-re-issue" rel="nofollow">
              "Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs (1993 Re-issue)"
             </a>
             .
             <i>
              <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Boxoffice_(magazine)" title="Boxoffice (magazine)">
               Boxoffice
              </a>
             </i>
             <span class="reference-accessdate">
              . Retrieved
              <span class="nowrap">
               May 29,
              </span>
              2016
             </span>
             .
             <q>
              North American box-office: $41,634,791
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Boxoffice&amp;rft.atitle=Snow+White+and+the+Seven+Dwarfs+%281993+Re-issue%29&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fpro.boxoffice.com%2Fmovie%2F9401%2Fsnow-white-and-the-seven-dwarfs-1993-re-issue&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Snow_White_&amp;_Pinocchio-171">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-Snow_White_&amp;_Pinocchio_171-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-Snow_White_&amp;_Pinocchio_171-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <i>
           Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs
          </i>
          and
          <i>
           Pinocchio
          </i>
          <ul>
           <li>
            <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
             Block &amp; Wilson 2010
            </a>
           </li>
          </ul>
          <dl>
           <dd>
            <dl>
             <dd>
              p.
              <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA207&amp;q=When%20the%20budget%20rose%20from%20%24250%2C000%20to%20%241%2C488%2C423" rel="nofollow">
               207
              </a>
              . "When the budget rose from $250,000 to $1,488,423 he even mortgaged his own home and automobile. Disney had bet more than his company on the success of
              <i>
               Snow White
              </i>
              ."
             </dd>
             <dd>
              p.
              <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA237&amp;q=%22%248%20million%20in%20worldwide%20rentals%22%20george%20lucas%20blockbusting" rel="nofollow">
               237
              </a>
              . "By the end of 1938, it had grossed more than $8 million in worldwide rentals and was ranked at the time as the second-highest-grossing film after the 1925 epic
              <i>
               Ben-Hur
              </i>
              ".
             </dd>
             <dd>
              p.
              <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA255&amp;q=%22foreign%20rentals%22" rel="nofollow">
               255
              </a>
              . "On its initial release
              <i>
               Pinocchio
              </i>
              brought in only $1.6 million in domestic rentals (compared with
              <i>
               Snow White
              </i>
              <span class="nowrap" style="padding-left:0.1em;">
               '
              </span>
              s $4.2 million) and $1.9 million in foreign rentals (compared with
              <i>
               Snow White
              </i>
              <span class="nowrap" style="padding-left:0.1em;">
               '
              </span>
              s $4.3 million)."
             </dd>
            </dl>
           </dd>
          </dl>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-172">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-172">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          1938
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             You Can't Take It With You
            </i>
            :
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation web cs1">
             <a class="external text" href="https://www.webcitation.org/6AeWlL1hk?url=http://focusfeatures.com/flashback?article_url=you_can_t_take_it_with_you_premieres" rel="nofollow">
              "You Can't Take It With You Premieres"
             </a>
             .
             <a href="/wiki/Focus_Features" title="Focus Features">
              Focus Features
             </a>
             . Archived from
             <a class="external text" href="http://focusfeatures.com/flashback?article_url=you_can_t_take_it_with_you_premieres" rel="nofollow">
              the original
             </a>
             on September 13, 2012.
             <q>
              <i>
               You Can't Take It With You
              </i>
              received excellent reviews, won Best Picture and Best Director at the 1938 Academy Awards, and earned over $5 million worldwide.
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=You+Can%27t+Take+It+With+You+Premieres&amp;rft.pub=Focus+Features&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Ffocusfeatures.com%2Fflashback%3Farticle_url%3Dyou_can_t_take_it_with_you_premieres&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Boys Town
            </i>
            :
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFBlock2010">
             Block, Alex Ben (2010),
             <i>
              Boys Town
             </i>
             , p.
             <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA215" rel="nofollow">
              215
             </a>
             ,
             <q>
              The film quickly became a smash nationwide, making a profit of over $2 million on worldwide rentals of $4 million.
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Boys+Town&amp;rft.pages=215&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Block&amp;rft.aufirst=Alex+Ben&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
            In:
            <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
             Block &amp; Wilson 2010
            </a>
            .
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             The Adventures of Robin Hood
            </i>
            :
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation journal cs1" id="CITEREFGlancy1995">
             Glancy, H. Mark (1995). "Warner Bros Film Grosses, 1921–51: the William Schaefer ledger".
             <i>
              Historical Journal of Film, Radio and Television
             </i>
             .
             <b>
              1
             </b>
             (15): 55–60.
             <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Doi_(identifier)" title="Doi (identifier)">
              doi
             </a>
             :
             <a class="external text" href="https://doi.org/10.1080%2F01439689500260031" rel="nofollow">
              10.1080/01439689500260031
             </a>
             .
             <q>
              $3.981 million.
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Historical+Journal+of+Film%2C+Radio+and+Television&amp;rft.atitle=Warner+Bros+Film+Grosses%2C+1921%E2%80%9351%3A+the+William+Schaefer+ledger&amp;rft.volume=1&amp;rft.issue=15&amp;rft.pages=55-60&amp;rft.date=1995&amp;rft_id=info%3Adoi%2F10.1080%2F01439689500260031&amp;rft.aulast=Glancy&amp;rft.aufirst=H.+Mark&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Alexander's Ragtime Band
            </i>
            :
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFBlock2010">
             Block, Hayley Taylor (2010),
             <i>
              Alexander's Ragtime Band
             </i>
             , p.
             <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA213&amp;q=%22worldwide%20rentals%22" rel="nofollow">
              213
             </a>
             ,
             <q>
              Once the confusion cleared, however, the film blossomed into a commercial success, with a profit of $978,000 on worldwide rentals of $3.6 million.
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Alexander%27s+Ragtime+Band&amp;rft.pages=213&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Block&amp;rft.aufirst=Hayley+Taylor&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
            In:
            <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
             Block &amp; Wilson 2010
            </a>
            .
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-173">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-173">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation magazine cs1" id="CITEREFChartier1938">
           Chartier, Roy (September 6, 1938).
           <a class="external text" href="https://www.variety.com/review/VE1117796537" rel="nofollow">
            "You Can't Take It With You"
           </a>
           .
           <i>

             Variety
            </a>
           </i>
           <span class="reference-accessdate">

            </span>

           </span>

          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Variety&amp;rft.atitle=You+Can%27t+Take+It+With+You&amp;rft.date=1938-09-06&amp;rft.aulast=Chartier&amp;rft.aufirst=Roy&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.variety.com%2Freview%2FVE1117796537&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">

          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Numbers&amp;rft.atitle=Gone+with+the+Wind&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.the-numbers.com%2Fmovies%2F1939%2F0GWTW.php&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">

        <li id="cite_note-175">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-175">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1">
           <a class="external text" href="http://pro.boxoffice.com/movie/4094/gone-with-the-wind" rel="nofollow">
            "Gone with the Wind"
           </a>
           .

          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Boxoffice&amp;rft.atitle=Gone+with+the+Wind&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fpro.boxoffice.com%2Fmovie%2F4094%2Fgone-with-the-wind&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">

        <li id="cite_note-gwtw_tcm-176">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-gwtw_tcm_176-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-gwtw_tcm_176-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1" id="CITEREFMillerStafford2007">
           Miller, Frank; Stafford, Jeff (January 5, 2007).
           <a class="external text" href="https://web.archive.org/web/20130728160304/http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/414427/Gone-With-the-Wind/articles.html" rel="nofollow">
            "Gone With the Wind (1939) – Articles"
           </a>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">
            Turner Classic Movies
           </a>
           . Archived from
           <a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/414427/Gone-With-the-Wind/articles.html" rel="nofollow">
            the original
           </a>
           on July 28, 2013.
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=Gone+With+the+Wind+%281939%29+%E2%80%93+Articles&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft.date=2007-01-05&amp;rft.aulast=Miller&amp;rft.aufirst=Frank&amp;rft.au=Stafford%2C+Jeff&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F414427%2FGone-With-the-Wind%2Farticles.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-177">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-177">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/movies/?id=gonewiththewind.htm" rel="nofollow">

           </i>

          at
          <a href="/wiki/Box_Office_Mojo" title="Box Office Mojo">

        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-178">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-178">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">
           Hall &amp; Neale 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA283" rel="nofollow">
           283
          </a>
          ."The final negative cost of
          <i>
           Gone with the Wind
          </i>
          (
          <i>
           GWTW
          </i>
          ) has been variously reported between $3.9 million and $4.25 million."
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-179">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-179">

          </b>

         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1">

           </a>
           .
           <i>

          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Boxoffice&amp;rft.atitle=Pinocchio+%281940%29&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fpro.boxoffice.com%2Fmovie%2F7922%2Fpinocchio-1940&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">

        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Barrier602-180">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-Barrier602_180-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFBarrier2003">
           Barrier, Michael (2003).
           <i>
            Hollywood Cartoons: American Animation in Its Golden Age
           </i>
           . New York:
           <a href="/wiki/Oxford_University_Press" title="Oxford University Press">
            Oxford University Press
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=xt5k08yuAXIC&amp;q=pinocchio%20%22negative%20cost%20was%20%242.6%20million%22" rel="nofollow">
            266
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-19-983922-3" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-19-983922-3">
            <bdi>
             978-0-19-983922-3
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            The film's negative cost was $2.6 million, more than $1 million higher than
            <i>
             Snow White
            </i>
            <span class="nowrap" style="padding-left:0.1em;">
             '
            </span>
            s.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Hollywood+Cartoons%3A+American+Animation+in+Its+Golden+Age&amp;rft.place=New+York&amp;rft.pages=266&amp;rft.pub=Oxford+University+Press&amp;rft.date=2003&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-19-983922-3&amp;rft.aulast=Barrier&amp;rft.aufirst=Michael&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Schatz_(1999)-181">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-Schatz_(1999)_181-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFSchatz1999">
           Schatz, Thomas (1999) [1st. pub. 1997].
           <i>
            Boom and Bust: American Cinema in the 1940s
           </i>
           . Volume 6 of History of the American Cinema.
           <a href="/wiki/University_of_California_Press" title="University of California Press">
            University of California Press
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=dwf5SUcfousC&amp;pg=PA466" rel="nofollow">
            466
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-520-22130-7" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-520-22130-7">
            <bdi>
             978-0-520-22130-7
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            <i>
             Boom Town
            </i>
            ($4.6 million).
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Boom+and+Bust%3A+American+Cinema+in+the+1940s&amp;rft.pages=466&amp;rft.pub=University+of+California+Press&amp;rft.date=1999&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-520-22130-7&amp;rft.aulast=Schatz&amp;rft.aufirst=Thomas&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-182">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-182">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          , pp.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA258" rel="nofollow">
           258
          </a>
          –
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA259&amp;q=boom%20town%20biggest%20moneymaker" rel="nofollow">
           259
          </a>
          . "Production Cost: $2.1 (Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s) ...
          <i>
           Boom Town
          </i>
          was the biggest moneymaker of 1940 and one of the top films of the decade."
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-183">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-183">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA267&amp;q=sergeant%20york%20worldwide" rel="nofollow">
           267
          </a>
          . "With worldwide rentals of $7.8 million in its initial release, the movie made a net profit of over $3 million."
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-184">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-184">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFFinler2003">
           Finler 2003
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=rvVhEJmbfrsC&amp;pg=PA301" rel="nofollow">
           301
          </a>
          . "The studio did particularly well with its war-related pictures, such as
          <i>
           Sergeant York
          </i>
          (1941), which cost $1.6 million but was the studio's biggest hit of the decade aside from
          <i>
           This is the Army
          </i>
          (1943), the Irving Berlin musical for which the profits were donated to the Army Emergency Relief fund."
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-185">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-185">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1">
           <a class="external text" href="http://pro.boxoffice.com/movie/810/bambi" rel="nofollow">
            "Bambi"
           </a>
           .
           <i>
            <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Boxoffice_(magazine)" title="Boxoffice (magazine)">
             Boxoffice
            </a>
           </i>
           <span class="reference-accessdate">
            . Retrieved
            <span class="nowrap">
             May 29,
            </span>
            2016
           </span>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Boxoffice&amp;rft.atitle=Bambi&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fpro.boxoffice.com%2Fmovie%2F810%2Fbambi&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-186">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-186">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA281&amp;q=%22worldwide+rentals%22" rel="nofollow">
           281
          </a>
          . "Worldwide rentals of $3,449,353 barely recouped the film's nearly $2 million production cost."
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Disney-187">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-Disney_187-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-Disney_187-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-Disney_187-2">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              c
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-Disney_187-3">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              d
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          , pp.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA712#v=twopage" rel="nofollow">
           712–713
          </a>
          .
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             Bambi
            </i>
            : "Worldwide Box Office: $266.8; Production Cost: $1.7 (Millions of $s)"
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             101 Dalmatians
            </i>
            : "Worldwide Box Office: $215.0; Production Cost: $3.6 (Millions of $s)"
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             The Jungle Book
            </i>
            : "Worldwide Box Office: $170.8"; Production Cost: $3.9 (Millions of $s)"
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Aladdin
            </i>
            : "Worldwide Box Office: $505.1"; Production Cost: $28.0 (Millions of $s)"
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-188">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-188">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFGlancy1999">
           Glancy, Mark (1999).
           <i>
            When Hollywood Loved Britain: The Hollywood 'British' Film 1939–1945
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Manchester_University_Press" title="Manchester University Press">
            Manchester University Press
           </a>
           . pp.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=T_Yryky_eA4C&amp;pg=PA94" rel="nofollow">
            94
           </a>
           –
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=T_Yryky_eA4C&amp;pg=PA95" rel="nofollow">
            95
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-7190-4853-1" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-7190-4853-1">
            <bdi>
             978-0-7190-4853-1
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            <i>
             Mrs Miniver
            </i>
            was a phenomenon. It was the most popular film of the year (from any studio) in both North America and Britain, and its foreign earnings were three times higher than those of any other MGM film released in the 1941–42 season. The production cost ($1,344,000) was one of the highest of the season, indicating the studio never thought of the film as a potential loss-maker. When the film earned a worldwide gross of $8,878,000, MGM had the highest profit ($4,831,000) in its history.
            <i>
             Random Harvest
            </i>
            nearly matched the success of
            <i>
             Mrs Miniver
            </i>
            with worldwide earnings of $8,147,000 yielding the second-highest profit in MGM's history ($4,384,000).
            <i>
             Random Harvest
            </i>
            was also the most popular film of the year in Britain, where it proved to be even more popular than Britain's most acclaimed war film,
            <i>
             In Which We Serve
            </i>
            .
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=When+Hollywood+Loved+Britain%3A+The+Hollywood+%27British%27+Film+1939%E2%80%931945&amp;rft.pages=94-95&amp;rft.pub=Manchester+University+Press&amp;rft.date=1999&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-7190-4853-1&amp;rft.aulast=Glancy&amp;rft.aufirst=Mark&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-189">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-189">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             Mrs. Miniver
            </i>
            :
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFBurns2010">
             Burns, Douglas (2010),
             <i>
              Mrs. Miniver
             </i>
             , p.
             <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA279" rel="nofollow">
              279
             </a>
             ,
             <q>
              <i>
               Mrs. Miniver'
              </i>
              s galvanizing effect on Americans spawned a record-breaking ten-week run at Radio City Music Hall and garnered a $5.4 million take in domestic rentals (making
              <i>
               Mrs. Miniver
              </i>
              1942's top grosser), with a $4.8 million profit on worldwide rentals of $8.9 million.
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Mrs.+Miniver&amp;rft.pages=279&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Burns&amp;rft.aufirst=Douglas&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Yankee Doodle Dandy
            </i>
            : p.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA275&amp;q=%22worldwide+rentals%22" rel="nofollow">
             275
            </a>
            . "It became the second biggest box-office hit of 1942 (after Mrs. Miniver) and was praised by critics, making a profit of $3.4 million on worldwide rentals of $6.5 million."
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-190">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-190">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFMcAdams2010">
           McAdams, Frank (2010),
           <i>
            For Whom the Bell Tolls
           </i>
           , p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA287" rel="nofollow">
            287
           </a>
           ,
           <q>
            Despite the early furor over the novel being "pro-red and immoral," the film opened to strong and favorable reviews and brought in $11 million in worldwide rentals in its initial release.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=For+Whom+the+Bell+Tolls&amp;rft.pages=287&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=McAdams&amp;rft.aufirst=Frank&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
          In:
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          .
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-191">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-191">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1">
           <a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/75353/For-Whom-the-Bell-Tolls/notes.html" rel="nofollow">
            "For Whom the Bell Tolls (1943) – Notes"
           </a>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">
            Turner Classic Movies
           </a>
           <span class="reference-accessdate">
            . Retrieved
            <span class="nowrap">
             June 24,
            </span>
            2012
           </span>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=For+Whom+the+Bell+Tolls+%281943%29+%E2%80%93+Notes&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F75353%2FFor-Whom-the-Bell-Tolls%2Fnotes.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-guy-named-joe-192">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-guy-named-joe_192-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-guy-named-joe_192-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1">
           <a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/191/A-Guy-Named-Joe/notes.html" rel="nofollow">
            "A Guy Named Joe (1944) – Notes"
           </a>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">
            Turner Classic Movies
           </a>
           <span class="reference-accessdate">
            . Retrieved
            <span class="nowrap">
             August 29,
            </span>
            2012
           </span>
           .
           <q>
            According to M-G-M studio records at the AMPAS Library, the film had a negative cost of $2,627,000 and took in $5,363,000 at the box office. When the picture was re-issued for the 1955–56 season, it took in an additional $150,000.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=A+Guy+Named+Joe+%281944%29+%E2%80%93+Notes&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F191%2FA-Guy-Named-Joe%2Fnotes.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-193">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-193">

           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>

           <a href="#cite_ref-196">

          </a>

          </a>
          . "(Unadjusted $s) in Millions of $s – Production Cost: $1.0"
         </span>

        <li id="cite_note-BlockWilson232-197">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson232_197-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson232_197-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA232" rel="nofollow">
           232
          </a>
          .
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             Mrs. Miniver
            </i>
            : "Domestic Rentals: $5,358,000; Foreign Rentals: $3,520,000 (Unadjusted $s)"
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Meet Me in St. Louis
            </i>
            : "Domestic Rentals: $5,016,000; Foreign Rentals: $1,623,630 (Unadjusted $s)"
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Easter Parade
            </i>
            : "Domestic Rentals: $4,144,000; Foreign Rentals: $1,774,134 (Unadjusted $s)"
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Schaefer_(1999)-198">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-Schaefer_(1999)_198-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFSchaefer1999">
           Schaefer, Eric (1999).
           <i>
            "Bold! Daring! Shocking! True!": A History of Exploitation Films, 1919–1959
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Duke_University_Press" title="Duke University Press">
            Duke University Press
           </a>
           . pp.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=CSBZqe0zPaMC&amp;pg=PA197" rel="nofollow">
            197
           </a>
           –199.
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-8223-2374-7" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-8223-2374-7">
            <bdi>
             978-0-8223-2374-7
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            Leading the pack of postwar sex hygiene films was
            <i>
             Mom and Dad
            </i>
            (1944), which would become not only the most successful sex hygiene film in history but the biggest pre-1960 exploitation film of any kind. At the end of 1947, the
            <i>
             Los Angeles Times
            </i>
            reported that
            <i>
             Mom and Dad
            </i>
            had grossed $2 million. By 1949
            <i>
             Time
            </i>
            had estimated that
            <i>
             Mom and Dad
            </i>
            had taken in $8 million from twenty million moviegoers. And publicity issuing from
            <i>
             Mom and Dad
            </i>
            <span class="nowrap" style="padding-left:0.1em;">
             '
            </span>
            s production company indicated that by the end of 1956 it had grossed over $80 million worldwide. Net rentals of around $22 million by 1956 would easily place it in the top ten films of the late 1940s and early 1950s had it appeared on conventional lists. Some estimates have placed its total gross over the years at up to $100 million, and it was still playing drive-in dates into 1975...The film was made for around $65,000 with a crew of Hollywood veterans including director William "One Shot" Beaudine, cinematographer Marcel LePicard, and a cast that sported old stalwarts Hardie Albright, Francis Ford, and John Hamilton.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=%22Bold%21+Daring%21+Shocking%21+True%21%22%3A+A+History+of+Exploitation+Films%2C+1919%E2%80%931959&amp;rft.pages=197-199&amp;rft.pub=Duke+University+Press&amp;rft.date=1999&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-8223-2374-7&amp;rft.aulast=Schaefer&amp;rft.aufirst=Eric&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-199">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-199">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          <ul>
           <li>
            p.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA296" rel="nofollow">
             296
            </a>
            . "Production Cost: $1.6 (Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)"
           </li>
           <li>
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFWasson2010">
             Wasson, Sam (2010),
             <i>
              The Bells of St. Mary's
             </i>
             , p.
             <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA297" rel="nofollow">
              297
             </a>
             ,
             <q>
              This was that rare sequel that did even better at the box office than the original, bringing in a $3.7 million profit on $11.2 million in worldwide rentals.
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+Bells+of+St.+Mary%27s&amp;rft.pages=297&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Wasson&amp;rft.aufirst=Sam&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-200">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-200">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1">
           <a class="external text" href="https://www.the-numbers.com/movies/1946/0SOTS.php" rel="nofollow">
            "Song of the South"
           </a>
           .
           <i>
            The Numbers
           </i>
           . Nash Information Services. LLC
           <span class="reference-accessdate">
            . Retrieved
            <span class="nowrap">
             July 10,
            </span>
            2011
           </span>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Numbers&amp;rft.atitle=Song+of+the+South&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.the-numbers.com%2Fmovies%2F1946%2F0SOTS.php&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-201">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-201">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFGabler2007">
           Gabler, Neal (2007).
           <i>
            Walt Disney: the biography
           </i>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Aurum_Press" title="Aurum Press">
            Aurum Press
           </a>
           . pp.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=EX0WAQAAIAAJ&amp;dq=%22song+of+the+south%22+%22million%22" rel="nofollow">
            438
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            Still, the film wound up grossing $3.3 million...
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Walt+Disney%3A+the+biography&amp;rft.pages=438&amp;rft.pub=Aurum+Press&amp;rft.date=2007&amp;rft.aulast=Gabler&amp;rft.aufirst=Neal&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-202">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-202">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1">
           <a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/90871/Song-of-the-South/notes.html" rel="nofollow">

           </a>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">

           </a>
           <span class="reference-accessdate">
            . Retrieved
            <span class="nowrap">
             July 25,
            </span>
            2012
           </span>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=Song+of+the+South+%281946%29+%E2%80%93+Notes&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F90871%2FSong-of-the-South%2Fnotes.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-203">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-203">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">
           Hall &amp; Neale 2010
          </a>
          <ul>
           <li>
            p.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA132" rel="nofollow">
             132
            </a>
            ."
            <i>
             Best Years
            </i>
            was considerably cheaper, costing only $2.1 million, and therefore vastly more profitable."
           </li>
           <li>
            p.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=Ro0hASPfC68C&amp;pg=PA286" rel="nofollow">
             286
            </a>
            (note 6.70). "Worldwide rentals for
            <i>
             The Best Years of Our Lives
            </i>
            amounted to $14,750,000."
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-204">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-204">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFBurns2010">
           Burns, Douglas (2010),
           <i>
            The Best years of Our Lives
           </i>
           , p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA301" rel="nofollow">
            301
           </a>
           ,
           <q>
            The film made a $5 million profit on worldwide rentals of $14.8 million.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+Best+years+of+Our+Lives&amp;rft.pages=301&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Burns&amp;rft.aufirst=Douglas&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
          In:
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          .
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-HallNeale285-205">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale285_205-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale285_205-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">
           Hall &amp; Neale 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA285" rel="nofollow">
           285
          </a>
          (note 6.56). "The cost of
          <i>
           Duel in the Sun
          </i>
          has been reported as both $5,255,000 (Haver,
          <i>
           David O'Selznick's Hollywood
          </i>
          , 361) and $6,480,000 (Thomson,
          <i>
           Showman: The Life of David O'Selznick
          </i>
          , 472); the latter figure may include distribution expenses.
          <i>
           Forever Amber
          </i>
          cost $6,375,000 (Solomon,
          <i>
           Twentieth Century-Fox: A Corporate and Financial History
          </i>
          , 243)."
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-206">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-206">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFChopra-Gant2006">
           Chopra-Gant, Mike (2006).
           <i>
            Hollywood Genres and Post-war America: Masculinity, Family and Nation in Popular Movies and Film Noir
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/I.B._Tauris" title="I.B. Tauris">
            I.B. Tauris
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=RVdCSSVd_KQC&amp;pg=PA18" rel="nofollow">
            18
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-85043-815-1" title="Special:BookSources/978-1-85043-815-1">
            <bdi>
             978-1-85043-815-1
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            <i>
             Forever Amber
            </i>
            : $8 million;
            <i>
             Unconquered
            </i>
            : $7.5 million;
            <i>
             Life with Father
            </i>
            : $6.25 million
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Hollywood+Genres+and+Post-war+America%3A+Masculinity%2C+Family+and+Nation+in+Popular+Movies+and+Film+Noir&amp;rft.pages=18&amp;rft.pub=I.B.+Tauris&amp;rft.date=2006&amp;rft.isbn=978-1-85043-815-1&amp;rft.aulast=Chopra-Gant&amp;rft.aufirst=Mike&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>

         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">

         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-208">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1" id="CITEREFMiller">
           Miller, Frank.
           <a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/2332/Easter-Parade/articles.html" rel="nofollow">
