           </a>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">
            Turner Classic Movies
           </a>
           <span class="reference-accessdate">
            . Retrieved
            <span class="nowrap">
             July 19,
            </span>
            2012
           </span>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=Easter+Parade+%281948%29+%E2%80%93+Articles&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft.aulast=Miller&amp;rft.aufirst=Frank&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F2332%2FEaster-Parade%2Farticles.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-209">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-209">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFStreet2002">
           Street, Sarah (2002).
           <i>
            Transatlantic Crossings: British Feature Films in the United States
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Continuum_International_Publishing_Group" title="Continuum International Publishing Group">
            Continuum International Publishing Group
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=Y5cn_OiCJRkC&amp;pg=PA110" rel="nofollow">
            110
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-8264-1395-6" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-8264-1395-6">
            <bdi>
             978-0-8264-1395-6
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            Although both films had higher than average budgets (
            <i>
             The Red Shoes
            </i>
            cost £505,581 and
            <i>
             Hamlet
            </i>
            cost £572,530, while the average cost of the other thirty films for which Rank supplied information was £233,000), they resulted in high takings at home and abroad.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Transatlantic+Crossings%3A+British+Feature+Films+in+the+United+States&amp;rft.pages=110&amp;rft.pub=Continuum+International+Publishing+Group&amp;rft.date=2002&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-8264-1395-6&amp;rft.aulast=Street&amp;rft.aufirst=Sarah&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-210">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-210">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1" id="CITEREFOfficer2011">
           Officer, Lawrence H. (2011).
           <a class="external text" href="http://www.measuringworth.com/datasets/exchangepound/result.php?year_source=1791&amp;year_result=2011" rel="nofollow">
            "Dollar-Pound Exchange Rate From 1791"

           . MeasuringWorth
           <span class="reference-accessdate">
            . Retrieved
            <span class="nowrap">
             November 18,
            </span>
            2012
           </span>
           .
           <q>
            1947–1948: $4.03 (per British pound)
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=Dollar-Pound+Exchange+Rate+From+1791&amp;rft.pub=MeasuringWorth&amp;rft.date=2011&amp;rft.aulast=Officer&amp;rft.aufirst=Lawrence+H.&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.measuringworth.com%2Fdatasets%2Fexchangepound%2Fresult.php%3Fyear_source%3D1791%26year_result%3D2011&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>

          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1">

          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Numbers&amp;rft.atitle=The+Snake+Pit&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.the-numbers.com%2Fmovies%2F1948%2F0SNPI.php&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>

        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-212">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-212">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation news cs1">
           "
           <span class="cs1-kern-left">
            '
           </span>
           Snake Pit' Seen No Problem After All".
           <i>
            <a href="/wiki/Variety_(magazine)" title="Variety (magazine)">
             Variety
            </a>
           </i>
           . January 19, 1949. p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/stream/variety173-1949-01#page/n348/mode/1up" rel="nofollow">
            7
           </a>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Variety&amp;rft.atitle=%27Snake+Pit%27+Seen+No+Problem+After+All&amp;rft.pages=7&amp;rft.date=1949-01-19&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-HallNeale136-213">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale136_213-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale136_213-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">
           Hall &amp; Neale 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA136" rel="nofollow">
           136
          </a>
          –139
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             Samson and Delilah
            </i>
            : "...the film became the highest grosser in the studio's history to date, with domestic rentals of $7,976,730 by 1955 and a further $6,232,520 overseas...For all their spectacle,
            <i>
             Samson
            </i>
            and
            <i>
             David
            </i>
            were quite economically produced, costing $3,097,563 and $2,170,000 respectively."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Quo Vadis
            </i>
            : "Production costs totaled a record $7,623,000...Worldwide rentals totaled $21,037,000, almost half of which came from the foreign market."
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-214">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-214">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1">
           <a class="external text" href="https://www.the-numbers.com/movie/Cinderella-(1950)" rel="nofollow">
            "Cinderella (1950)"
           </a>
           .
           <i>
            The Numbers
           </i>
           . Nash Information Services
           <span class="reference-accessdate">
            . Retrieved
            <span class="nowrap">
             January 2,
            </span>
            2017
           </span>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Numbers&amp;rft.atitle=Cinderella+%281950%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.the-numbers.com%2Fmovie%2FCinderella-%281950%29&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-215">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-215">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFEisnerSchwartz2009">
           Eisner, Michael D.; Schwartz, Tony (2009).
           <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/workinprogress00eisn_0/page/178" rel="nofollow">
            <i>
             Work in Progress
            </i>
           </a>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Pennsylvania_State_University" title="Pennsylvania State University">
            Pennsylvania State University
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=73_WAAAAMAAJ&amp;q=cinderella+%22Released+in+February+1950,+it+cost+nearly+%243+million+to+make+but+earned+more+than+%2420+million+worldwide%22" rel="nofollow">
            178
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-7868-8507-7" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-7868-8507-7">
            <bdi>
             978-0-7868-8507-7
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            <i>
             Cinderella
            </i>
            revived its fortunes. Re-released in February 1950, it cost nearly $3 million to make but earned more than $20 million worldwide.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Work+in+Progress&amp;rft.pages=178&amp;rft.pub=Pennsylvania+State+University&amp;rft.date=2009&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-7868-8507-7&amp;rft.aulast=Eisner&amp;rft.aufirst=Michael+D.&amp;rft.au=Schwartz%2C+Tony&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Farchive.org%2Fdetails%2Fworkinprogress00eisn_0%2Fpage%2F178&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-216">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-216">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFBarrier2003">
           Barrier, Michael (2003).
           <i>
            Hollywood Cartoons: American Animation in Its Golden Age
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Oxford_University_Press" title="Oxford University Press">
            Oxford University Press
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=zDJXnzMh7bkC&amp;pg=PA401#v=snippet&amp;q=%242.2%20million%20cinderella%20gross%20rentals%20disney%20and%20rko%20%247.8%20million" rel="nofollow">
            401
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-19-516729-0" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-19-516729-0">
            <bdi>
             978-0-19-516729-0
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            It cost around $2.2 million, little more than each of the two package features, Melody Time and The Adventures of Ichabod and Mr. Toad (as Tluo Fabulous Characters had ultimately been named), that just preceded it, but its gross rentals—an amount shared by Disney and RKO—were $7.8 million, almost twice as much as the two package features combined.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Hollywood+Cartoons%3A+American+Animation+in+Its+Golden+Age&amp;rft.pages=401&amp;rft.pub=Oxford+University+Press&amp;rft.date=2003&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-19-516729-0&amp;rft.aulast=Barrier&amp;rft.aufirst=Michael&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Mannix-217">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-Mannix_217-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1">
           <i>
            The E. J. Mannix ledger
           </i>
           . Margaret Herrick Library,
           <a href="/wiki/Academy_of_Motion_Picture_Arts_and_Sciences" title="Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences">
            Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences
           </a>
           : Howard Strickling Collection. 1962.
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+E.+J.+Mannix+ledger&amp;rft.place=Margaret+Herrick+Library%2C+Academy+of+Motion+Picture+Arts+and+Sciences&amp;rft.pub=Howard+Strickling+Collection&amp;rft.date=1962&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Lev-218">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-Lev_218-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-Lev_218-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFLev2006">
           Lev, Peter (2006).
           <i>
            Transforming the Screen, 1950–1959
           </i>
           . Volume 7 of History of the American Cinema.
           <a href="/wiki/University_of_California_Press" title="University of California Press">
            University of California Press
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-520-24966-0" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-520-24966-0">
            <bdi>
             978-0-520-24966-0
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Transforming+the+Screen%2C+1950%E2%80%931959&amp;rft.pub=University+of+California+Press&amp;rft.date=2006&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-520-24966-0&amp;rft.aulast=Lev&amp;rft.aufirst=Peter&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             Quo Vadis
            </i>
            : p.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=TEGl2Ele_XoC&amp;pg=PA15" rel="nofollow">
             15
            </a>
            . "MGM's most expensive film of the period,
            <i>
             Quo Vadis
            </i>
            (1951) also did extremely well. The cost was $7,623,000, earnings were an estimated $21.2 million (with foreign earnings almost 50 percent of this total), and profit was estimated at $5,562,000."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Rear Window
            </i>
            : pp.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=TEGl2Ele_XoC&amp;pg=PA203" rel="nofollow">
             203
            </a>
            –
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=TEGl2Ele_XoC&amp;pg=PA204" rel="nofollow">
             204
            </a>
            . "Rear Window (1954) was an excellent commercial success, with a cost of $1 million and North American rentals of $5.3 million."
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-BlockWilson335-219">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson335_219-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson335_219-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA335" rel="nofollow">
           335
          </a>
          .
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             The Robe
            </i>
            : "Domestic Rentals: $16.7; Foreign Rentals: $9.4; Production Cost: $4.1 (Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Quo Vadis
            </i>
            : "Domestic Rentals: $11.1; Foreign Rentals: $15.6; Production Cost: $7.5 (Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-220">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-220">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFMulligan1956">
           Mulligan, Hugh A. (September 23, 1956). "Cinerama Pushing Ahead As Biggest Money-Maker".
           <i>
            <a href="/wiki/The_Register-Guard" title="The Register-Guard">
             The Register-Guard
            </a>
           </i>
           . Eugene, Oregon. p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://news.google.com/newspapers?id=GPRVAAAAIBAJ&amp;sjid=YeIDAAAAIBAJ&amp;pg=6899,3861317" rel="nofollow">
            7B
           </a>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Register-Guard&amp;rft.atitle=Cinerama+Pushing+Ahead+As+Biggest+Money-Maker&amp;rft.pages=7B&amp;rft.date=1956-09-23&amp;rft.aulast=Mulligan&amp;rft.aufirst=Hugh+A.&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-221">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-221">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFZone2012">
           Zone, Ray (2012).
           <i>
            3-D Revolution: The History of Modern Stereoscopic Cinema
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/University_Press_of_Kentucky" title="University Press of Kentucky">
            University Press of Kentucky
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=k2Ajw-EEOEkC&amp;pg=PA71" rel="nofollow">
            71
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-8131-3611-0" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-8131-3611-0">
            <bdi>
             978-0-8131-3611-0
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            Produced at a cost of $1 million,
            <i>
             This is Cinerama
            </i>
            ran 122 weeks, earning $4.7 million in its initial New York run alone and eventually grossed over $32 million. It was obvious to Hollywood that the public was ready for a new form of motion picture entertainment. The first five Cinerama feature-length travelogues, though they only played in twenty-two theaters, pulled in a combined gross of $82 million.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=3-D+Revolution%3A+The+History+of+Modern+Stereoscopic+Cinema&amp;rft.pages=71&amp;rft.pub=University+Press+of+Kentucky&amp;rft.date=2012&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-8131-3611-0&amp;rft.aulast=Zone&amp;rft.aufirst=Ray&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-222">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-222">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFBurns2010">
           Burns, Douglas (2010),
           <i>
            The Greatest Show on Earth
           </i>
           , pp. 354–
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA355" rel="nofollow">
            355
           </a>
           ,
           <q>
            By May 1953,
            <i>
             Variety
            </i>
            was reporting that the Best Picture winner had amassed $18.35 million in worldwide rentals.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+Greatest+Show+on+Earth&amp;rft.pages=354-355&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Burns&amp;rft.aufirst=Douglas&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
          In:
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          .
         </span>
        </li>

          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation magazine cs1">
           <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/stream/variety193-1954-01#page/n301/mode/2up" rel="nofollow">
            "Top Grossers of 1953"
           </a>
           .
           <i>

           . January 13, 1954. p. 10
           <span class="reference-accessdate">

          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Variety&amp;rft.atitle=Top+Grossers+of+1953&amp;rft.pages=10&amp;rft.date=1954-01-13&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Farchive.org%2Fstream%2Fvariety193-1954-01%23page%2Fn301%2Fmode%2F2up&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">

        <li id="cite_note-HallNeale147-226">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale147_226-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">
           Hall &amp; Neale 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA147" rel="nofollow">
           147
          </a>
          –
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA148" rel="nofollow">
           148
          </a>
          . "To take full advantage of CinemaScope's panoramic possibilities, shooting was delayed for the sets to be redesigned and rebuilt, adding $500,000 to the eventual $4.1 million budget...It ultimately returned domestic rentals of $17.5 million and $25 million worldwide, placing it second only to
          <i>
           Gone with the Wind
          </i>
          in
          <i>
           Variety
          </i>
          <span class="nowrap" style="padding-left:0.1em;">
           '
          </span>
          s annually updated chart."
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-227">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-227">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA367&amp;q=%22foreign%20rentals%22" rel="nofollow">
           367
          </a>
          . "It brought in $16.7 million in domestic rentals, $9.4 million in foreign rentals, and made a net profit of $8.1 million."
         </span>
        </li>

        <li id="cite_note-231">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-231">

          . "VistaVision was first used for the musical
          <i>
           White Christmas
          </i>
          (1954), which
          <i>
           Variety
          </i>
          named the top grosser of its year with anticipated domestic rentals of $12 million."

         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFMinego1956">
           Minego, Pete (May 21, 1956). "Pete's Pungent Patter".

            <a href="/wiki/Portsmouth_Daily_Times" title="Portsmouth Daily Times">
             Portsmouth Daily Times
            </a>

           . Portsmouth, Ohio. p.

            19
           </a>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Portsmouth+Daily+Times&amp;rft.atitle=Pete%27s+Pungent+Patter&amp;rft.pages=19&amp;rft.date=1956-05-21&amp;rft.aulast=Minego&amp;rft.aufirst=Pete&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-238">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-238">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1">
           <a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/493857/Cinerama-Holiday/notes.html" rel="nofollow">
            "Cinerama Holiday (1955) – Notes"
           </a>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">
            Turner Classic Movies
           </a>
           <span class="reference-accessdate">
            . Retrieved
            <span class="nowrap">
             July 7,
            </span>
            2012
           </span>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=Cinerama+Holiday+%281955%29+%E2%80%93+Notes&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F493857%2FCinerama-Holiday%2Fnotes.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-239">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-239">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          <ul>
           <li>
            p.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA382" rel="nofollow">
             382
            </a>
            . "Production Cost: $2.4 (Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)"
           </li>
           <li>
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFBurns2010">
             Burns, Douglas (2010),
             <i>
              Mister Roberts
             </i>
             , p.
             <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA383#v=onepage&amp;q=%22%249.9%20million%22" rel="nofollow">
              383
             </a>
             ,
             <q>
              <i>
               Mister Roberts
              </i>
              sailed onto movie screens buoyed by enthusiastic reviews and receptive audiences. For pr, Fonda, Cagney, and lemmon reenacted several scenes on ed sullivan's popular
              <i>
               Toast of the Town
              </i>
              television variety show. It returned a net profit of $4.5 million on worldwide rentals of $9.9 million, putting it in the top 5 domestic films of 1955.
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Mister+Roberts&amp;rft.pages=383&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Burns&amp;rft.aufirst=Douglas&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-BlockWilson327-240">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson327_240-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA327" rel="nofollow">
           327
          </a>
          . "Production cost: $13.3 million; Domestic Film Rental: $31.3; Foreign Film Rental: $23.9; Worldwide Box office (estimated): $122.7 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-HallNeale159-241">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale159_241-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale159_241-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">
           Hall &amp; Neale 2010
          </a>
          , pp.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA159" rel="nofollow">
           159
          </a>
          –161
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             The Ten Commandments
            </i>
            : "No film did more to entrench roadshow policy than
            <i>
             The Ten Commandments
            </i>
            . While the success of
            <i>
             This Is Cinerama
            </i>
            ,
            <i>
             The Robe
            </i>
            , and even
            <i>
             Eighty Days
            </i>
            could be attributed, at least in part, to their respective photographic and projection formats, that of DeMille's film (which cost a record $13,266,491) could not...General release began at normal prices in 1959 and continued until the end of the following year, when the film was temporarily withdrawn (the first of several reissues came in 1966). The worldwide rental by this time was around $60 million. In the domestic market it dislodged
            <i>
             Gone with the Wind
            </i>
            from the number one position on Variety's list of All-Time Rentals Champs.
            <i>
             GWTW
            </i>
            had hitherto maintained its lead through several reissues (and was soon to regain it through another in 1961)."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             The Bridge on the River Kwai
            </i>
            : Columbia's Anglo-American war film
            <i>
             The Bridge on the River Kwai
            </i>
            (1957) opened on a roadshow basis in selected U.S. cities (including New York, Chicago, Boston, and Los Angeles) and in London. Costing only $2,840,000 to produce, it grossed $30.6 million worldwide on first release."
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-242">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-242">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">
           Hall &amp; Neale 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA153" rel="nofollow">
           153
          </a>
          . "
          <i>
           South Pacific
          </i>
          also became for a time the most successful film ever released in the United Kingdom, where it earned a box-office gross three times its negative cost of $5,610,000. Anticipated global rentals after three years were $30 million."
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-243">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-243">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFRoss2011">
           Ross, Steven J. (2011).
           <i>
            Hollywood Left and Right: How Movie Stars Shaped American Politics
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Oxford_University_Press" title="Oxford University Press">
            Oxford University Press
           </a>
           . pp.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=pn1CuZUHk9MC&amp;pg=PA278#v=twopage" rel="nofollow">
            278–279
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-19-991143-1" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-19-991143-1">
            <bdi>
             978-0-19-991143-1
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            Costing $15 million to produce, the film earned $47 million by the end of 1961 and $90 million worldwide by January 1989.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Hollywood+Left+and+Right%3A+How+Movie+Stars+Shaped+American+Politics&amp;rft.pages=278-279&amp;rft.pub=Oxford+University+Press&amp;rft.date=2011&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-19-991143-1&amp;rft.aulast=Ross&amp;rft.aufirst=Steven+J.&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-244">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-244">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA324" rel="nofollow">
           324
          </a>
          . "Worldwide box office: $146.9 million; Worldwide rentals: $66.1 million; Production cost: $15.9 million. (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)"
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-245">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-245">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFReid2006">
           Reid, John Howard (2006).
           <i>
            America's Best, Britain's Finest: A Survey of Mixed Movies
           </i>
           . Volume 14 of Hollywood classics.
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Lulu_(company)" title="Lulu (company)">
            Lulu
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=zCm5aNkafSYC&amp;pg=PA243" rel="nofollow">
            243
           </a>
           –245.
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-4116-7877-4" title="Special:BookSources/978-1-4116-7877-4">
            <bdi>
             978-1-4116-7877-4
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            Negative cost: around $4 million; Worldwide film rentals gross (including 1968 American reissue) to 1970: $30 million.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=America%27s+Best%2C+Britain%27s+Finest%3A+A+Survey+of+Mixed+Movies&amp;rft.pages=243-245&amp;rft.pub=Lulu&amp;rft.date=2006&amp;rft.isbn=978-1-4116-7877-4&amp;rft.aulast=Reid&amp;rft.aufirst=John+Howard&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-246">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-246">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFWebster2010">
           Webster, Patrick (2010).
           <i>
            Love and Death in Kubrick: A Critical Study of the Films from Lolita Through Eyes Wide Shut
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/McFarland_%26_Company" title="McFarland &amp; Company">
            McFarland &amp; Company
           </a>
           . pp.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=Bz_x37RSSqIC&amp;pg=PA298" rel="nofollow">
            298
           </a>
           (note 2.23).
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-7864-5916-2" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-7864-5916-2">
            <bdi>
             978-0-7864-5916-2
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            <i>
             Spartacus
            </i>
            cost $12 million and grossed some $60 million at the box office, figures Kubrick rarely again matched.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Love+and+Death+in+Kubrick%3A+A+Critical+Study+of+the+Films+from+Lolita+Through+Eyes+Wide+Shut&amp;rft.pages=298+%28note+2.23%29&amp;rft.pub=McFarland+%26+Company&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-7864-5916-2&amp;rft.aulast=Webster&amp;rft.aufirst=Patrick&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-HallNeale179-247">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale179_247-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale179_247-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">
           Hall &amp; Neale 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA179" rel="nofollow">
           179
          </a>
          .
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             Spartacus
            </i>
            : "In the case of
            <i>
             Spartacus
            </i>
            , overseas earnings to 1969 amounted to $12,462,044, while U.S. and Canadian rentals (even including a million-dollar TV sale) were only $10,643,181. But the film failed to show a profit on production costs of $10,284,014 because of the distribution charges and expenses amounting to an additional $15,308,083."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             The Bible
            </i>
            : "
            <i>
             The Bible—In the Beginning...
            </i>
            (1966) was financed by the Italian producer Dino De Laurentiis from private investors and Swiss banks. He then sold distribution rights outside Italy jointly to Fox and Seven Arts for $15 million (70 percent of which came from Fox), thereby recouping the bulk of his $18 million investment. Although
            <i>
             The Bible
            </i>
            returned a respectable world rental of $25.3 million, Fox was still left with a net loss of just over $1.5 million. It was the last biblical epic to be released by any major Hollywood studio for nearly twenty years."
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-248">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-248">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>

         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">

            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1" id="CITEREFTube.1961">
           Tube. (January 18, 1961).
           <a class="external text" href="https://www.variety.com/review/VE1117793715" rel="nofollow">
            "One Hundred and One Dalmatians"
           </a>
           .

          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Daily+Variety&amp;rft.atitle=One+Hundred+and+One+Dalmatians&amp;rft.date=1961-01-18&amp;rft.au=Tube.&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.variety.com%2Freview%2FVE1117793715&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-250">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-250">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFBlock2010">
           Block, Hayley Taylor (2010),
           <i>
            West Side Story
           </i>
           , p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA449" rel="nofollow">
            449
           </a>
           ,
           <q>
            With its three rereleases, it took in over $105 million in worldwide box office ($720 million in 2005 dollars).
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=West+Side+Story&amp;rft.pages=449&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Block&amp;rft.aufirst=Hayley+Taylor&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
          In:
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          .
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-BlockWilson434-251">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson434_251-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>

             </b>

           <sup>

          <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson434_251-5">
           <sup>

             <b>
              f
             </b>
            </i>

          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson434_251-6">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              g
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA434" rel="nofollow">
           434
          </a>
          .
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             The Sound of Music
            </i>
            : "Domestic Rentals: $68.4; Foreign Rentals: $46.2; Production Cost: $8.0 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             The Dirty Dozen
            </i>
            : "Domestic Rentals: $20.1; Foreign Rentals: $11.2; Production Cost: $5.4 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             2001: A Space Odyssey
            </i>
            : "Domestic Rentals: $16.4; Foreign Rentals: $5.5; Production Cost: $10.3 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Cleopatra
            </i>
            : "Domestic Rentals: $22.1; Foreign Rentals: $18.2; Production Cost: $44.0 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             West Side Story
            </i>
            : "Domestic Rentals: $16.2; Foreign Rentals: $15.6; Production Cost: $7.0 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             The Longest Day
            </i>
            : "Domestic Rentals: $13.9; Foreign Rentals: $19.3; Production Cost: $8.6 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid
            </i>
            : "Domestic Rentals: $29.2; Foreign Rentals: $7.9; Production Cost: $6.6 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-252">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-252">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <i>
           Lawrence of Arabia
          </i>
          <ul>
           <li>
            1962 release:
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation web cs1">
             <a class="external text" href="https://www.the-numbers.com/movies/1962/0LWRN.php" rel="nofollow">
              "Lawrence of Arabia"
             </a>
             .
             <i>
              The Numbers
             </i>
             . Nash Information Services. LLC
             <span class="reference-accessdate">
              . Retrieved
              <span class="nowrap">
               August 15,
              </span>
              2011
             </span>
             .
             <q>
              Worldwide Box Office: $69,995,385; International Box Office: $32,500,000
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Numbers&amp;rft.atitle=Lawrence+of+Arabia&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.the-numbers.com%2Fmovies%2F1962%2F0LWRN.php&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
           <li>
            U.S. total (including reissues):
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation web cs1">
             <a class="external text" href="http://pro.boxoffice.com/movie/5851/lawrence-of-arabia" rel="nofollow">
              "Lawrence of Arabia"
             </a>
             .
             <i>
              <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Boxoffice_(magazine)" title="Boxoffice (magazine)">
               Boxoffice
              </a>
             </i>
             <span class="reference-accessdate">
              . Retrieved
              <span class="nowrap">
               May 29,
              </span>
              2016
             </span>
             .
             <q>
              $44,824,852
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Boxoffice&amp;rft.atitle=Lawrence+of+Arabia&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fpro.boxoffice.com%2Fmovie%2F5851%2Flawrence-of-arabia&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-HallNeale165-253">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale165_253-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale165_253-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale165_253-2">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              c
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">
           Hall &amp; Neale 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA165" rel="nofollow">
           165
          </a>
          –
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA166" rel="nofollow">
           166
          </a>
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             Lawrence of Arabia
            </i>
            : Columbia released the $13.8 million
            <i>
             Lawrence of Arabia
            </i>
            (1962), filmed in Super Panavision 70, exclusively on a hard-ticket basis, but opened
            <i>
             Barabbas
            </i>
            (1962),
            <i>
             The Cardinal
            </i>
            (1963), and the $12 million Joseph Conrad adaptation
            <i>
             Lord Jim
            </i>
            (1965) as 70 mm roadshows in selected territories only."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             The Longest Day
            </i>
            : "Darryl's most ambitious independent production was
            <i>
             The Longest Day
            </i>
            (1962), a three-hour reconstruction of D-Day filmed in black-and-white CinemaScope at a cost of $8 million. It grossed over $30 million worldwide as a roadshow followed by general release, thereby helping the studio regain stability during its period of reorganization."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Cleopatra
            </i>
            : "With top tickets set at an all-time high of $5.50,
            <i>
             Cleopatra
            </i>
            had amassed as much as $20 million in such guarantees from exhibitors even before its premiere. Fox claimed the film had cost in total $44 million, of which $31,115,000 represented the direct negative cost and the rest distribution, print and advertising expenses. (These figures excluded the more than $5 million spent on the production's abortive British shoot in 1960–61, prior to its relocation to Italy.) By 1966 worldwide rentals had reached $38,042,000 including $23.5 million from the United States."
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-HallNeale164-254">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale164_254-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">
           Hall &amp; Neale 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA164" rel="nofollow">
           164
          </a>
          . "
          <i>
           West
          </i>
          cost $14,483,000; although it earned $35 million worldwide in just under three years, with ultimate domestic rentals totaling $20,932,883, high distribution costs severely limited its profitability."
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Bond_grosses-255">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-Bond_grosses_255-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-Bond_grosses_255-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-Bond_grosses_255-2">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              c
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-Bond_grosses_255-3">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              d
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          , pp.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA428#v=twopage" rel="nofollow">
           428–429
          </a>
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             From Russia With Love
            </i>
            : "Worldwide Box Office: 78.9; Production Cost: 2.0 (in millions of $s)"
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Goldfinger
            </i>
            : "Worldwide Box Office: 124.9; Production Cost: 3.0 (in millions of $s)"
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Diamonds Are Forever
            </i>
            : "Worldwide Box Office: 116; Production Cost: 7.2 (in millions of $s)"
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Moonraker
            </i>
            : "Worldwide Box Office: 210.3; Production Cost: 34.0 (in millions of $s)"
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Chapman_(2007)-256">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-Chapman_(2007)_256-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-Chapman_(2007)_256-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFChapman2007">
           Chapman, James (2007).
           <i>
            Licence to thrill: a cultural history of the James Bond films
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/I.B._Tauris" title="I.B. Tauris">
            I.B. Tauris
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-84511-515-9" title="Special:BookSources/978-1-84511-515-9">
            <bdi>
             978-1-84511-515-9
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Licence+to+thrill%3A+a+cultural+history+of+the+James+Bond+films&amp;rft.pub=I.B.+Tauris&amp;rft.date=2007&amp;rft.isbn=978-1-84511-515-9&amp;rft.aulast=Chapman&amp;rft.aufirst=James&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             From Russia With Love
            </i>
            : "The American release of
            <i>
             From Russia With Love
            </i>
            again followed on some six months after it had been shown in Britain. North American rentals of $9.9 million were an improvement on its predecessor, helped by a slightly wider release, though they were still only half the $19.5 million of foreign rentals... (
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=E8wqAQAAIAAJ&amp;q=%22the+american+release+of+from+russia+with+love%22" rel="nofollow">
             Online copy
            </a>
            at
            <a href="/wiki/Google_Books" title="Google Books">
             Google Books
            </a>
            )"
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Diamonds Are Forever
            </i>
            : "
            <i>
             Diamonds Are Forever
            </i>
            marked a return to the box-office heights of the Bond films of the mid-1960s. Its worldwide rentals were $45.7 million..."
            <sup class="noprint Inline-Template" style="white-space:nowrap;">
             [
             <i>
              <a href="/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources" title="Wikipedia:Citing sources">
               <span title="This citation requires a reference to the specific page or range of pages in which the material appears. (November 2018)">
                page needed
               </span>
              </a>
             </i>
             ]
            </sup>
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Moonraker
            </i>
            : "These figures were surpassed by
            <i>
             Moonraker
            </i>
            , which earned total worldwide rentals of $87.7 million, of which $33 million came from North America. (
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=E8wqAQAAIAAJ&amp;q=$87.7+million" rel="nofollow">
             Online copy
            </a>
            at
            <a href="/wiki/Google_Books" title="Google Books">
             Google Books
            </a>
            )"
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Balio_(2009)-257">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-Balio_(2009)_257-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-Balio_(2009)_257-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFBalio2009">
           Balio, Tino (2009).
           <i>
            United Artists, Volume 2, 1951–1978: the Company that Changed the Film Industry
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/University_of_Wisconsin_Press" title="University of Wisconsin Press">
            University of Wisconsin Press
           </a>
           . p. 261.
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-299-23014-2" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-299-23014-2">
            <bdi>
             978-0-299-23014-2
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=United+Artists%2C+Volume+2%2C+1951%E2%80%931978%3A+the+Company+that+Changed+the+Film+Industry&amp;rft.pages=261&amp;rft.pub=University+of+Wisconsin+Press&amp;rft.date=2009&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-299-23014-2&amp;rft.aulast=Balio&amp;rft.aufirst=Tino&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             From Russia With Love
            </i>
            : "The picture grossed twice as much as
            <i>
             Dr. No
            </i>
            , both domestic and foreign—$12.5 million worldwide (
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=KKoLAQAAMAAJ&amp;q=%22%2412.5+million+worldwide%22" rel="nofollow">
             Online copy
            </a>
            at
            <a href="/wiki/Google_Books" title="Google Books">
             Google Books
            </a>
            )"
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Goldfinger
            </i>
            : "Produced on a budget of around $3 million,
            <i>
             Goldfinger
            </i>
            grossed a phenomenal $46 million worldwide the first time around. (
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=KKoLAQAAMAAJ&amp;q=Goldfinger+%22$46+million+worldwide%22" rel="nofollow">
             Online copy
            </a>
            at
            <a href="/wiki/Google_Books" title="Google Books">
             Google Books
            </a>
            )"
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-HallNeale184-258">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale184_258-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale184_258-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">
           Hall &amp; Neale 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA184" rel="nofollow">
           184
          </a>
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             My Fair Lady
            </i>
            : "
            <i>
             My Fair Lady
            </i>
            (1964) cost Warners $17 million to make, including a record $5.5 million just for the film rights to the Alan Jay Lerner and Frederick Loewe stage show and a million-dollar fee for star Audrey Hepburn. By 1967 it was reported to have grossed $55 million from roadshowing worldwide."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Mary Poppins
            </i>
            : "
            <i>
             Mary Poppins
            </i>
            (1964), which cost $5.2 million, was neither a stage adaptation nor a roadshow. But by the end of its first release, it had grossed nearly $50 million worldwide."
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-259">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-259">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFBurns2010">
           Burns, Douglas (2010),
           <i>
            Mary Poppins
           </i>
           , p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA469" rel="nofollow">
            469
           </a>
           ,
           <q>
            In its initial run,
            <i>
             Poppins
            </i>
            garnered an astounding $44 million in worldwide rentals and became the company's first Best Picture Oscar contender.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Mary+Poppins&amp;rft.pages=469&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Burns&amp;rft.aufirst=Douglas&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
          In:
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          .
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-260">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-260">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>

           .

         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">

           <a href="#cite_ref-262">

           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1">
