            "Hawaii"
           </a>
           .
           <i>
            The Numbers
           </i>
           . Nash Information Services. LLC
           <span class="reference-accessdate">
            . Retrieved
            <span class="nowrap">
             August 18,
            </span>
            2011
           </span>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Numbers&amp;rft.atitle=Hawaii&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.the-numbers.com%2Fmovies%2F1966%2F0HWII.php&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-263">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-263">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1">
           <a class="external text" href="https://www.the-numbers.com/movie/Whos-Afraid-of-Virginia-Wolf#tab=summary" rel="nofollow">
            "Who's Afraid of Virginia Wolf? (1966)"
           </a>
           .
           <i>
            The Numbers
           </i>
           . Nash Information Services. LLC
           <span class="reference-accessdate">
            . Retrieved
            <span class="nowrap">
             February 3,
            </span>
            2020
           </span>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Numbers&amp;rft.atitle=Who%27s+Afraid+of+Virginia+Wolf%3F+%281966%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.the-numbers.com%2Fmovie%2FWhos-Afraid-of-Virginia-Wolf%23tab%3Dsummary&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-HallNeale188-264">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale188_264-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">
           Hall &amp; Neale 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA188" rel="nofollow">
           188
          </a>
          . "The negative cost of Warners' adaptation of Edward Albee's play
          <i>
           Who's Afraid of Virginia Woolf?
          </i>
          (1966)—filmed in widescreen and black-and-white, largely set in domestic interiors and with a cast of only four principal actors—amounted to $7,613,000, in part because stars Elizabeth Taylor and Richard Burton received up-front fees of $1 million and $750,000 respectively, against 10 percent of the gross apiece. (Their participation was presumably added to the budget)."
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-265">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-265">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation news cs1">
           "Animals Portray Parts in Disney's "Robin Hood
           <span class="cs1-kern-right">
            "
           </span>
           ".
           <i>
            <a href="/wiki/The_Blade_(Toledo,_Ohio)" title="The Blade (Toledo, Ohio)">
             Toledo Blade
            </a>
           </i>
           . October 18, 1970.
           <a class="external text" href="https://news.google.com/newspapers?nid=1350&amp;dat=19701018&amp;id=k8hOAAAAIBAJ&amp;sjid=xQEEAAAAIBAJ&amp;pg=2133,463153" rel="nofollow">
            Sec. G, p. 7
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            "The Jungle Book," in it's [
            <i>
             sic
            </i>
            ] initial world-wide release, has grossed $23.8 million to date...
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Toledo+Blade&amp;rft.atitle=Animals+Portray+Parts+in+Disney%27s+%22Robin+Hood%22&amp;rft.pages=Sec.+G%2C+p.+7&amp;rft.date=1970-10-18&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-266">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-266">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1">
           <a class="external text" href="https://variety.com/1966/film/reviews/the-jungle-book-1200421257/" rel="nofollow">
            "The Jungle Book"
           </a>
           .
           <i>
            <a href="/wiki/Variety_(magazine)" title="Variety (magazine)">
             Variety
            </a>
           </i>
           . December 31, 1966
           <span class="reference-accessdate">
            . Retrieved
            <span class="nowrap">
             March 14,
            </span>
            2018
           </span>
           .
           <q>
            It was filmed at a declared cost of $4 million over a 42-month period.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Variety&amp;rft.atitle=The+Jungle+Book&amp;rft.date=1966-12-31&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fvariety.com%2F1966%2Ffilm%2Freviews%2Fthe-jungle-book-1200421257%2F&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Denisoff_&amp;_Romanowski_(1991)-267">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-Denisoff_&amp;_Romanowski_(1991)_267-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-Denisoff_&amp;_Romanowski_(1991)_267-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFDenisoffRomanowski1991">
           Denisoff, R. Serge; Romanowski, William D. (1991).
           <i>
            Risky Business: Rock in Film
           </i>
           . Transaction Publishers.
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-88738-843-9" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-88738-843-9">
            <bdi>
             978-0-88738-843-9
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Risky+Business%3A+Rock+in+Film&amp;rft.pub=Transaction+Publishers&amp;rft.date=1991&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-88738-843-9&amp;rft.aulast=Denisoff&amp;rft.aufirst=R.+Serge&amp;rft.au=Romanowski%2C+William+D.&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             The Graduate
            </i>
            : p.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=kT0fKUCTUC4C&amp;pg=PA167" rel="nofollow">
             167
            </a>
            . "World net rental was estimated at more than $85 million by January 1971."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Grease
            </i>
            : p.
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=kT0fKUCTUC4C&amp;pg=PA236" rel="nofollow">
             236
            </a>
            . "The film was produced for $6 million and Paramount reportedly spent another $3 million on promotion."
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-HallNeale191-268">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale191_268-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale191_268-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">
           Hall &amp; Neale 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA191" rel="nofollow">
           191
          </a>
          –192
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             The Graduate
            </i>
            : "
            <i>
             The Graduate
            </i>
            eventually earned U.S. rentals of $44,090,729 on a production cost of $3.1 million to become the most lucrative non-roadshow picture (and independent release) to date."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid
            </i>
            : "None of these films was roadshown in the United States; most were set in contemporary America or had a contemporary "take" on the past (the casting of genuine teenagers to play Romeo and Juliet, the urbane sophistication of the dialogue in
            <i>
             Butch Cassidy
            </i>
            , the antiauthoritarianism of
            <i>
             Bonnie and Clyde
            </i>
            and
            <i>
             MASH
            </i>
            ); most were produced on modest or medium-sized budgets (as low as $450,000 for
            <i>
             Easy Rider
            </i>
            and no higher than $6,825,000 for
            <i>
             Butch Cassidy
            </i>
            ); and all grossed upward of $10 million domestically."
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-269">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-269">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <i>
           2001: A Space Odyssey
          </i>
          <ul>
           <li>
            Total:
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation web cs1" id="CITEREFMiller">
             Miller, Frank.
             <a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.turner.com/tcmdb/title/628/2001-A-Space-Odyssey/articles.html" rel="nofollow">
              "2001: A Space Odyssey (1968) – Articles"
             </a>
             .
             <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">
              Turner Classic Movies
             </a>
             <span class="reference-accessdate">
              . Retrieved
              <span class="nowrap">
               August 24,
              </span>
              2011
             </span>
             .
             <q>
              2001: A Space Odyssey made $15 million on its initial U.S. release, and currently shows a worldwide gross of over $190 million.
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=2001%3A+A+Space+Odyssey+%281968%29+%E2%80%93+Articles&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft.aulast=Miller&amp;rft.aufirst=Frank&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.turner.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F628%2F2001-A-Space-Odyssey%2Farticles.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
           <li>
            As of 2006:
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFPalmer2006">
             Palmer, R. Barton (2006). "2001: The Critical Reception and the Generation Gap".  In Kolker, Robert Phillip (ed.).
             <i>
              Stanley Kubrick's 2001: a Space Odyssey: New Essays
             </i>
             .
             <a href="/wiki/Oxford_University_Press" title="Oxford University Press">
              Oxford University Press
             </a>
             . p.
             <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=YpritcZXPFoC&amp;pg=PA16" rel="nofollow">
              16
             </a>
             .
             <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
              ISBN
             </a>
             <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-19-517452-6" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-19-517452-6">
              <bdi>
               978-0-19-517452-6
              </bdi>
             </a>
             .
             <q>
              With its initial and subsequent releases, domestic and worldwide, Kubrick's arty, intellectual film earned nearly $138 million, which was, at that time, an astounding figure.
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=bookitem&amp;rft.atitle=2001%3A+The+Critical+Reception+and+the+Generation+Gap&amp;rft.btitle=Stanley+Kubrick%27s+2001%3A+a+Space+Odyssey%3A+New+Essays&amp;rft.pages=16&amp;rft.pub=Oxford+University+Press&amp;rft.date=2006&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-19-517452-6&amp;rft.aulast=Palmer&amp;rft.aufirst=R.+Barton&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
           <li>
            2013 re-release:
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation web cs1">
             <a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/movies/?page=intl&amp;id=20012013.htm" rel="nofollow">
              "2001: A Space Odyssey (2013 re-release)"
             </a>
             .
             <i>
              <a href="/wiki/Box_Office_Mojo" title="Box Office Mojo">
               Box Office Mojo
              </a>
             </i>
             <span class="reference-accessdate">
              . Retrieved
              <span class="nowrap">
               September 26,
              </span>
              2018
             </span>
             .
             <q>
              Domestic Total Gross: $214,618; United Kingdom: $568,997
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=2001%3A+A+Space+Odyssey+%282013+re-release%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Fmovies%2F%3Fpage%3Dintl%26id%3D20012013.htm&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
           <li>
            2014 re-release:
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation web cs1">
             <a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/movies/?id=20012014.htm" rel="nofollow">
              "2001: A Space Odyssey (2014 re-release)"
             </a>
             .
             <i>
              <a href="/wiki/Box_Office_Mojo" title="Box Office Mojo">
               Box Office Mojo
              </a>
             </i>
             <span class="reference-accessdate">
              . Retrieved
              <span class="nowrap">
               September 26,
              </span>
              2018
             </span>
             .
             <q>
              Domestic Total Gross: $135,370
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=2001%3A+A+Space+Odyssey+%282014+re-release%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Fmovies%2F%3Fid%3D20012014.htm&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
           <li>
            2018 re-release:
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation web cs1">
             <a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/movies/?page=intl&amp;id=20012018.htm" rel="nofollow">
              "2001: A Space Odyssey (2018 re-release)"
             </a>
             .
             <i>
              <a href="/wiki/Box_Office_Mojo" title="Box Office Mojo">
               Box Office Mojo
              </a>
             </i>
             <span class="reference-accessdate">
              . Retrieved
              <span class="nowrap">
               September 26,
              </span>
              2018
             </span>
             .
             <q>
              Domestic Total Gross: $1,283,820; Australia: $192,457; Greece: $27,510; Netherlands: $159,068; New Zealand: $5,046; Russia: $155,841; United Kingdom: $296,525
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=2001%3A+A+Space+Odyssey+%282018+re-release%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Fmovies%2F%3Fpage%3Dintl%26id%3D20012018.htm&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-270">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-270">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFHaber1969">
           Haber, Joyces (March 27, 1969).
           <a class="external text" href="https://www.google.com/search?hl=en&amp;tbm=nws&amp;q=%22funny+girl%22+%22%2480+to+%24100%22+million+worldwide" rel="nofollow">
            "
            <span class="cs1-kern-left">
             '
            </span>
            Funny Girl' a Box Office Winner"
           </a>
           .
           <i>
            <a href="/wiki/Los_Angeles_Times" title="Los Angeles Times">
             Los Angeles Times
            </a>
           </i>
           . Archived from
           <a class="external text" href="https://pqasb.pqarchiver.com/latimes/access/672252782.html?dids=672252782:672252782&amp;FMT=ABS&amp;FMTS=ABS:AI" rel="nofollow">
            the original
           </a>
           on March 29, 2012
           <span class="reference-accessdate">
            . Retrieved
            <span class="nowrap">
             March 29,
            </span>
            2012
           </span>
           .
           <q>
            ..."Funny Girl" will gross an estimated $80 to $100 million worldwide.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Los+Angeles+Times&amp;rft.atitle=%27Funny+Girl%27+a+Box+Office+Winner&amp;rft.date=1969-03-27&amp;rft.aulast=Haber&amp;rft.aufirst=Joyces&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fpqasb.pqarchiver.com%2Flatimes%2Faccess%2F672252782.html%3Fdids%3D672252782%3A672252782%26FMT%3DABS%26FMTS%3DABS%3AAI&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-271">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-271">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFWelles1970">
           Welles, Chris (September 7, 1970). "Behind the Silence at Columbia Pictures—No Moguls, No Minions, Just Profits".
           <i>
            <a href="/wiki/New_York_(magazine)" title="New York (magazine)">
             New York
            </a>
           </i>
           .
           <b>
            3
           </b>
           (36). New York Media. pp. 42–
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=8-ICAAAAMBAJ&amp;pg=PA47" rel="nofollow">
            47
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            While Columbia, battling Ray Stark over every dollar, did
            <i>
             Funny Girl
            </i>
            for around $8.8 million, a million or so over budget, Fox spent nearly $24 million on
            <i>
             Hello, Dolly!
            </i>
            , more than twice the initial budget, and the film will thus have to gross three times as much to break even.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=New+York&amp;rft.atitle=Behind+the+Silence+at+Columbia+Pictures%E2%80%94No+Moguls%2C+No+Minions%2C+Just+Profits&amp;rft.volume=3&amp;rft.issue=36&amp;rft.pages=42-47&amp;rft.date=1970-09-07&amp;rft.aulast=Welles&amp;rft.aufirst=Chris&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-272">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-272">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <i>
           Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid
          </i>
          <ul>
           <li>
            United States and Canada:
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation web cs1">
             <a class="external text" href="http://pro.boxoffice.com/movie/1631/butch-cassidy-and-the-sundance-kid" rel="nofollow">
              "Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid"
             </a>
             .
             <i>
              <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Boxoffice_(magazine)" title="Boxoffice (magazine)">
               Boxoffice
              </a>
             </i>
             <span class="reference-accessdate">
              . Retrieved
              <span class="nowrap">
               May 29,
              </span>
              2016
             </span>
             .
             <q>
              $102,308,525
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Boxoffice&amp;rft.atitle=Butch+Cassidy+and+the+Sundance+Kid&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fpro.boxoffice.com%2Fmovie%2F1631%2Fbutch-cassidy-and-the-sundance-kid&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
           <li>
            Outside North America:
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation book cs1">
             <a href="/wiki/Vanity_Fair_(magazine)" title="Vanity Fair (magazine)">
              <i>
               Vanity Fair
              </i>
             </a>
             . 2008. p.
             <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=VloTAQAAMAAJ&amp;q=%22by+the+spring+of+1970+it+had+taken+in+$46+million+in+North+America+and+grossed+another+$50+million+abroad%22&amp;dq=%22by+the+spring+of+1970+it+had+taken+in+$46+million+in+North+America+and+grossed+another+$50+million+abroad%22" rel="nofollow">
              388
             </a>
             .
             <q>
              <i>
               Butch Cassidy
              </i>
              went on to be a huge hit—by the spring of 1970 it had taken in $46 million in North America and grossed another $50 million abroad.
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Vanity+Fair&amp;rft.pages=388&amp;rft.date=2008&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-273">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-273">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation news cs1">
           "
           <span class="cs1-kern-left">
            '
           </span>
           Love Story' II: Ryan Redux?".
           <i>
            <a href="/wiki/New_York_(magazine)" title="New York (magazine)">
             New York
            </a>
           </i>
           .
           <b>
            9
           </b>
           . New York Media. 1976. p. 389.
           <q>
            Bring those handkerchiefs out of retirement. ... After all, the first movie made around $80 million worldwide.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=New+York&amp;rft.atitle=%27Love+Story%27+II%3A+Ryan+Redux%3F&amp;rft.volume=9&amp;rft.pages=389&amp;rft.date=1976&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-274">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-274">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFBlock2010">
           Block, Hayley Taylor (2010),
           <i>
            Love Story
           </i>
           , p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA545" rel="nofollow">
            545
           </a>
           ,
           <q>
            The final cost came in at $2,260,000.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Love+Story&amp;rft.pages=545&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Block&amp;rft.aufirst=Hayley+Taylor&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
          In:
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          .
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-275">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-275">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFScott1979">
           Scott, Vernon (June 30, 1979). "
           <span class="cs1-kern-left">
            "
           </span>
           Airports" Flourish".
           <i>
            <a href="/wiki/The_Bryan_Times" title="The Bryan Times">
             The Bryan Times
            </a>
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/United_Press_International" title="United Press International">
            United Press International
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://news.google.com/newspapers?id=_L8wAAAAIBAJ&amp;sjid=x1EDAAAAIBAJ&amp;pg=5730,9236104" rel="nofollow">
            10
           </a>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Bryan+Times&amp;rft.atitle=%22Airports%22+Flourish&amp;rft.pages=10&amp;rft.date=1979-06-30&amp;rft.aulast=Scott&amp;rft.aufirst=Vernon&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-276">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-276">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA541" rel="nofollow">
           541
          </a>
          . "Screenwriter and director George Seaton was given a then-whopping production budget of $10 million to make what would be his last big movie after a long career as an actor in radio, a screenwriter, and a director."
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-277">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-277">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA549" rel="nofollow">
           549
          </a>
          . "Fiddler had the highest domestic box office of 1971 (it was second in worldwide box office after
          <i>
           Diamonds Are Forever
          </i>
          ), with more than $100 million in unadjusted worldwide box office on its initial release. The soundtrack album was also a huge seller. The 1979 rerelease was not as successful, with the $3.8 million print and ad costs almost as high as the $4.3 million in worldwide rentals."
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-BlockWilson527-278">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson527_278-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson527_278-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson527_278-2">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              c
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson527_278-3">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              d
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson527_278-4">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              e
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson527_278-5">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              f
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">
           Block &amp; Wilson 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA527" rel="nofollow">
           527
          </a>
          .
          <ul>
           <li>
            <i>
             Star Wars
            </i>
            : "Domestic Rentals: $127.0; Foreign Rentals: $141.5; Production Cost: $13.0 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             The Godfather
            </i>
            : "Domestic Rentals: $85.6; Foreign Rentals: $42.0; Production Cost: $7.2 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Fiddler on the Roof
            </i>
            : "Domestic Rentals: $34.0; Foreign Rentals: $11.1; Production Cost: $9.0 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."
           </li>
           <li>
            <i>
             Rocky
            </i>
            : "Domestic Rentals: $56.0; Foreign Rentals: $21.1; Production Cost: $1.6 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-279">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-279">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <i>
           The Godfather
          </i>
          <ul>
           <li>
            1974:
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation book cs1">
             <a href="/wiki/Newsweek" title="Newsweek">
              <i>
               Newsweek
              </i>
             </a>
             .
             <b>
              84
             </b>
             . 1974. p.
             <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=sCXkAAAAMAAJ&amp;q=%22%24285+million%22" rel="nofollow">
              74
             </a>
             .
             <q>
              The original
              <i>
               Godfather
              </i>
              has grossed a mind-boggling $285 million...
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Newsweek&amp;rft.pages=74&amp;rft.date=1974&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
           <li>
            1991:
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFVon_Gunden1991">
             Von Gunden, Kenneth (1991).
             <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/postmodernauteur0000vong/page/36" rel="nofollow">
              <i>
               Postmodern auteurs: Coppola, Lucas, De Palma, Spielberg, and Scorsese
              </i>
             </a>
             .
             <a href="/wiki/McFarland_%26_Company" title="McFarland &amp; Company">
              McFarland &amp; Company
             </a>
             . p.
             <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/postmodernauteur0000vong/page/36" rel="nofollow">
              36
             </a>
             .
             <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
              ISBN
             </a>
             <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-89950-618-0" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-89950-618-0">
              <bdi>
               978-0-89950-618-0
              </bdi>
             </a>
             .
             <q>
              Since
              <i>
               The Godfather
              </i>
              had earned over $85 million in U.S.-Canada rentals (the worldwide box-office gross was $285 million), a sequel, according to the usual formula, could be expected to earn approximately two-thirds of the original's box-office take (ultimately
              <i>
               Godfather II
              </i>
              had rentals of $30 million).
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Postmodern+auteurs%3A+Coppola%2C+Lucas%2C+De+Palma%2C+Spielberg%2C+and+Scorsese&amp;rft.pages=36&amp;rft.pub=McFarland+%26+Company&amp;rft.date=1991&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-89950-618-0&amp;rft.aulast=Von+Gunden&amp;rft.aufirst=Kenneth&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Farchive.org%2Fdetails%2Fpostmodernauteur0000vong%2Fpage%2F36&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
           <li>
            Releases:
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation web cs1">
             <a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/title/tt0068646/" rel="nofollow">
              "The Godfather (1972)"
             </a>
             .
             <i>
              <a href="/wiki/Box_Office_Mojo" title="Box Office Mojo">
               Box Office Mojo
              </a>
             </i>
             <span class="reference-accessdate">
              . Retrieved
              <span class="nowrap">
               January 22,
              </span>
              2020
             </span>
             .
             <q>
              Original release: $243,862,778; 1997 re-release: $1,267,490; 2009 re-release: $121,323; 2011 re-release: $818,333; 2014 re-release: $29,349; 2018 re-release: $21,701; Budget: $6,000,000
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=The+Godfather+%281972%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Ftitle%2Ftt0068646%2F&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-280">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-280">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFJacobs1980">
           Jacobs, Diane (1980).
           <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/hollywoodrenaiss00jaco/page/115" rel="nofollow">
            <i>
             Hollywood Renaissance
            </i>
           </a>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Dell_Publishing" title="Dell Publishing">
            Dell Publishing
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=KP0HAQAAMAAJ&amp;q=%22million+in+worldwide+sales%22+godfather" rel="nofollow">
            115
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-440-53382-5" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-440-53382-5">
            <bdi>
             978-0-440-53382-5
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            The Godfather catapulted Coppola to overnight celebrity, earning three Academy Awards and a then record-breaking $142 million in worldwide sales.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Hollywood+Renaissance&amp;rft.pages=115&amp;rft.pub=Dell+Publishing&amp;rft.date=1980&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-440-53382-5&amp;rft.aulast=Jacobs&amp;rft.aufirst=Diane&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Farchive.org%2Fdetails%2Fhollywoodrenaiss00jaco%2Fpage%2F115&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-godfather-tcm-281">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-godfather-tcm_281-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1">
           <a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.turner.com/tcmdb/title/443184/The-Godfather/notes.html" rel="nofollow">
            "The Godfather (1972) – Notes"
           </a>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">
            Turner Classic Movies
           </a>
           <span class="reference-accessdate">
            . Retrieved
            <span class="nowrap">
             January 23,
            </span>
            2020
           </span>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=The+Godfather+%281972%29+%E2%80%93+Notes&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.turner.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F443184%2FThe-Godfather%2Fnotes.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-282">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-282">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1">
           <a class="external text" href="http://pro.boxoffice.com/movie/3253/the-exorcist" rel="nofollow">
            "The Exorcist"
           </a>
           .
           <i>
            <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Boxoffice_(magazine)" title="Boxoffice (magazine)">
             Boxoffice
            </a>
           </i>
           <span class="reference-accessdate">
            . Retrieved
            <span class="nowrap">
             May 29,
            </span>
            2016
           </span>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Boxoffice&amp;rft.atitle=The+Exorcist&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fpro.boxoffice.com%2Fmovie%2F3253%2Fthe-exorcist&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-283">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-283">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFStanleySteinberg1976">
           Stanley, Robert Henry; Steinberg, Charles Side (1976).
           <i>
            The media environment: mass communications in American society
           </i>
           . Hastings House. p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=Z30tAAAAMAAJ&amp;q=%22the+sting%22+million+worldwide+exorcist&amp;dq=%22" rel="nofollow">
            76
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-8038-4681-4" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-8038-4681-4">
            <bdi>
             978-0-8038-4681-4
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            ...further reflected by the phenomenal successes of
            <i>
             The Sting
            </i>
            ,
            <i>
             Chinatown
            </i>
            and
            <i>
             The Exorcist
            </i>
            . The latter film, which cost about $10 million to produce, has grossed over $110 million worldwide.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+media+environment%3A+mass+communications+in+American+society&amp;rft.pages=76&amp;rft.pub=Hastings+House&amp;rft.date=1976&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-8038-4681-4&amp;rft.aulast=Stanley&amp;rft.aufirst=Robert+Henry&amp;rft.au=Steinberg%2C+Charles+Side&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-284">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-284">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation magazine cs1">
           "Big Rental Films of 1974".
           <i>
            <a href="/wiki/Variety_(magazine)" title="Variety (magazine)">
             Variety
            </a>
           </i>
           . January 8, 1975. p. 24.
           <q>
            U.S-Canada market only $66,3000,000
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Variety&amp;rft.atitle=Big+Rental+Films+of+1974&amp;rft.pages=24&amp;rft.date=1975-01-08&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-WBos-285">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-WBos_285-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-WBos_285-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>

         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation magazine cs1" id="CITEREFPollock1979">
           Pollock, Dale (May 9, 1979). "WB Adds To Its Record Collection".
           <i>
            <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Daily_Variety" title="Daily Variety">
             Daily Variety
            </a>
           </i>
           . p. 1.
           <q>
            "Towering Inferno" did $56,000,000 overseas in billings while "The Exorcist" toted up $46,000,000
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Daily+Variety&amp;rft.atitle=WB+Adds+To+Its+Record+Collection&amp;rft.pages=1&amp;rft.date=1979-05-09&amp;rft.aulast=Pollock&amp;rft.aufirst=Dale&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-286">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-286">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation cs2">
           <a href="/wiki/New_York_(magazine)" title="New York (magazine)">
            <i>
             New York
            </i>
           </a>
           ,
           <b>
            8
           </b>
           , New York Media, 1975,
           <q>
            ...
            <i>
             Jaws
            </i>
            should outstrip another MCA hit,
            <i>
             The Sting
            </i>
            , which had world-wide revenues of $115 million. (
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jW8pAQAAIAAJ&amp;q=%22The+Sting%2C+which+had+world-wide%22+revenues" rel="nofollow">

          </span>
         </span>
        </li>

         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-289">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">

          </a>

          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=Ro0hASPfC68C&amp;pg=PA354" rel="nofollow">
           206
          </a>
          –208. "The most successful entry in the disaster cycle was the $15 million
          <i>
           The Towering Inferno
          </i>
          which earned over $48,650,000 in domestic rentals and about $40 million foreign."
         </span>

          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-294">
            ^
           </a>

         </span>

           Priggé, Steven (2004).
           <i>
            Movie Moguls Speak: Interviews With Top Film Producers
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/McFarland_%26_Company" title="McFarland &amp; Company">
            McFarland &amp; Company
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=40UTI-uUHpwC&amp;pg=PA8" rel="nofollow">
            8
           </a>
           .
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
            ISBN
           </a>
           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-7864-1929-6" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-7864-1929-6">
            <bdi>
             978-0-7864-1929-6
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            The budget for the first
            <i>
             Jaws
            </i>
            was $4 million and the picture wound up costing $9 million.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Movie+Moguls+Speak%3A+Interviews+With+Top+Film+Producers&amp;rft.pages=8&amp;rft.pub=McFarland+%26+Company&amp;rft.date=2004&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-7864-1929-6&amp;rft.aulast=Prigg%C3%A9&amp;rft.aufirst=Steven&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-HallNeale214-295">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale214_295-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">
           Hall &amp; Neale 2010
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA214" rel="nofollow">
           214
          </a>
          . "
          <i>
           Rocky
          </i>
          was the "sleeper of the decade". Produced by UA and costing just under $1 million, it went on to earn a box-office gross of $117,235,247 in the United States and $225 million worldwide."
         </span>
        </li>

            583

           ,
           <q>
            The budget was $1,075,000 plus producer's fees of $100,000.

          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Rocky&amp;rft.pages=583&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Block&amp;rft.aufirst=Alex+Ben&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">

          <cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFWuntch1985">
           Wuntch, Philip (July 19, 1985).
           <span class="cs1-lock-subscription" title="Paid subscription required">
            <a class="external text" href="http://nl.newsbank.com/nl-search/we/Archives?p_product=DM&amp;p_text_direct-0=0ED3CD81CFEA1C2E&amp;p_field_direct-0=document_id" rel="nofollow">

            </a>
           </span>
           .
           <i>
            <a href="/wiki/The_Dallas_Morning_News" title="The Dallas Morning News">
             The Dallas Morning News
            </a>
           </i>
           <span class="reference-accessdate">
            . Retrieved
            <span class="nowrap">
             March 6,
            </span>
            2012
           </span>
           .
           <q>
            Its worldwide box-office gross was $619 million, toppling the record of $530 million set by Star Wars.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Dallas+Morning+News&amp;rft.atitle=Return+of+E.T.&amp;rft.date=1985-07-19&amp;rft.aulast=Wuntch&amp;rft.aufirst=Philip&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fnl.newsbank.com%2Fnl-search%2Fwe%2FArchives%3Fp_product%3DDM%26p_text_direct-0%3D0ED3CD81CFEA1C2E%26p_field_direct-0%3Ddocument_id&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>

          </a>

          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA218" rel="nofollow">
           218
          </a>
          . "Eventually costing $11,293,151,
          <i>
           Star Wars
          </i>
          was previewed at the Northpoint Theatre in San Francisco on May 1, 1977."

        </li>

           <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-4596-0007-2" title="Special:BookSources/978-1-4596-0007-2">
            <bdi>
             978-1-4596-0007-2
            </bdi>
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            Despite the fact that Grease was well on its way to becoming the highest-grossing movie musical in the world, and eventually grossed over $341 million...
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Party+Animals%3A+A+Hollywood+Tale+of+Sex%2C+Drugs%2C+and+Rock+%27N%27+Roll+Starring+the+Fabulous+Allan+Carr&amp;rft.pages=145&amp;rft.pub=ReadHowYouWant.com&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.isbn=978-1-4596-0007-2&amp;rft.aulast=Hofler&amp;rft.aufirst=Robert&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-302">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-302">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1">
           <a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/movies/intl/?page=&amp;wk=2018W34&amp;id=_fGREASE401" rel="nofollow">
            "Grease (40th Anniversary)"
           </a>
           .
           <i>
            <a href="/wiki/Box_Office_Mojo" title="Box Office Mojo">
             Box Office Mojo
            </a>
           </i>
           <span class="reference-accessdate">
            . Retrieved
            <span class="nowrap">
             August 30,
            </span>
            2018
           </span>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=Grease+%2840th+Anniversary%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Fmovies%2Fintl%2F%3Fpage%3D%26wk%3D2018W34%26id%3D_fGREASE401&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-kramer-303">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          ^
          <a href="#cite_ref-kramer_303-0">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              a
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
          <a href="#cite_ref-kramer_303-1">
           <sup>
            <i>
             <b>
              b
             </b>
            </i>
           </sup>
          </a>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <i>
           Kramer vs. Kramer
          </i>
          <ul>
           <li>
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation book cs1">
             <i>
              United &amp; Babson Investment Report
             </i>
             .
             <b>
              72
             </b>
             .
             <a href="/wiki/Babson-United,_Inc." title="Babson-United, Inc.">
              Babson-United, Inc.
             </a>
             1980. p. 262.
             <q>
              Columbia Pictures Industries is continuing to rake in the box office dollars from its Oscar-winning
              <i>
               Kramer vs. Kramer
              </i>
              , which has topped $100 million in domestic grosses and $70 million overseas.
              <i>
               Kramer
              </i>
              , which cost less than $8 million to make, is now the second...
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=United+%26+Babson+Investment+Report&amp;rft.pages=262&amp;rft.pub=Babson-United%2C+Inc.&amp;rft.date=1980&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
           <li>
            <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
            <cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFPrince2002">
             Prince, Stephen (2002).
             <i>
              A New Pot of Gold: Hollywood Under the Electronic Rainbow, 1980–1989
             </i>
             .
             <a href="/wiki/University_of_California_Press" title="University of California Press">
              University of California Press
             </a>
             . p.
             <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/newpotofgold00step/page/7" rel="nofollow">
              7
             </a>
             .
             <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">
              ISBN
             </a>
             <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-520-23266-2" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-520-23266-2">
              <bdi>
               978-0-520-23266-2
              </bdi>
             </a>
             .
             <q>
              Much of this was attributable to the performance of its hit film,
              <i>
               Kramer vs. Kramer
              </i>
              ($94 million worldwide and the number two film in the domestic market).
             </q>
            </cite>
            <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=A+New+Pot+of+Gold%3A+Hollywood+Under+the+Electronic+Rainbow%2C+1980%E2%80%931989&amp;rft.pages=7&amp;rft.pub=University+of+California+Press&amp;rft.date=2002&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-520-23266-2&amp;rft.aulast=Prince&amp;rft.aufirst=Stephen&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
            </span>
           </li>
          </ul>
         </span>
        </li>

         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">

        <li id="cite_note-317">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-317">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <a href="#CITEREFFinler2003">
           Finler 2003
          </a>
          , p.
          <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=rvVhEJmbfrsC&amp;pg=PA268" rel="nofollow">
           268
          </a>
          . "The studio had a record operating income of $212 million in 1982, the year of Spielberg's
          <i>
           E.T. The Extra-Terrestrial
          </i>
          (which had cost only slightly over $10 million) and $150 million in 1985, mainly due to another Spielberg production, the $22 million Back to the Future, which became the top box office hit of the year."
         </span>
        </li>

        <li id="cite_note-Ansen_(1991)-327">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-Ansen_(1991)_327-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFAnsen1991">

           <a class="external text" href="http://www.newsweek.com/1991/07/07/conan-the-humanitarian.html" rel="nofollow">

         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>

           </a>
           .

        <li id="cite_note-Thomas_(1963)-381">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">

           <a href="#cite_ref-Thomas_(1963)_381-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFThomas1963">
           <a href="/wiki/Bob_Thomas_(reporter)" title="Bob Thomas (reporter)">
            Thomas, Bob
           </a>
           (August 1, 1963). "Movie Finances Are No Longer Hidden From Scrutiny".
           <i>
            <a href="/wiki/The_Robesonian" title="The Robesonian">
             The Robesonian
            </a>
           </i>
           .
           <a href="/wiki/Associated_Press" title="Associated Press">
            Associated Press
           </a>
           . p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://news.google.com/newspapers?id=QsBVAAAAIBAJ&amp;sjid=iUANAAAAIBAJ&amp;pg=7022,1836881" rel="nofollow">
            10
           </a>
           .
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Robesonian&amp;rft.atitle=Movie+Finances+Are+No+Longer+Hidden+From+Scrutiny&amp;rft.pages=10&amp;rft.date=1963-08-01&amp;rft.aulast=Thomas&amp;rft.aufirst=Bob&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-Atlantic_Monthly_(1973)-382">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-Atlantic_Monthly_(1973)_382-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1">
           <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/The_Atlantic_Monthly" title="The Atlantic Monthly">
            <i>
             The Atlantic Monthly
            </i>
           </a>
           .
           <b>
            231
           </b>
           . 1973. p.
           <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=38wmAQAAIAAJ&amp;q=%22As+of+the+end+of+1971,+GWTW+stood+as+the+all-time+money-drawing+movie,+with+a+take+of+$116+million%22&amp;dq=%22As+of+the+end+of+1971,+GWTW+stood+as+the+all-time+money-drawing+movie,+with+a+take+of+$116+million%22" rel="nofollow">
            2
           </a>
           .
           <q>
            As of the end of 1971, GWTW stood as the all-time money-drawing movie, with a take of $116 million, and, with this year's reissues, it should continue to run ahead of the second place contender and all-time
            <i>
             kaffee-mit-schlag
            </i>
            spectacle.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+Atlantic+Monthly&amp;rft.pages=2&amp;rft.date=1973&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-383">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-383">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation book cs1">
           <a href="/wiki/New_Times_(magazine)" title="New Times (magazine)">
            <i>
             New Times
            </i>
           </a>
           .
           <b>
            2
           </b>
           . 1974.
           <q>
            Coppola is King Midas, the most individually powerful U.S. filmmaker ." His credits include directing the first
            <i>
             Godfather
            </i>
            (worldwide earnings: $142 million, ahead of
            <i>
             Gone with the Wind
            </i>
            ,
            <i>
             The Sound of Music
            </i>
            and
            <i>
             The Exorcist
            </i>
            )...(
            <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=YSBXAAAAMAAJ&amp;q=%24142+million" rel="nofollow">
             Online copy
            </a>
            at
            <a href="/wiki/Google_Books" title="Google Books">
             Google Books
            </a>
            )
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=New+Times&amp;rft.date=1974&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-star-wars_first-run-388">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-star-wars_first-run_388-0">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFHarmetz1980">
           Harmetz, Aljean (May 18, 1980).
           <a class="external text" href="https://www.google.com/search?hl=en&amp;tbm=nws&amp;q=%22star+wars%22++%24410+million" rel="nofollow">
            "The Saga Beyond 'Star Wars
            <span class="cs1-kern-right">
             '
            </span>
            "
           </a>
           .
           <i>
            <a href="/wiki/The_New_York_Times" title="The New York Times">
             The New York Times
            </a>
           </i>
           . Archived from
           <a class="external text" href="https://select.nytimes.com/gst/abstract.html?res=FB0617FE345D17728DDDA10994DD405B8084F1D3" rel="nofollow">
            the original
           </a>
           on January 31, 2012
           <span class="reference-accessdate">
            . Retrieved
            <span class="nowrap">
             January 30,
            </span>
            2012
           </span>
           .
           <q>
            "Star Wars" has brought 20th Century-Fox approximately $250 million in film rentals ... "Star Wars" grossed $410 million, and his share was enough to allow him to finance its sequel, "The Empire Strikes Back," himself.
           </q>
          </cite>
          <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+New+York+Times&amp;rft.atitle=The+Saga+Beyond+%27Star+Wars%27&amp;rft.date=1980-05-18&amp;rft.aulast=Harmetz&amp;rft.aufirst=Aljean&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fselect.nytimes.com%2Fgst%2Fabstract.html%3Fres%3DFB0617FE345D17728DDDA10994DD405B8084F1D3&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
          </span>
         </span>
        </li>
        <li id="cite_note-390">
         <span class="mw-cite-backlink">
          <b>
           <a href="#cite_ref-390">
            ^
           </a>
          </b>
         </span>
         <span class="reference-text">
          <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
          <cite class="citation web cs1">

           </span>

             <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>

             <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=Batman+%281989%29+30th+Anniversary&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Fmovies%2F%3Fpage%3Dintl%26id%3Dbatman30.htm&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
             </span>
            </li>

             <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
             <cite class="citation web cs1">

              .

              <span class="reference-accessdate">
               . Retrieved
               <span class="nowrap">
                May 25,
               </span>

              </span>
              .
              <q>
               UK: $10,057
              </q>
             </cite>
             <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=Batman+Returns+%28Event%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Fmovies%2F%3Fpage%3Dintl%26id%3Dbatmanreturnsevent.htm&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
             </span>
            </li>
            <li>
             Batman Forever:
             <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
             <cite class="citation web cs1">
              <a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/movies/?page=intl&amp;id=batmanforeverfathomevent.htm" rel="nofollow">

              <span class="reference-accessdate">
               . Retrieved
               <span class="nowrap">
                May 25,
               </span>
               2019
              </span>
              .
              <q>
               US: $38,014; UK: $373
              </q>
             </cite>
             <span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=Batman+Forever+%28Fathom+Event%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Fmovies%2F%3Fpage%3Dintl%26id%3Dbatmanforeverfathomevent.htm&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films">
             </span>
            </li>
            <li>
             Batman and Robin:
             <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/>
             <cite class="citation web cs1">

       <li>
        <a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/alltime/world/" rel="nofollow">

        at

        </a>
       </li>
       <li>
        <a class="external text" href="https://www.the-numbers.com/box-office-records/worldwide/all-movies/cumulative/all-time" rel="nofollow">

        </a>
        at
        <a href="/wiki/The_Numbers_(website)" title="The Numbers (website)">
         The Numbers
        </a>
       </li>
       <li>
        <a class="external text" href="http://www.filmsite.org/boxoffice.html" rel="nofollow">
         Box-Office Top 100 Films of All-Time
        </a>
        at
        <a href="/wiki/Filmsite.org" title="Filmsite.org">
         Filmsite.org
        </a>
       </li>
      </ul>

176,\n  [\"Ref label\"] = 2,\n  [\"Refbegin\"] = 7,\n  [\"Refend\"] = 7,\n  [\"Reflist\"] = 2,\n  [\"Refn\"] = 1,\n  [\"See also\"] = 3,\n  [\"Short description\"] = 1,\n  [\"Sic\"] = 1,\n {\n}\n","limitreport-profile":[["recursiveClone \u003Cmw.lua:661\u003E","220","5.5"],["type","160","4.0"],["Scribunto_LuaSandboxCallback::find","120","3.0"],["Scribunto_LuaSandboxCallback::anchorEncode","100","2.5"],["[others]","1160","28.9"]]},"cachereport":{"origin":"mw1319","timestamp":"20210311181017","ttl":3600,"transientcontent":true}}});});

   {"@context":"https:\/\/schema.org","@type":"Article","name":"List of highest-grossing films","url":"https:\/\/en.wikipedia.org\/wiki\/List_of_highest-grossing_films","sameAs":"http:\/\/www.wikidata.org\/entity\/Q392108","mainEntity":"http:\/\/www.wikidata.org\/entity\/Q392108","author":{"@type":"Organization","name":"Contributors to Wikimedia projects"},"publisher":{"@type":"Organization","name":"Wikimedia Foundation, Inc.","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https:\/\/www.wikimedia.org\/static\/images\/wmf-hor-googpub.png"}},"datePublished":"2002-06-28T04:14:04Z","dateModified":"2021-03-11T10:45:49Z","image":"https:\/\/upload.wikimedia.org\/wikipedia\/commons\/2\/27\/Poster_-_Gone_With_the_Wind_01.jpg","headline":"Wikimedia list article"}

 </body>
</html>

What’s in a Soup?#

  • A soup is essentially a collection of tag objects

    • each tag from the html is a tag object in the soup

    • the tag’s maintain the hierarchy of the html page, so tag objects will contain other tag objects that were under it in the html tree.

  • Each tag has a:

    • .name

    • .contents

    • .string

  • A tag can be access by name (like a column in a dataframe using dot notation)

    • and then you can access the tags within the new tag-variable just like the first tag

    # Access tags by name
meta = soup.meta
head = soup.head
body = soup.body
p = soup.p
# and so on...

  • [!] BUT this will only return the FIRST tag of that type, to access all occurances of a tag-type, we will need to navigate the html family tree

# soup.contents

## check .head
print(soup.head)
