(2011).  <bdi lang="ru">Гиннесс. Мировые рекорды</bdi> [<i>Guinness World Records</i>] (in Russian). Translated by Andrianov, P.I.; Palova, I.V. (2012 ed.). Moscow: <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Astrel" title="Astrel">Astrel</a>. p. 211. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-5-271-36423-5" title="Special:BookSources/978-5-271-36423-5"><bdi>978-5-271-36423-5</bdi></a>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=%D0%93%D0%B8%D0%BD%D0%BD%D0%B5%D1%81%D1%81.+%D0%9C%D0%B8%D1%80%D0%BE%D0%B2%D1%8B%D0%B5+%D1%80%D0%B5%D0%BA%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%B4%D1%8B&amp;rft.place=Moscow&amp;rft.pages=211&amp;rft.edition=2012&amp;rft.pub=Astrel&amp;rft.date=2011&amp;rft.isbn=978-5-271-36423-5&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-114"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-114">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/movies/?page=intl&amp;id=titanic3d.htm" rel="nofollow">"Titanic 3D (2012) – International Box Office results"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/Box_Office_Mojo" title="Box Office Mojo">Box Office Mojo</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">November 25,</span> 2012</span>. <q>North America: $57,884,114; Overseas: $285,666,656</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=Titanic+3D+%282012%29+%E2%80%93+International+Box+Office+results&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Fmovies%2F%3Fpage%3Dintl%26id%3Dtitanic3d.htm&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-115"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-115">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/movies/?id=titanic20thanniversary.htm" rel="nofollow">"Titanic (20th Anniversary)"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/Box_Office_Mojo" title="Box Office Mojo">Box Office Mojo</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">July 1,</span> 2019</span>. <q>Domestic Total Gross: $691,642</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=Titanic+%2820th+Anniversary%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Fmovies%2F%3Fid%3Dtitanic20thanniversary.htm&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Cones-116"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-Cones_116-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Cones_116-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFCones1997">Cones, John W. (1997). <i>The feature film distribution deal: a critical analysis of the single most important film industry agreement</i>. <a href="/wiki/Southern_Illinois_University_Press" title="Southern Illinois University Press">Southern Illinois University Press</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=EoeJ7VmwnDIC&amp;pg=PA41" rel="nofollow">41</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-8093-2082-0" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-8093-2082-0"><bdi>978-0-8093-2082-0</bdi></a>. <q>Distributor rentals: It is also important to know and recognize the difference between the distributor's gross receipts and the gross rentals. The term "rentals" refers to the aggregate amount of the film distributor's share of monies paid at theatre box offices computed on the basis of negotiated agreements between the distributor and the exhibitor. Note that gross receipts refers to amounts actually received and from all markets and media, whereas gross rentals refers to amounts earned from theatrical exhibition only, regardless of whether received by the distributor. Thus, gross receipts is the much broader term and includes distributor rentals. The issue of film rentals (i.e., what percentage of a film's box office gross comes back to the distributor) is of key importance...More current numbers suggest that distributor rentals for the major studio/distributor released films average in the neighborhood of 43% of box office gross. Again, however, such an average is based on widely divergent distributor rental ratios on individual films.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+feature+film+distribution+deal%3A+a+critical+analysis+of+the+single+most+important+film+industry+agreement&amp;rft.pages=41&amp;rft.pub=Southern+Illinois+University+Press&amp;rft.date=1997&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-8093-2082-0&amp;rft.aulast=Cones&amp;rft.aufirst=John+W.&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Marich-117"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-Marich_117-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFMarich2009">Marich, Robert (2009) [1st. pub. <a href="/wiki/Focal_Press" title="Focal Press">Focal Press</a>:2005]. <i>Marketing to moviegoers: a handbook of strategies used by major studios and independents</i> (2 ed.). <a href="/wiki/Southern_Illinois_University_Press" title="Southern Illinois University Press">Southern Illinois University Press</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=_6USFaYxyeUC&amp;pg=PA252" rel="nofollow">252</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-8093-2884-0" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-8093-2884-0"><bdi>978-0-8093-2884-0</bdi></a>. <q>Rentals are the distributors' share of the box office gross and typically set by a complex, two-part contract.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Marketing+to+moviegoers%3A+a+handbook+of+strategies+used+by+major+studios+and+independents&amp;rft.pages=252&amp;rft.edition=2&amp;rft.pub=Southern+Illinois+University+Press&amp;rft.date=2009&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-8093-2884-0&amp;rft.aulast=Marich&amp;rft.aufirst=Robert&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Balio-118"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-Balio_118-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFBalio2005">Balio, Tino (2005). <i>The American film industry</i>. <a href="/wiki/University_of_Wisconsin_Press" title="University of Wisconsin Press">University of Wisconsin Press</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=OBpDf_uUp30C&amp;pg=pa296" rel="nofollow">296</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-299-09874-2" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-299-09874-2"><bdi>978-0-299-09874-2</bdi></a>. <q>Film Rentals as Percent of Volume of Business (1939): 36.4</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+American+film+industry&amp;rft.pages=296&amp;rft.pub=University+of+Wisconsin+Press&amp;rft.date=2005&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-299-09874-2&amp;rft.aulast=Balio&amp;rft.aufirst=Tino&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-119"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-119">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFBalio1987">Balio, Tino (1987). <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/unitedartistscom00bali/page/124" rel="nofollow"><i>United Artists: the Company that Changed the Film Industry</i></a>. <a href="/wiki/University_of_Wisconsin_Press" title="University of Wisconsin Press">University of Wisconsin Press</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=9EeK5s3aw44C&amp;pg=PA124#v=twopage" rel="nofollow">124–125</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-299-11440-4" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-299-11440-4"><bdi>978-0-299-11440-4</bdi></a>. <q>To rekindle interest in the movies, Hollywood not only had to compete with television but also with other leisure-time activities...Movies made a comeback by 1955, but audiences had changed. Moviegoing became a special event for most people, creating the phenomenon of the big picture.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=United+Artists%3A+the+Company+that+Changed+the+Film+Industry&amp;rft.pages=124-125&amp;rft.pub=University+of+Wisconsin+Press&amp;rft.date=1987&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-299-11440-4&amp;rft.aulast=Balio&amp;rft.aufirst=Tino&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Farchive.org%2Fdetails%2Funitedartistscom00bali%2Fpage%2F124&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-HallNeale179-120"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale179_120-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">Hall &amp; Neale 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA179" rel="nofollow">179</a>. "Later epics proved far more disastrous for the backers. Samuel Bronston's <i>The Fall of the Roman Empire</i>, filmed in Spain, cost $17,816,876 and grossed only $1.9 million in America. George Stevens's long-gestating life of Christ, <i>The Greatest Story Ever Told</i> (1965), which had been in planning since 1954 and in production since 1962, earned domestic rentals of $6,962,715 on a $21,481,745 negative cost, the largest amount yet spent on a production made entirely within the United States. <i>The Bible—in the Beginning</i>... (1966) was financed by the Italian producer Dino De Laurentiis from private investors and Swiss banks. He then sold distribution rights outside Italy jointly to Fox and Seven Arts for $15 million (70 percent of which came from Fox), thereby recouping the bulk of his $18 million investment. Although <i>The Bible</i> returned a respectable world rental of $25.3 million, Fox was still left with a net loss of just over $1.5 million. It was the last biblical epic to be released by any major Hollywood studio for nearly twenty years."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-121"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-121">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1" id="CITEREFWilliams2015">Williams, Trey (September 25, 2015). <a class="external text" href="http://www.marketwatch.com/story/ridley-scotts-latest-alien-announcement-drives-hollywoods-sequel-problem-2015-09-25" rel="nofollow">"Ridley Scott's latest 'Alien' announcement drives Hollywood's sequel problem"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/MarketWatch" title="MarketWatch">MarketWatch</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">May 12,</span> 2016</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=MarketWatch&amp;rft.atitle=Ridley+Scott%27s+latest+%27Alien%27+announcement+drives+Hollywood%27s+sequel+problem&amp;rft.date=2015-09-25&amp;rft.aulast=Williams&amp;rft.aufirst=Trey&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.marketwatch.com%2Fstory%2Fridley-scotts-latest-alien-announcement-drives-hollywoods-sequel-problem-2015-09-25&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-122"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-122">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/yearly/?view2=worldwide&amp;view=releasedate&amp;p=.htm" rel="nofollow">"Yearly Box Office"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/Box_Office_Mojo" title="Box Office Mojo">Box Office Mojo</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">January 6,</span> 2012</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=Yearly+Box+Office&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Fyearly%2F%3Fview2%3Dworldwide%26view%3Dreleasedate%26p%3D.htm&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-123"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-123">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.the-numbers.com/movies" rel="nofollow">"Movie Index By Year"</a>. <i>The Numbers</i>. Nash Information Services. LLC<span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">January 6,</span> 2012</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Numbers&amp;rft.atitle=Movie+Index+By+Year&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.the-numbers.com%2Fmovies&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Dirks_(year)-124"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-Dirks_(year)_124-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1" id="CITEREFDirks">Dirks, Tim. <a class="external text" href="http://www.filmsite.org/boxoffice2.html" rel="nofollow">"All-Time Box-Office Hits By Decade and Year"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/Filmsite.org" title="Filmsite.org">Filmsite.org</a></i>. <a href="/wiki/AMC_Networks" title="AMC Networks">American Movie Classics</a><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">January 5,</span> 2012</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Filmsite.org&amp;rft.atitle=All-Time+Box-Office+Hits+By+Decade+and+Year&amp;rft.aulast=Dirks&amp;rft.aufirst=Tim&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.filmsite.org%2Fboxoffice2.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-346"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-346">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.the-numbers.com/movies/1916/020LU.php" rel="nofollow">"20,000 Leagues Under the Sea"</a>. <i>The Numbers</i>. Nash Information Services, LLC<span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">January 5,</span> 2012</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Numbers&amp;rft.atitle=20%2C000+Leagues+Under+the+Sea&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.the-numbers.com%2Fmovies%2F1916%2F020LU.php&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Finler358-347"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-Finler358_347-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Finler358_347-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFFinler2003">Finler 2003</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=rvVhEJmbfrsC&amp;pg=PA358" rel="nofollow">358</a></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-348"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-348">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1"><a href="/wiki/Milwaukee_Magazine" title="Milwaukee Magazine"><i>Milwaukee Magazine</i></a>. <b>32</b>. 2007. <q>The year's top–grossing movie, <i>Aloma</i> made $3 million in the first three months and brought Gray back to Milwaukee for its opening at the Wisconsin Theatre.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Milwaukee+Magazine&amp;rft.date=2007&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-349"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-349">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFParkinson2007">Parkinson, David (2007). <i>The Rough Guide to Film Musicals</i>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Dorling_Kindersley" title="Dorling Kindersley">Dorling Kindersley</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=Zff4BhUOOnwC&amp;pg=PA28" rel="nofollow">28</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-84353-650-5" title="Special:BookSources/978-1-84353-650-5"><bdi>978-1-84353-650-5</bdi></a>. <q>But they had previously succeeded in showing how musicals could centre on ordinary people with <i>Sunny Side Up</i> (1929), which had grossed $2 million at the box office and demonstrated a new maturity and ingenuity in the staging of story and dance.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+Rough+Guide+to+Film+Musicals&amp;rft.pages=28&amp;rft.pub=Dorling+Kindersley&amp;rft.date=2007&amp;rft.isbn=978-1-84353-650-5&amp;rft.aulast=Parkinson&amp;rft.aufirst=David&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-HallNeale6-350"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale6_350-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale6_350-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">Hall &amp; Neale 2010</a>, pp. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=Ro0hASPfC68C&amp;pg=PA6" rel="nofollow">6</a>–<a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=Ro0hASPfC68C&amp;pg=PA7" rel="nofollow">7</a>. "For similar reasons of accountability, <i>Variety</i> has typically used figures for domestic (U.S. and Canadian) rather than worldwide revenue. This became its standard policy in 1940, when the advent of war in Europe persuaded the American film industry (temporarily, as it turned out) that it should be wholly reliant on the home market for profitability. Where specific rentals data are reported in <i>Variety</i> before this (which tended to be only sporadically) they were often for worldwide rather domestic performance. This was also the case with other trade sources, such as <i>Quigley</i><span class="nowrap" style="padding-left:0.1em;">'</span>s annual <i>Motion Picture Almanac</i>, which published its own all-time hits lists from the early 1930s onward. The subsequent confusion of domestic and worldwide figures, and of rental and box-office figures, has plagued many published accounts of Hollywood history (sometimes including those in <i>Variety</i> itself), and we have attempted to be diligant in clarifying the differences between them."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-351"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-351">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFShearer1947">Shearer, Lloyd (October 26, 1947). <a class="external text" href="https://www.nytimes.com/packages/html/movies/bestpictures/wind-ar6.html" rel="nofollow">"GWTW: Supercolossal Saga of an Epic"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/The_New_York_Times" title="The New York Times">The New York Times</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">July 14,</span> 2012</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+New+York+Times&amp;rft.atitle=GWTW%3A+Supercolossal+Saga+of+an+Epic&amp;rft.date=1947-10-26&amp;rft.aulast=Shearer&amp;rft.aufirst=Lloyd&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.nytimes.com%2Fpackages%2Fhtml%2Fmovies%2Fbestpictures%2Fwind-ar6.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-352"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-352">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://www.time.com/time/magazine/article/0,9171,817905,00.html" rel="nofollow">"Cinema: The Big Grossers"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/Time_(magazine)" title="Time (magazine)">Time</a></i>. February 2, 1953<span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">September 15,</span> 2012</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Time&amp;rft.atitle=Cinema%3A+The+Big+Grossers&amp;rft.date=1953-02-02&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.time.com%2Ftime%2Fmagazine%2Farticle%2F0%2C9171%2C817905%2C00.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-BlockWilson129-353"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson129_353-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA129&amp;q=%22Gone%20with%20the%20Wind%20includes%20initial%20release%20plus%20four%20rereleases%20(1941%2C1942%2C1947%20and%201954)%20since%20foreign%20rental%20revenues%20were%20available%20only%20cumulative%20through%201956.%22" rel="nofollow">129</a>. "Domestic Rentals: $30,015,000 (61%); Foreign Rentals: $18,964,000 (39%)...Gone with the Wind includes initial release plus four rereleases (1941,1942,1947 and 1954) since foreign rental revenues were available only cumulative through 1956."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-354"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-354">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFMcDermott2010">McDermott, Christine (2010), <i>Life with Father</i>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA307" rel="nofollow">307</a>, <q>No matter what the billing, the movie became a worldwide hit with $6.5 million in worldwide rentals, from Pappa och vi in Sweden to Vita col padre in Italy, although it booked a net loss of $350,000.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Life+with+Father&amp;rft.pages=307&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=McDermott&amp;rft.aufirst=Christine&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span> In: <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>.</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-355"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-355">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFMulligan1956">Mulligan, Hugh A. (September 23, 1956). "Cinerama Pushing Ahead As Biggest Money-Maker". <i><a href="/wiki/The_Register-Guard" title="The Register-Guard">The Register-Guard</a></i>. Eugene, Oregon. p. <a class="external text" href="https://news.google.com/newspapers?id=GPRVAAAAIBAJ&amp;sjid=YeIDAAAAIBAJ&amp;pg=6899,3861317" rel="nofollow">7B</a>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Register-Guard&amp;rft.atitle=Cinerama+Pushing+Ahead+As+Biggest+Money-Maker&amp;rft.pages=7B&amp;rft.date=1956-09-23&amp;rft.aulast=Mulligan&amp;rft.aufirst=Hugh+A.&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-356"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-356">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">Hall &amp; Neale 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA145" rel="nofollow">145</a>. "The commercial success of the five Cinerama travelogues, which earned an aggregate worldwide box-office gross of $120 million by 1962 (including $82 million in the United States and Canada), nevertheless demonstrated to the mainstream industry the market value of special screen formats."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Dirks_(2010)-357"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-Dirks_(2010)_357-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Dirks_(2010)_357-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Dirks_(2010)_357-2"><sup><i><b>c</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Dirks_(2010)_357-3"><sup><i><b>d</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Dirks_(2010)_357-4"><sup><i><b>e</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Dirks_(2010)_357-5"><sup><i><b>f</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1" id="CITEREFDirks">Dirks, Tim. <a class="external text" href="http://www.filmsite.org/greatfilmssummary.html" rel="nofollow">"Top Films of All-Time: Part 1 – Box-Office Blockbusters"</a>. <a href="/wiki/Filmsite.org" title="Filmsite.org">Filmsite.org</a><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">August 11,</span> 2010</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=Top+Films+of+All-Time%3A+Part+1+%E2%80%93+Box-Office+Blockbusters&amp;rft.pub=Filmsite.org&amp;rft.aulast=Dirks&amp;rft.aufirst=Tim&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.filmsite.org%2Fgreatfilmssummary.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Wasko-358"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-Wasko_358-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Wasko_358-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFWasko1986">Wasko, Janet (1986). "D.W. Griffiths and the banks: a case study in film financing".  In Kerr, Paul (ed.). <i>The Hollywood Film Industry: A Reader</i>. <a href="/wiki/Routledge" title="Routledge">Routledge</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=jMINAAAAQAAJ&amp;pg=PA34" rel="nofollow">34</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-7100-9730-9" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-7100-9730-9"><bdi>978-0-7100-9730-9</bdi></a>. <q>Various accounts have cited $15 to $18 million profits during the first few years of release, while in a letter to a potential investor in the proposed sound version, Aitken noted that a $15 to $18 million box-office gross was a 'conservative estimate'. For years <i>Variety</i> has listed <i>The Birth of a Nation'</i>s total rental at $50 million. (This reflects the total amount paid to the distributor, not box-office gross.) This 'trade legend' has finally been acknowledged by <i>Variety</i> as a 'whopper myth', and the amount has been revised to $5 million. That figure seems far more feasible, as reports of earnings in the Griffith collection list gross receipts for 1915–1919 at slightly more than $5.2 million (including foreign distribution) and total earnings after deducting general office expenses, but not royalties, at about $2 million.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=bookitem&amp;rft.atitle=D.W.+Griffiths+and+the+banks%3A+a+case+study+in+film+financing&amp;rft.btitle=The+Hollywood+Film+Industry%3A+A+Reader&amp;rft.pages=34&amp;rft.pub=Routledge&amp;rft.date=1986&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-7100-9730-9&amp;rft.aulast=Wasko&amp;rft.aufirst=Janet&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Variety_(1932)-359"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-Variety_(1932)_359-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1">"Biggest Money Pictures". <i><a href="/wiki/Variety_(magazine)" title="Variety (magazine)">Variety</a></i>. June 21, 1932. p. 1.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Variety&amp;rft.atitle=Biggest+Money+Pictures&amp;rft.pages=1&amp;rft.date=1932-06-21&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span> Cited in <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://web.archive.org/web/20111105043830/http://www.cinemaweb.com/silentfilm/bookshelf/7_v_32_4.htm" rel="nofollow">"Biggest Money Pictures"</a>. Cinemaweb. Archived from <a class="external text" href="http://www.cinemaweb.com/silentfilm/bookshelf/7_v_32_4.htm" rel="nofollow">the original</a> on November 5, 2011<span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">June 25,</span> 2015</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=Biggest+Money+Pictures&amp;rft.pub=Cinemaweb&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.cinemaweb.com%2Fsilentfilm%2Fbookshelf%2F7_v_32_4.htm&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-360"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-360">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1">"<span class="cs1-kern-left">'</span>Peter Pan' flies again". <i><a href="/wiki/Daily_Record_(Washington)" title="Daily Record (Washington)">Daily Record</a></i>. Ellensburg, Washington. <a href="/wiki/United_Press_International" title="United Press International">United Press International</a>. July 21, 1989. p. <a class="external text" href="https://news.google.com/newspapers?id=r3dUAAAAIBAJ&amp;sjid=WY8DAAAAIBAJ&amp;pg=2540,2188129" rel="nofollow">16</a>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Daily+Record&amp;rft.atitle=%27Peter+Pan%27+flies+again&amp;rft.pages=16&amp;rft.date=1989-07-21&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-361"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-361">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA237" rel="nofollow">237</a>. "By the end of 1938, it had grossed more than $8 million in worldwide rentals and was ranked at the time as the second-highest-grossing film after the 1925 epic <i>Ben-Hur</i>".</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-362"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-362">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFFinler2003">Finler 2003</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=rvVhEJmbfrsC&amp;pg=PA47" rel="nofollow">47</a>. "Walt Disney took a big risk when he decided to invest $1.5 million in his first feature-length animated film, <i>Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs</i>. It became the biggest hit of the sound era and the largest-grossing movie since <i>The Birth of a Nation</i> – until the release of independent producer David O. Selznick's <i>Gone with the Wind</i> just two years later."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-363"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-363">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFBarrios1995">Barrios, Richard (1995). <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/songindarkbirtho00barr/page/49" rel="nofollow"><i>A Song in the Dark: The Birth of the Musical Film</i></a>. <a href="/wiki/Oxford_University_Press" title="Oxford University Press">Oxford University Press</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/songindarkbirtho00barr/page/49" rel="nofollow">49</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-19-508811-3" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-19-508811-3"><bdi>978-0-19-508811-3</bdi></a>. <q>Since it's rarely seen today, <i>The Singing Fool</i> is frequently confused with <i>The Jazz Singer</i>; although besides Jolson and a pervasively maudlin air the two have little in common. In the earlier film Jolson was inordinately attached to his mother and sang "Mammy"; here the fixation was on his young son, and "Sonny Boy" became an enormous hit. So did the film, which amassed a stunning world-wide gross of $5.9 million...Some sources give it as the highest gross of <i>any</i> film in its initial release prior to <i>Gone with the Wind</i>. This is probably overstating it—MGM's records show that <i>Ben-Hur</i> and <i>The Big Parade</i> grossed more, and no one knows just how much <i>The Birth of a Nation</i> brought in. Still, by the standards of the time it's an amazing amount.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=A+Song+in+the+Dark%3A+The+Birth+of+the+Musical+Film&amp;rft.pages=49&amp;rft.pub=Oxford+University+Press&amp;rft.date=1995&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-19-508811-3&amp;rft.aulast=Barrios&amp;rft.aufirst=Richard&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Farchive.org%2Fdetails%2Fsongindarkbirtho00barr%2Fpage%2F49&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-364"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-364">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFEverson1998">Everson, William K. (1998) [First published 1978]. <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/americansilentfi00ever_0/page/374" rel="nofollow"><i>American silent film</i></a>. <a href="/wiki/Da_Capo_Press" title="Da Capo Press">Da Capo Press</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=pyQaKxBjjzMC&amp;pg=PA374" rel="nofollow">374</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-306-80876-0" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-306-80876-0"><bdi>978-0-306-80876-0</bdi></a>. <q>Putting <i>The Birth of a Nation</i> in fifth place is open to question, since it is generally conceded to be the top-grossing film of all time. However, it has always been difficult to obtain reliable box-office figures for this film, and it may have been even more difficult in the mid-1930s. After listing it until the mid-1970s as <i>the</i> top-grosser, though finding it impossible to quote exact figures, <i>Variety</i>, the trade journal, suddenly repudiated the claim but without giving specific details or reasons. On the basis of the number of paid admissions, and <i>continuous</i> exhibition, its number one position seems justified.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=American+silent+film&amp;rft.pages=374&amp;rft.pub=Da+Capo+Press&amp;rft.date=1998&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-306-80876-0&amp;rft.aulast=Everson&amp;rft.aufirst=William+K.&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Farchive.org%2Fdetails%2Famericansilentfi00ever_0%2Fpage%2F374&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-HallNeale163-365"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale163_365-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">Hall &amp; Neale 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA163" rel="nofollow">163</a>. "MGM's silent <i>Ben-Hur</i>, which opened at the end of 1925, had out-grossed all the other pictures released by the company in 1926 combined. With worldwide rentals of $9,386,000 on first release it was, with the sole possible exception of <i>The Birth of a Nation</i>, the highest-earning film of the entire silent era."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-du_Brow-366"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-du_Brow_366-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-du_Brow_366-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFdu_Brow1965">du Brow, Rick (September 22, 1965). "Documentary On The Klan Made Quite An Impact On Du Brow". <i><a href="/wiki/The_Columbus_Dispatch" title="The Columbus Dispatch">The Columbus Dispatch</a></i>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://news.google.com/newspapers?id=i9sbAAAAIBAJ&amp;sjid=ClEEAAAAIBAJ&amp;pg=7022,1479191" rel="nofollow">12</a>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Columbus+Dispatch&amp;rft.atitle=Documentary+On+The+Klan+Made+Quite+An+Impact+On+Du+Brow&amp;rft.pages=12&amp;rft.date=1965-09-22&amp;rft.aulast=du+Brow&amp;rft.aufirst=Rick&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-367"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-367">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFHodgkinson2004">Hodgkinson, Will (April 12, 2004). <a class="external text" href="https://www.telegraph.co.uk/culture/donotmigrate/3615281/Culture-quake-The-Birth-of-a-Nation.html" rel="nofollow">"Culture quake: The Birth of a Nation"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/The_Daily_Telegraph" title="The Daily Telegraph">The Daily Telegraph</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">January 31,</span> 2012</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Daily+Telegraph&amp;rft.atitle=Culture+quake%3A+The+Birth+of+a+Nation&amp;rft.date=2004-04-12&amp;rft.aulast=Hodgkinson&amp;rft.aufirst=Will&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.telegraph.co.uk%2Fculture%2Fdonotmigrate%2F3615281%2FCulture-quake-The-Birth-of-a-Nation.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-368"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-368">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFThomas1963"><a href="/wiki/Bob_Thomas_(reporter)" title="Bob Thomas (reporter)">Thomas, Bob</a> (January 18, 1963). "<span class="cs1-kern-left">'</span>West Side Story' Earned $19 Million Last Year". <i><a href="/wiki/Reading_Eagle" title="Reading Eagle">Reading Eagle</a></i>. <a href="/wiki/Associated_Press" title="Associated Press">Associated Press</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://news.google.com/newspapers?id=YxMrAAAAIBAJ&amp;sjid=gpwFAAAAIBAJ&amp;pg=3048,210679" rel="nofollow">20</a>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Reading+Eagle&amp;rft.atitle=%27West+Side+Story%27+Earned+%2419+Million+Last+Year&amp;rft.pages=20&amp;rft.date=1963-01-18&amp;rft.aulast=Thomas&amp;rft.aufirst=Bob&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-369"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-369">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation journal cs1" id="CITEREFKlopschSandisonTalmage1965">Klopsch, Louis; Sandison, George Henry; Talmage, Thomas De Witt (1965). "Christian Herald". <b>88</b>: <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=Ou3eAAAAMAAJ&amp;dq=%22ten+commandments%22+rentals&amp;q=%22ten+commandments%22+rentals" rel="nofollow">68</a>. <q>Yet "The Ten Commandments" has earned 58 million dollars in film rentals and is expected to bring in 10 to 15 million each year it is reissued.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.atitle=Christian+Herald&amp;rft.volume=88&amp;rft.pages=68&amp;rft.date=1965&amp;rft.aulast=Klopsch&amp;rft.aufirst=Louis&amp;rft.au=Sandison%2C+George+Henry&amp;rft.au=Talmage%2C+Thomas+De+Witt&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span> <span class="cs1-hidden-error error citation-comment">Cite journal requires <code class="cs1-code">|journal=</code> (<a href="/wiki/Help:CS1_errors#missing_periodical" title="Help:CS1 errors">help</a>)</span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-HallNeale160-370"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale160_370-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">Hall &amp; Neale 2010</a>, pp. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA160#v=twopage" rel="nofollow">160–161</a>. "General release began at normal prices in 1959 and continued until the end of the following year, when the film was temporarily withdrawn (the first of several reissues came in 1966). The worldwide rental by this time was around $60 million. In the domestic market it dislodged <i>Gone with the Wind</i> from the number one position on <i>Variety</i><span class="nowrap" style="padding-left:0.1em;">'</span>s list of All-Time Rentals Champs. <i>GWTW</i> had hitherto maintained its lead through several reissues (and was soon to regain it through another in 1961)."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Oviatt_(1961)-371"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-Oviatt_(1961)_371-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFOviatt1961">Oviatt, Ray (April 16, 1961). "The Memory Isn't Gone With The Wind". <i><a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Toledo_Blade" title="Toledo Blade">Toledo Blade</a></i>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://news.google.com/newspapers?id=V75OAAAAIBAJ&amp;sjid=EAEEAAAAIBAJ&amp;pg=4564,6940480" rel="nofollow">67</a>–68.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Toledo+Blade&amp;rft.atitle=The+Memory+Isn%27t+Gone+With+The+Wind&amp;rft.pages=67-68&amp;rft.date=1961-04-16&amp;rft.aulast=Oviatt&amp;rft.aufirst=Ray&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-372"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-372">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/415095/Ben-Hur/notes.html" rel="nofollow">"Ben-Hur (1959) – Notes"</a>. <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">Turner Classic Movies</a><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">November 17,</span> 2012</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=Ben-Hur+%281959%29+%E2%80%93+Notes&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F415095%2FBen-Hur%2Fnotes.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Thomas_(1963)-373"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-Thomas_(1963)_373-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFThomas1963"><a href="/wiki/Bob_Thomas_(reporter)" title="Bob Thomas (reporter)">Thomas, Bob</a> (August 1, 1963). "Movie Finances Are No Longer Hidden From Scrutiny". <i><a href="/wiki/The_Robesonian" title="The Robesonian">The Robesonian</a></i>. <a href="/wiki/Associated_Press" title="Associated Press">Associated Press</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://news.google.co.uk/newspapers?id=QsBVAAAAIBAJ&amp;sjid=iUANAAAAIBAJ&amp;pg=7022,1836881" rel="nofollow">10</a>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Robesonian&amp;rft.atitle=Movie+Finances+Are+No+Longer+Hidden+From+Scrutiny&amp;rft.pages=10&amp;rft.date=1963-08-01&amp;rft.aulast=Thomas&amp;rft.aufirst=Bob&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-374"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-374">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA324" rel="nofollow">324</a>. "Worldwide rentals: $66.1 million (initial release)"</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-375"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-375">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFWashington_(AP)1984">Washington (AP) (September 13, 1984). "<span class="cs1-kern-left">'</span>Deep Throat' star against pornography". <i><a href="/wiki/The_Free_Lance%E2%80%93Star" title="The Free Lance–Star">The Free Lance–Star</a></i>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://news.google.com/newspapers?id=deJLAAAAIBAJ&amp;sjid=x4sDAAAAIBAJ&amp;pg=5792,1886785" rel="nofollow">12</a>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Free+Lance%E2%80%93Star&amp;rft.atitle=%27Deep+Throat%27+star+against+pornography&amp;rft.pages=12&amp;rft.date=1984-09-13&amp;rft.au=Washington+%28AP%29&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-376"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-376">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFHiltzik2005">Hiltzik, Michael (February 24, 2005). <a class="external text" href="https://articles.latimes.com/2005/feb/24/business/fi-golden24" rel="nofollow">"<span class="cs1-kern-left">'</span>Deep Throat' Numbers Just Don't Add Up"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/Los_Angeles_Times" title="Los Angeles Times">Los Angeles Times</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">February 2,</span> 2012</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Los+Angeles+Times&amp;rft.atitle=%27Deep+Throat%27+Numbers+Just+Don%27t+Add+Up&amp;rft.date=2005-02-24&amp;rft.aulast=Hiltzik&amp;rft.aufirst=Michael&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Farticles.latimes.com%2F2005%2Ffeb%2F24%2Fbusiness%2Ffi-golden24&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-377"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-377">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFMemphis_(UPI)1976">Memphis (UPI) (May 1, 1976). "<span class="cs1-kern-left">'</span>Deep Throat' Defendant Found Guilty of Conspiring". <i><a href="/wiki/The_Palm_Beach_Post" title="The Palm Beach Post">The Palm Beach Post</a></i>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://news.google.com/newspapers?id=5lc0AAAAIBAJ&amp;sjid=YM0FAAAAIBAJ&amp;pg=1025,8602" rel="nofollow">A2</a>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Palm+Beach+Post&amp;rft.atitle=%27Deep+Throat%27+Defendant+Found+Guilty+of+Conspiring&amp;rft.pages=A2&amp;rft.date=1976-05-01&amp;rft.au=Memphis+%28UPI%29&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-378"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-378">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1" id="CITEREFEbert2005"><a href="/wiki/Roger_Ebert" title="Roger Ebert">Ebert, Roger</a> (February 11, 2005). <a class="external text" href="http://rogerebert.suntimes.com/apps/pbcs.dll/article?AID=/20050210/REVIEWS/50128001" rel="nofollow">"Inside Deep Throat"</a>. <i><a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Rogerebert.com" title="Rogerebert.com">rogerebert.com</a></i>. <a href="/wiki/Chicago_Sun-Times" title="Chicago Sun-Times">Chicago Sun-Times</a>. <a class="external text" href="https://web.archive.org/web/20110606050046/http://rogerebert.suntimes.com/apps/pbcs.dll/article?AID=%2F20050210%2FREVIEWS%2F50128001" rel="nofollow">Archived</a> from the original on June 6, 2011<span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">February 2,</span> 2012</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=rogerebert.com&amp;rft.atitle=Inside+Deep+Throat&amp;rft.date=2005-02-11&amp;rft.aulast=Ebert&amp;rft.aufirst=Roger&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Frogerebert.suntimes.com%2Fapps%2Fpbcs.dll%2Farticle%3FAID%3D%2F20050210%2FREVIEWS%2F50128001&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Bartel-379"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-Bartel_379-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFBartel1989">Bartel, Pauline (1989). <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/completegonewith00bart/page/127" rel="nofollow"><i>The Complete Gone with the Wind Trivia Book: The Movie and More</i></a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Taylor_Trade_Publishing" title="Taylor Trade Publishing">Taylor Trade Publishing</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=ZJLb5GC35E8C&amp;pg=PA127" rel="nofollow">127</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-87833-619-7" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-87833-619-7"><bdi>978-0-87833-619-7</bdi></a>. <q>At the end of the 1941 general release, MGM decided to withdraw GWTW again. The prints were battered, but the studio believed one final fling for GWTW was possible. The film returned to movie theaters for the third time in the spring of 1942 and stayed in release until late 1943 ... When MGM finally pulled the film from exhibition, all worn-out prints were destroyed, and GWTW was at last declared out of circulation. MGM, which by then had sole ownership of the film, announced that GWTW had grossed over $32 million.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+Complete+Gone+with+the+Wind+Trivia+Book%3A+The+Movie+and+More&amp;rft.pages=127&amp;rft.pub=Taylor+Trade+Publishing&amp;rft.date=1989&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-87833-619-7&amp;rft.aulast=Bartel&amp;rft.aufirst=Pauline&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Farchive.org%2Fdetails%2Fcompletegonewith00bart%2Fpage%2F127&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-384"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-384">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFDick1997">Dick, Bernard F. (1997). <i>City of Dreams: The Making and Remaking of Universal Pictures</i>. <a href="/wiki/University_Press_of_Kentucky" title="University Press of Kentucky">University Press of Kentucky</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=e5gzKoozo6sC&amp;pg=PA168" rel="nofollow">168</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-8131-2016-4" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-8131-2016-4"><bdi>978-0-8131-2016-4</bdi></a>. <q><i>Jaws</i> (1975) saved the day, grossing $104 million domestically and $132 million worldwide by January 1976.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=City+of+Dreams%3A+The+Making+and+Remaking+of+Universal+Pictures&amp;rft.pages=168&amp;rft.pub=University+Press+of+Kentucky&amp;rft.date=1997&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-8131-2016-4&amp;rft.aulast=Dick&amp;rft.aufirst=Bernard+F.&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-jaws-385"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-jaws_385-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFKilday1977">Kilday, Gregg (July 5, 1977). "Director of 'Jaws II' Abandons His 'Ship<span class="cs1-kern-right">'</span>". <i><a href="/wiki/The_Victoria_Advocate" title="The Victoria Advocate">The Victoria Advocate</a></i>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://news.google.co.uk/newspapers?id=xy4PAAAAIBAJ&amp;sjid=WYUDAAAAIBAJ&amp;pg=4243,812320" rel="nofollow">6B</a>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Victoria+Advocate&amp;rft.atitle=Director+of+%27Jaws+II%27+Abandons+His+%27Ship%27&amp;rft.pages=6B&amp;rft.date=1977-07-05&amp;rft.aulast=Kilday&amp;rft.aufirst=Gregg&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-386"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-386">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFNew_York_(AP)1978">New York (AP) (May 26, 1978). "Scariness of Jaws 2 unknown quantity". <i><a href="/wiki/The_StarPhoenix" title="The StarPhoenix">The StarPhoenix</a></i>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://news.google.com/newspapers?id=TpZjAAAAIBAJ&amp;sjid=dnoNAAAAIBAJ&amp;pg=5679,3145694" rel="nofollow">21</a>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+StarPhoenix&amp;rft.atitle=Scariness+of+Jaws+2+unknown+quantity&amp;rft.pages=21&amp;rft.date=1978-05-26&amp;rft.au=New+York+%28AP%29&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-387"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-387">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFFenner1978">Fenner, Pat C. (January 16, 1978). "Independent Action". <i><a href="/wiki/Evening_Independent" title="Evening Independent">Evening Independent</a></i>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://news.google.com/newspapers?id=JgAMAAAAIBAJ&amp;sjid=mVgDAAAAIBAJ&amp;pg=6721,49636" rel="nofollow">11-A</a>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Evening+Independent&amp;rft.atitle=Independent+Action&amp;rft.pages=11-A&amp;rft.date=1978-01-16&amp;rft.aulast=Fenner&amp;rft.aufirst=Pat+C.&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Cook_(2002)-389"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-Cook_(2002)_389-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFCook2002">Cook, David A. (2002). <i>Lost Illusions: American Cinema in the Shadow of Watergate and Vietnam, 1970–1979</i>. Volume 9 of History of the American Cinema, Charles Harpole. <a href="/wiki/University_of_California_Press" title="University of California Press">University of California Press</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=HVygqYMVP2wC&amp;pg=PA50" rel="nofollow">50</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-520-23265-5" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-520-23265-5"><bdi>978-0-520-23265-5</bdi></a>. <q>The industry was stunned when <i>Star Wars</i> earned nearly $3 million in its first week and by the end of August had grossed $100 million; it played continuously throughout 1977–1978, and was officially re-released in 1978 and 1979, by the end of which it had earned $262 million in rentals worldwide to become the top- grossing film of all time – a position it would maintain until surpassed by Universal's <i>E.T.: The Extra-Terrestrial</i> in January 1983.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Lost+Illusions%3A+American+Cinema+in+the+Shadow+of+Watergate+and+Vietnam%2C+1970%E2%80%931979&amp;rft.pages=50&amp;rft.pub=University+of+California+Press&amp;rft.date=2002&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-520-23265-5&amp;rft.aulast=Cook&amp;rft.aufirst=David+A.&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-BBC_(1998)-391"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-BBC_(1998)_391-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://news.bbc.co.uk/1/hi/world/59913.stm" rel="nofollow">"<i>Titanic</i> sinks competitors without a trace"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/BBC_News" title="BBC News">BBC News</a></i>. BBC. February 25, 1998<span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">August 13,</span> 2010</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=BBC+News&amp;rft.atitle=Titanic+sinks+competitors+without+a+trace&amp;rft.date=1998-02-25&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fnews.bbc.co.uk%2F1%2Fhi%2Fworld%2F59913.stm&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Cieply_(2010)-392"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-Cieply_(2010)_392-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFCieply2010">Cieply, Michael (January 26, 2010). <a class="external text" href="https://www.nytimes.com/2010/01/27/movies/awardsseason/27record.html" rel="nofollow">"He Doth Surpass Himself: 'Avatar' Outperforms 'Titanic<span class="cs1-kern-right">'</span>"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/The_New_York_Times" title="The New York Times">The New York Times</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">January 27,</span> 2010</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+New+York+Times&amp;rft.atitle=He+Doth+Surpass+Himself%3A+%27Avatar%27+Outperforms+%27Titanic%27&amp;rft.date=2010-01-26&amp;rft.aulast=Cieply&amp;rft.aufirst=Michael&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.nytimes.com%2F2010%2F01%2F27%2Fmovies%2Fawardsseason%2F27record.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Segers_(2010)-393"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-Segers_(2010)_393-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFSegers2010">Segers, Frank (January 25, 2010). <a class="external text" href="https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/avatar-breaks-titanic-worldwide-record-19914" rel="nofollow">"<span class="cs1-kern-left">'</span>Avatar' breaks 'Titanic' worldwide record"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/The_Hollywood_Reporter" title="The Hollywood Reporter">The Hollywood Reporter</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">December 4,</span> 2010</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Hollywood+Reporter&amp;rft.atitle=%27Avatar%27+breaks+%27Titanic%27+worldwide+record&amp;rft.date=2010-01-25&amp;rft.aulast=Segers&amp;rft.aufirst=Frank&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.hollywoodreporter.com%2Fnews%2Favatar-breaks-titanic-worldwide-record-19914&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-394"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-394">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1" id="CITEREFWhitten2019">Whitten, Sarah (July 21, 2019). <a class="external text" href="https://www.cnbc.com/2019/07/21/avengers-endgame-is-the-highest-grossing-film-of-all-time.html" rel="nofollow">"<span class="cs1-kern-left">'</span>Avengers: Endgame' is now the highest-grossing film of all time, dethroning 'Avatar<span class="cs1-kern-right">'</span>"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/CNBC" title="CNBC">CNBC</a></i>. <a class="external text" href="https://web.archive.org/web/20190721154219/https://www.cnbc.com/2019/07/21/avengers-endgame-is-the-highest-grossing-film-of-all-time.html" rel="nofollow">Archived</a> from the original on July 21, 2019<span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">July 21,</span> 2019</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=CNBC&amp;rft.atitle=%27Avengers%3A+Endgame%27+is+now+the+highest-grossing+film+of+all+time%2C+dethroning+%27Avatar%27&amp;rft.date=2019-07-21&amp;rft.aulast=Whitten&amp;rft.aufirst=Sarah&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.cnbc.com%2F2019%2F07%2F21%2Favengers-endgame-is-the-highest-grossing-film-of-all-time.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-395"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-395">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1" id="CITEREFRobbins2019">Robbins, Shawn (July 20, 2019). <a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficepro.com/breaking-avengers-endgame-surpasses-avatar-as-1-global-release-of-all-time/" rel="nofollow">"Avengers: Endgame Surpasses Avatar As #1 Global Release of All Time on Saturday"</a>. <i><a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/BoxOffice_(magazine)" title="BoxOffice (magazine)">BoxOffice</a></i>. <a class="external text" href="https://web.archive.org/web/20190724232409/https://www.boxofficepro.com/breaking-avengers-endgame-surpasses-avatar-as-1-global-release-of-all-time/" rel="nofollow">Archived</a> from the original on July 24, 2019<span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">July 24,</span> 2019</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=BoxOffice&amp;rft.atitle=Avengers%3A+Endgame+Surpasses+Avatar+As+%231+Global+Release+of+All+Time+on+Saturday&amp;rft.date=2019-07-20&amp;rft.aulast=Robbins&amp;rft.aufirst=Shawn&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficepro.com%2Fbreaking-avengers-endgame-surpasses-avatar-as-1-global-release-of-all-time%2F&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-396"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-396">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.the-numbers.com/movies/series/JamesBond.php" rel="nofollow">"Box Office History for James Bond Movies"</a>. <i>The Numbers</i>. Nash Information Services, LLC<span class="reference-accessdate">. November 6, 2015<span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">January 7,</span> 2018</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+New+York+Times&amp;rft.atitle=How+%27Spectre%27+May+Stack+Up+With+%27Thunderball%27+as+a+James+Bond+Blockbuster&amp;rft.date=2015-11-06&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.nytimes.com%2Finteractive%2F2015%2F11%2F05%2Fbusiness%2Fmedia%2Fjames-bond-films-most-popular-box-office.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-410"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-410">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text">Prior to the release of <i>Spectre</i> in 2015, the James Bond series had grossed approximately $17.7 billion at 2015 prices;<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-409"><a href="#cite_note-409">[103]</a></sup> after factoring in earnings of almost $900 million from <i>Spectre</i>, the series has earned at least $18.6 billion adjusted for inflation.</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Harrod-411"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-Harrod_411-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFHarrod2011">Harrod, Horatia (May 17, 2011). <a class="external text" href="https://www.telegraph.co.uk/culture/8509310/Pixars-6-billion-playthings.html" rel="nofollow">"Pixar's $6 billion playthings"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/The_Daily_Telegraph" title="The Daily Telegraph">The Daily Telegraph</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">April 12,</span> 2012</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Daily+Telegraph&amp;rft.atitle=Pixar%27s+%246+billion+playthings&amp;rft.date=2011-05-17&amp;rft.aulast=Harrod&amp;rft.aufirst=Horatia&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.telegraph.co.uk%2Fculture%2F8509310%2FPixars-6-billion-playthings.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-412"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-412">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1" id="CITEREFSwatman2015">Swatman, Rachel (June 16, 2015). <a class="external text" href="http://www.guinnessworldrecords.com/news/2015/6/star-wars-the-force-awakens-preview-becomes-most-viewed-movie-trailer-on-youtube-385758" rel="nofollow">"Star Wars: The Force Awakens second trailer sets YouTube world record"</a>. <a href="/wiki/Guinness_World_Records" title="Guinness World Records">Guinness World Records</a><span class="reference-accessdate">. </li>
<li id="cite_note-413"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-413">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://web.archive.org/web/20150215024949/https://www.cia.gov/library/publications/the-world-factbook//fields/2076.html" rel="nofollow">"Field Listings – Exchange Rates"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/The_World_Factbook" title="The World Factbook">The World Factbook</a></i>. <a href="/wiki/Central_Intelligence_Agency" title="Central Intelligence Agency">Central Intelligence Agency</a>. <li id="cite_note-endgame-14"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-endgame_14-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-endgame_14-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-endgame_14-2"><sup><i><b>c</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/title/tt4154796/" rel="nofollow">"Avengers: Endgame (2019)"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/Box_Office_Mojo" title="Box Office Mojo">Box Office Mojo</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. <dd>Total as of November 26, 2017: £42,840,559 ($1 = £0.7497)</dd>
<dd>Total as of December 3, 2017: £42,976,318 ($1 = £0.742)</dd></dl></dd></dl>
<ul><li><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/movies/?page=intl&amp;country=DE&amp;id=frozen2013.htm" rel="nofollow">"Frozen (2013) – International Box Office Results: Germany"</a>. <i>Box Office Mojo</i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">March 30,</span> 2016</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=Frozen+%282013%29+%E2%80%93+International+Box+Office+Results%3A+Germany&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Fmovies%2F%3Fpage%3Dintl%26country%3DDE%26id%3Dfrozen2013.htm&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li></ul>
<dl><dd><dl><dd>Total as of March 30, 2014: €35,098,170</dd>
<dd>Total as of October 18, 2015: €42,526,744
<dl><dd>nb. the exact euro to dollar conversion rate is unknown for earnings since April 2014, but the euro never fell below parity with the dollar during 2014 and 2015 (as can be verified by comparing the exchange rate on the individual date entries at the provided reference) so an approximate conversion rate of €1: $1 is used here to give a lower-bound.</dd></dl></dd></dl></dd></dl>
</span></li>

<li id="cite_note-jp-77"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-jp_77-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-jp_77-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-jp_77-2"><sup><i><b>c</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><i>Jurassic Park</i>
<ul><li>Total: <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/movies/?id=jurassicpark.htm" rel="nofollow">"Jurassic Park (1993)"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/Box_Office_Mojo" title="Box Office Mojo">Box Office Mojo</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">September 15,</span> 2013</span>. <q>Worldwide: $1,029,153,882; Production Budget: $63 million</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=Jurassic+Park+%281993%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Fmovies%2F%3Fid%3Djurassicpark.htm&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li>
<li>Original release: <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://web.archive.org/web/20130119003445/http://boxofficemojo.com/movies/?id=jurassicpark.htm" rel="nofollow">"Jurassic Park (1993)"</a>. <i>Box Office Mojo</i>. Archived from <a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/movies/?id=jurassicpark.htm" rel="nofollow">the original</a> on January 19, 2013<span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">April 5,</span> 2013</span>. <q>$914,691,118</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=Jurassic+Park+%281993%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Fmovies%2F%3Fid%3Djurassicpark.htm&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li>
<li>2011 re-release: <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://boxofficemojo.com/movies/intl/?id=_fJURASSICPARK201&amp;country=UK&amp;wk=2011W38&amp;id=_fJURASSICPARK201" rel="nofollow">"Jurassic Park (2011 re-release)"</a>. <i>Box Office Mojo</i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">January 17,</span> 2015</span>. <q>United Kingdom: $786,021</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=Jurassic+Park+%282011+re-release%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fboxofficemojo.com%2Fmovies%2Fintl%2F%3Fid%3D_fJURASSICPARK201%26country%3DUK%26wk%3D2011W38%26id%3D_fJURASSICPARK201&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li>
<li>As of 2010: <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, pp. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA756#v=twopage" rel="nofollow">756–757</a>. "Production Cost: $70.0 (Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s) ... <i>Jurassic Park</i> was a smash at the box office, bringing in $920 million in worldwide box office and spawning two sequels."</li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-Jurassic_Park_peak-78"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-Jurassic_Park_peak_78-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Jurassic_Park_peak_78-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFKrämer1999">Krämer, Peter (1999). <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/titanicanatomyof0000unse/page/108" rel="nofollow">"Women First: Titanic, Action-Adventure Films, and Hollywood's Female Audience"</a>.  In Sandler, Kevin S.; Studlar, Gaylyn (eds.). <i>Titanic: Anatomy of a Blockbuster</i>. <a href="/wiki/Rutgers_University_Press" title="Rutgers University Press">Rutgers University Press</a>. pp. <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/titanicanatomyof0000unse/page/108" rel="nofollow">108–131</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-8135-2669-0" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-8135-2669-0"><bdi>978-0-8135-2669-0</bdi></a>. <q><a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=q1Q8PlAnosQC&amp;pg=PA130" rel="nofollow">p. 130</a>: The list has <i>Jurassic Park</i> at number one with $913 million, followed by <i>The Lion King</i>...</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=bookitem&amp;rft.atitle=Women+First%3A+Titanic%2C+Action-Adventure+Films%2C+and+Hollywood%27s+Female+Audience&amp;rft.btitle=Titanic%3A+Anatomy+of+a+Blockbuster&amp;rft.pages=108-131&amp;rft.pub=Rutgers+University+Press&amp;rft.date=1999&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-8135-2669-0&amp;rft.aulast=Kr%C3%A4mer&amp;rft.aufirst=Peter&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Farchive.org%2Fdetails%2Ftitanicanatomyof0000unse%2Fpage%2F108&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>

<li id="cite_note-tdk-89"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-tdk_89-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-tdk_89-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><i>The Dark Knight</i>
