"On its initial release <i>Pinocchio</i> brought in only $1.6 million in domestic rentals (compared with <i>Snow White</i><span class="nowrap" style="padding-left:0.1em;">'</span>s $4.2 million) and $1.9 million in foreign rentals (compared with <i>Snow White</i><span class="nowrap" style="padding-left:0.1em;">'</span>s $4.3 million)."</dd></dl></dd></dl>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-172"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-172">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text">1938
<ul><li><i>You Can't Take It With You</i>:<link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.webcitation.org/6AeWlL1hk?url=http://focusfeatures.com/flashback?article_url=you_can_t_take_it_with_you_premieres" rel="nofollow">"You Can't Take It With You Premieres"</a>. <a href="/wiki/Focus_Features" title="Focus Features">Focus Features</a>. Archived from <a class="external text" href="http://focusfeatures.com/flashback?article_url=you_can_t_take_it_with_you_premieres" rel="nofollow">the original</a> on September 13, 2012. <q><i>You Can't Take It With You</i> received excellent reviews, won Best Picture and Best Director at the 1938 Academy Awards, and earned over $5 million worldwide.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=You+Can%27t+Take+It+With+You+Premieres&amp;rft.pub=Focus+Features&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Ffocusfeatures.com%2Fflashback%3Farticle_url%3Dyou_can_t_take_it_with_you_premieres&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li>
<li><i>Boys Town</i>: <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFBlock2010">Block, Alex Ben (2010), <i>Boys Town</i>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA215" rel="nofollow">215</a>, <q>The film quickly became a smash nationwide, making a profit of over $2 million on worldwide rentals of $4 million.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Boys+Town&amp;rft.pages=215&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Block&amp;rft.aufirst=Alex+Ben&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span> In: <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>.</li>
<li><i>The Adventures of Robin Hood</i>: <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation journal cs1" id="CITEREFGlancy1995">Glancy, H. Mark (1995). "Warner Bros Film Grosses, 1921–51: the William Schaefer ledger". <i>Historical Journal of Film, Radio and Television</i>. <b>1</b> (15): 55–60. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Doi_(identifier)" title="Doi (identifier)">doi</a>:<a class="external text" href="https://doi.org/10.1080%2F01439689500260031" rel="nofollow">10.1080/01439689500260031</a>. <q>$3.981 million.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Historical+Journal+of+Film%2C+Radio+and+Television&amp;rft.atitle=Warner+Bros+Film+Grosses%2C+1921%E2%80%9351%3A+the+William+Schaefer+ledger&amp;rft.volume=1&amp;rft.issue=15&amp;rft.pages=55-60&amp;rft.date=1995&amp;rft_id=info%3Adoi%2F10.1080%2F01439689500260031&amp;rft.aulast=Glancy&amp;rft.aufirst=H.+Mark&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li>
<li><i>Alexander's Ragtime Band</i>: <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFBlock2010">Block, Hayley Taylor (2010), <i>Alexander's Ragtime Band</i>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA213&amp;q=%22worldwide%20rentals%22" rel="nofollow">213</a>, <q>Once the confusion cleared, however, the film blossomed into a commercial success, with a profit of $978,000 on worldwide rentals of $3.6 million.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Alexander%27s+Ragtime+Band&amp;rft.pages=213&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Block&amp;rft.aufirst=Hayley+Taylor&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span> In: <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>.</li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-173"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-173">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation magazine cs1" id="CITEREFChartier1938">Chartier, Roy (September 6, 1938). <a class="external text" href="https://www.variety.com/review/VE1117796537" rel="nofollow">"You Can't Take It With You"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/Variety_(magazine)" title="Variety (magazine)">Variety</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">September 13,</span> 2011</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Variety&amp;rft.atitle=You+Can%27t+Take+It+With+You&amp;rft.date=1938-09-06&amp;rft.aulast=Chartier&amp;rft.aufirst=Roy&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.variety.com%2Freview%2FVE1117796537&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-174"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-174">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.the-numbers.com/movies/1939/0GWTW.php" rel="nofollow">"Gone with the Wind"</a>. <i>The Numbers</i>. Nash Information Services. LLC<span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">February 8,</span> 2013</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Numbers&amp;rft.atitle=Gone+with+the+Wind&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.the-numbers.com%2Fmovies%2F1939%2F0GWTW.php&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-175"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-175">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://pro.boxoffice.com/movie/4094/gone-with-the-wind" rel="nofollow">"Gone with the Wind"</a>. <i><a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Boxoffice_(magazine)" title="Boxoffice (magazine)">Boxoffice</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">May 29,</span> 2016</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Boxoffice&amp;rft.atitle=Gone+with+the+Wind&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fpro.boxoffice.com%2Fmovie%2F4094%2Fgone-with-the-wind&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-gwtw_tcm-176"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-gwtw_tcm_176-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-gwtw_tcm_176-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1" id="CITEREFMillerStafford2007">Miller, Frank; Stafford, Jeff (January 5, 2007). <a class="external text" href="https://web.archive.org/web/20130728160304/http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/414427/Gone-With-the-Wind/articles.html" rel="nofollow">"Gone With the Wind (1939) – Articles"</a>. <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">Turner Classic Movies</a>. Archived from <a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/414427/Gone-With-the-Wind/articles.html" rel="nofollow">the original</a> on July 28, 2013.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=Gone+With+the+Wind+%281939%29+%E2%80%93+Articles&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft.date=2007-01-05&amp;rft.aulast=Miller&amp;rft.aufirst=Frank&amp;rft.au=Stafford%2C+Jeff&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F414427%2FGone-With-the-Wind%2Farticles.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-177"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-177">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/movies/?id=gonewiththewind.htm" rel="nofollow"><i>Gone with the Wind</i></a> at <a href="/wiki/Box_Office_Mojo" title="Box Office Mojo">Box Office Mojo</a></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-178"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-178">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">Hall &amp; Neale 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA283" rel="nofollow">283</a> ."The final negative cost of <i>Gone with the Wind</i> (<i>GWTW</i>) has been variously reported between $3.9 million and $4.25 million."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-179"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-179">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://pro.boxoffice.com/movie/7922/pinocchio-1940" rel="nofollow">"Pinocchio (1940)"</a>. <i><a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Boxoffice_(magazine)" title="Boxoffice (magazine)">Boxoffice</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">May 29,</span> 2016</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Boxoffice&amp;rft.atitle=Pinocchio+%281940%29&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fpro.boxoffice.com%2Fmovie%2F7922%2Fpinocchio-1940&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Barrier602-180"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-Barrier602_180-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFBarrier2003">Barrier, Michael (2003). <i>Hollywood Cartoons: American Animation in Its Golden Age</i>. New York: <a href="/wiki/Oxford_University_Press" title="Oxford University Press">Oxford University Press</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=xt5k08yuAXIC&amp;q=pinocchio%20%22negative%20cost%20was%20%242.6%20million%22" rel="nofollow">266</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-19-983922-3" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-19-983922-3"><bdi>978-0-19-983922-3</bdi></a>. <q>The film's negative cost was $2.6 million, more than $1 million higher than <i>Snow White</i><span class="nowrap" style="padding-left:0.1em;">'</span>s.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Hollywood+Cartoons%3A+American+Animation+in+Its+Golden+Age&amp;rft.place=New+York&amp;rft.pages=266&amp;rft.pub=Oxford+University+Press&amp;rft.date=2003&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-19-983922-3&amp;rft.aulast=Barrier&amp;rft.aufirst=Michael&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Schatz_(1999)-181"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-Schatz_(1999)_181-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFSchatz1999">Schatz, Thomas (1999) [1st. pub. 1997]. <i>Boom and Bust: American Cinema in the 1940s</i>. Volume 6 of History of the American Cinema. <a href="/wiki/University_of_California_Press" title="University of California Press">University of California Press</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=dwf5SUcfousC&amp;pg=PA466" rel="nofollow">466</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-520-22130-7" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-520-22130-7"><bdi>978-0-520-22130-7</bdi></a>. <q><i>Boom Town</i> ($4.6 million).</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Boom+and+Bust%3A+American+Cinema+in+the+1940s&amp;rft.pages=466&amp;rft.pub=University+of+California+Press&amp;rft.date=1999&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-520-22130-7&amp;rft.aulast=Schatz&amp;rft.aufirst=Thomas&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-182"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-182">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, pp. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA258" rel="nofollow">258</a>–<a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA259&amp;q=boom%20town%20biggest%20moneymaker" rel="nofollow">259</a>. "Production Cost: $2.1 (Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s) ... <i>Boom Town</i> was the biggest moneymaker of 1940 and one of the top films of the decade."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-183"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-183">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA267&amp;q=sergeant%20york%20worldwide" rel="nofollow">267</a>. "With worldwide rentals of $7.8 million in its initial release, the movie made a net profit of over $3 million."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-184"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-184">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFFinler2003">Finler 2003</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=rvVhEJmbfrsC&amp;pg=PA301" rel="nofollow">301</a>. "The studio did particularly well with its war-related pictures, such as <i>Sergeant York</i> (1941), which cost $1.6 million but was the studio's biggest hit of the decade aside from <i>This is the Army</i> (1943), the Irving Berlin musical for which the profits were donated to the Army Emergency Relief fund."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-185"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-185">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://pro.boxoffice.com/movie/810/bambi" rel="nofollow">"Bambi"</a>. <i><a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Boxoffice_(magazine)" title="Boxoffice (magazine)">Boxoffice</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">May 29,</span> 2016</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Boxoffice&amp;rft.atitle=Bambi&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fpro.boxoffice.com%2Fmovie%2F810%2Fbambi&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-186"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-186">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA281&amp;q=%22worldwide+rentals%22" rel="nofollow">281</a>. "Worldwide rentals of $3,449,353 barely recouped the film's nearly $2 million production cost."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Disney-187"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-Disney_187-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Disney_187-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Disney_187-2"><sup><i><b>c</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Disney_187-3"><sup><i><b>d</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, pp. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA712#v=twopage" rel="nofollow">712–713</a>.
<ul><li><i>Bambi</i>: "Worldwide Box Office: $266.8; Production Cost: $1.7 (Millions of $s)"</li>
<li><i>101 Dalmatians</i>: "Worldwide Box Office: $215.0; Production Cost: $3.6 (Millions of $s)"</li>
<li><i>The Jungle Book</i>: "Worldwide Box Office: $170.8"; Production Cost: $3.9 (Millions of $s)"</li>
<li><i>Aladdin</i>: "Worldwide Box Office: $505.1"; Production Cost: $28.0 (Millions of $s)"</li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-188"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-188">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFGlancy1999">Glancy, Mark (1999). <i>When Hollywood Loved Britain: The Hollywood 'British' Film 1939–1945</i>. <a href="/wiki/Manchester_University_Press" title="Manchester University Press">Manchester University Press</a>. pp. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=T_Yryky_eA4C&amp;pg=PA94" rel="nofollow">94</a>–<a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=T_Yryky_eA4C&amp;pg=PA95" rel="nofollow">95</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-7190-4853-1" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-7190-4853-1"><bdi>978-0-7190-4853-1</bdi></a>. <q><i>Mrs Miniver</i> was a phenomenon. It was the most popular film of the year (from any studio) in both North America and Britain, and its foreign earnings were three times higher than those of any other MGM film released in the 1941–42 season. The production cost ($1,344,000) was one of the highest of the season, indicating the studio never thought of the film as a potential loss-maker. When the film earned a worldwide gross of $8,878,000, MGM had the highest profit ($4,831,000) in its history. <i>Random Harvest</i> nearly matched the success of <i>Mrs Miniver</i> with worldwide earnings of $8,147,000 yielding the second-highest profit in MGM's history ($4,384,000). <i>Random Harvest</i> was also the most popular film of the year in Britain, where it proved to be even more popular than Britain's most acclaimed war film, <i>In Which We Serve</i>.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=When+Hollywood+Loved+Britain%3A+The+Hollywood+%27British%27+Film+1939%E2%80%931945&amp;rft.pages=94-95&amp;rft.pub=Manchester+University+Press&amp;rft.date=1999&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-7190-4853-1&amp;rft.aulast=Glancy&amp;rft.aufirst=Mark&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-189"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-189">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>
<ul><li><i>Mrs. Miniver</i>: <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFBurns2010">Burns, Douglas (2010), <i>Mrs. Miniver</i>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA279" rel="nofollow">279</a>, <q><i>Mrs. Miniver'</i>s galvanizing effect on Americans spawned a record-breaking ten-week run at Radio City Music Hall and garnered a $5.4 million take in domestic rentals (making <i>Mrs. Miniver</i> 1942's top grosser), with a $4.8 million profit on worldwide rentals of $8.9 million.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Mrs.+Miniver&amp;rft.pages=279&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Burns&amp;rft.aufirst=Douglas&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li>
<li><i>Yankee Doodle Dandy</i>: p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA275&amp;q=%22worldwide+rentals%22" rel="nofollow">275</a>. "It became the second biggest box-office hit of 1942 (after Mrs. Miniver) and was praised by critics, making a profit of $3.4 million on worldwide rentals of $6.5 million."</li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-190"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-190">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFMcAdams2010">McAdams, Frank (2010), <i>For Whom the Bell Tolls</i>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA287" rel="nofollow">287</a>, <q>Despite the early furor over the novel being "pro-red and immoral," the film opened to strong and favorable reviews and brought in $11 million in worldwide rentals in its initial release.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=For+Whom+the+Bell+Tolls&amp;rft.pages=287&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=McAdams&amp;rft.aufirst=Frank&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span> In: <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>.</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-191"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-191">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/75353/For-Whom-the-Bell-Tolls/notes.html" rel="nofollow">"For Whom the Bell Tolls (1943) – Notes"</a>. <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">Turner Classic Movies</a><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">June 24,</span> 2012</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=For+Whom+the+Bell+Tolls+%281943%29+%E2%80%93+Notes&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F75353%2FFor-Whom-the-Bell-Tolls%2Fnotes.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-guy-named-joe-192"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-guy-named-joe_192-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-guy-named-joe_192-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/191/A-Guy-Named-Joe/notes.html" rel="nofollow">"A Guy Named Joe (1944) – Notes"</a>. <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">Turner Classic Movies</a><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">August 29,</span> 2012</span>. <q>According to M-G-M studio records at the AMPAS Library, the film had a negative cost of $2,627,000 and took in $5,363,000 at the box office. When the picture was re-issued for the 1955–56 season, it took in an additional $150,000.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=A+Guy+Named+Joe+%281944%29+%E2%80%93+Notes&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F191%2FA-Guy-Named-Joe%2Fnotes.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-193"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-193">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation journal cs1" id="CITEREFBergreen1996">Bergreen, Laurence (Summer 1996). <a class="external text" href="https://www.archives.gov/publications/prologue/1996/summer/irving-berlin-1.html" rel="nofollow">"Irving Berlin: This Is the Army"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/Prologue_(magazine)" title="Prologue (magazine)">Prologue</a></i>. <b>28</b> (2). <a class="external text" href="https://www.archives.gov/publications/prologue/1996/summer/irving-berlin-3.html" rel="nofollow">Part 3</a><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">August 22,</span> 2012</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Prologue&amp;rft.atitle=Irving+Berlin%3A+This+Is+the+Army&amp;rft.ssn=summer&amp;rft.volume=28&amp;rft.issue=2&amp;rft.pages=Part+3&amp;rft.date=1996&amp;rft.aulast=Bergreen&amp;rft.aufirst=Laurence&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.archives.gov%2Fpublications%2Fprologue%2F1996%2Fsummer%2Firving-berlin-1.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-194"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-194">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/92968/This-Is-the-Army/notes.html" rel="nofollow">"This Is the Army (1943) – Notes"</a>. <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">Turner Classic Movies</a><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">July 9,</span> 2011</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=This+Is+the+Army+%281943%29+%E2%80%93+Notes&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F92968%2FThis-Is-the-Army%2Fnotes.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Finler356-195"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-Finler356_195-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Finler356_195-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Finler356_195-2"><sup><i><b>c</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Finler356_195-3"><sup><i><b>d</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Finler356_195-4"><sup><i><b>e</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Finler356_195-5"><sup><i><b>f</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Finler356_195-6"><sup><i><b>g</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFFinler2003">Finler 2003</a>, pp. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=rvVhEJmbfrsC&amp;pg=PA356" rel="nofollow">356</a>–363</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-196"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-196">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA420&amp;q=%22going%20my%20way%22" rel="nofollow">420</a>. "(Unadjusted $s) in Millions of $s – Production Cost: $1.0"</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-BlockWilson232-197"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson232_197-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson232_197-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA232" rel="nofollow">232</a>.
<ul><li><i>Mrs. Miniver</i>: "Domestic Rentals: $5,358,000; Foreign Rentals: $3,520,000 (Unadjusted $s)"</li>
<li><i>Meet Me in St. Louis</i>: "Domestic Rentals: $5,016,000; Foreign Rentals: $1,623,630 (Unadjusted $s)"</li>
<li><i>Easter Parade</i>: "Domestic Rentals: $4,144,000; Foreign Rentals: $1,774,134 (Unadjusted $s)"</li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-Schaefer_(1999)-198"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-Schaefer_(1999)_198-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFSchaefer1999">Schaefer, Eric (1999). <i>"Bold! Daring! Shocking! True!": A History of Exploitation Films, 1919–1959</i>. <a href="/wiki/Duke_University_Press" title="Duke University Press">Duke University Press</a>. pp. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=CSBZqe0zPaMC&amp;pg=PA197" rel="nofollow">197</a>–199. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-8223-2374-7" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-8223-2374-7"><bdi>978-0-8223-2374-7</bdi></a>. <q>Leading the pack of postwar sex hygiene films was <i>Mom and Dad</i> (1944), which would become not only the most successful sex hygiene film in history but the biggest pre-1960 exploitation film of any kind. At the end of 1947, the <i>Los Angeles Times</i> reported that <i>Mom and Dad</i> had grossed $2 million. By 1949 <i>Time</i> had estimated that <i>Mom and Dad</i> had taken in $8 million from twenty million moviegoers. And publicity issuing from <i>Mom and Dad</i><span class="nowrap" style="padding-left:0.1em;">'</span>s production company indicated that by the end of 1956 it had grossed over $80 million worldwide. Net rentals of around $22 million by 1956 would easily place it in the top ten films of the late 1940s and early 1950s had it appeared on conventional lists. Some estimates have placed its total gross over the years at up to $100 million, and it was still playing drive-in dates into 1975...The film was made for around $65,000 with a crew of Hollywood veterans including director William "One Shot" Beaudine, cinematographer Marcel LePicard, and a cast that sported old stalwarts Hardie Albright, Francis Ford, and John Hamilton.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=%22Bold%21+Daring%21+Shocking%21+True%21%22%3A+A+History+of+Exploitation+Films%2C+1919%E2%80%931959&amp;rft.pages=197-199&amp;rft.pub=Duke+University+Press&amp;rft.date=1999&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-8223-2374-7&amp;rft.aulast=Schaefer&amp;rft.aufirst=Eric&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-199"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-199">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>
<ul><li>p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA296" rel="nofollow">296</a>. "Production Cost: $1.6 (Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)"</li>
<li><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFWasson2010">Wasson, Sam (2010), <i>The Bells of St. Mary's</i>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA297" rel="nofollow">297</a>, <q>This was that rare sequel that did even better at the box office than the original, bringing in a $3.7 million profit on $11.2 million in worldwide rentals.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+Bells+of+St.+Mary%27s&amp;rft.pages=297&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Wasson&amp;rft.aufirst=Sam&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-200"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-200">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.the-numbers.com/movies/1946/0SOTS.php" rel="nofollow">"Song of the South"</a>. <i>The Numbers</i>. Nash Information Services. LLC<span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">July 10,</span> 2011</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Numbers&amp;rft.atitle=Song+of+the+South&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.the-numbers.com%2Fmovies%2F1946%2F0SOTS.php&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-201"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-201">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFGabler2007">Gabler, Neal (2007). <i>Walt Disney: the biography</i>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Aurum_Press" title="Aurum Press">Aurum Press</a>. pp. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=EX0WAQAAIAAJ&amp;dq=%22song+of+the+south%22+%22million%22" rel="nofollow">438</a>. <q>Still, the film wound up grossing $3.3 million...</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Walt+Disney%3A+the+biography&amp;rft.pages=438&amp;rft.pub=Aurum+Press&amp;rft.date=2007&amp;rft.aulast=Gabler&amp;rft.aufirst=Neal&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-202"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-202">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/90871/Song-of-the-South/notes.html" rel="nofollow">"Song of the South (1946) – Notes"</a>. <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">Turner Classic Movies</a><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">July 25,</span> 2012</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=Song+of+the+South+%281946%29+%E2%80%93+Notes&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F90871%2FSong-of-the-South%2Fnotes.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-203"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-203">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">Hall &amp; Neale 2010</a>
<ul><li>p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA132" rel="nofollow">132</a>."<i>Best Years</i> was considerably cheaper, costing only $2.1 million, and therefore vastly more profitable."</li>
<li>p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=Ro0hASPfC68C&amp;pg=PA286" rel="nofollow">286</a> (note 6.70). "Worldwide rentals for <i>The Best Years of Our Lives</i> amounted to $14,750,000."</li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-204"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-204">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFBurns2010">Burns, Douglas (2010), <i>The Best years of Our Lives</i>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA301" rel="nofollow">301</a>, <q>The film made a $5 million profit on worldwide rentals of $14.8 million.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+Best+years+of+Our+Lives&amp;rft.pages=301&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Burns&amp;rft.aufirst=Douglas&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span> In: <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>.</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-HallNeale285-205"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale285_205-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale285_205-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">Hall &amp; Neale 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA285" rel="nofollow">285</a> (note 6.56). "The cost of <i>Duel in the Sun</i> has been reported as both $5,255,000 (Haver, <i>David O'Selznick's Hollywood</i>, 361) and $6,480,000 (Thomson, <i>Showman: The Life of David O'Selznick</i>, 472); the latter figure may include distribution expenses. <i>Forever Amber</i> cost $6,375,000 (Solomon, <i>Twentieth Century-Fox: A Corporate and Financial History</i>, 243)."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-206"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-206">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFChopra-Gant2006">Chopra-Gant, Mike (2006). <i>Hollywood Genres and Post-war America: Masculinity, Family and Nation in Popular Movies and Film Noir</i>. <a href="/wiki/I.B._Tauris" title="I.B. Tauris">I.B. Tauris</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=RVdCSSVd_KQC&amp;pg=PA18" rel="nofollow">18</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-85043-815-1" title="Special:BookSources/978-1-85043-815-1"><bdi>978-1-85043-815-1</bdi></a>. <q><i>Forever Amber</i>: $8 million; <i>Unconquered</i>: $7.5 million; <i>Life with Father</i>: $6.25 million</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Hollywood+Genres+and+Post-war+America%3A+Masculinity%2C+Family+and+Nation+in+Popular+Movies+and+Film+Noir&amp;rft.pages=18&amp;rft.pub=I.B.+Tauris&amp;rft.date=2006&amp;rft.isbn=978-1-85043-815-1&amp;rft.aulast=Chopra-Gant&amp;rft.aufirst=Mike&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-207"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-207">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/4415/Unconquered/notes.html" rel="nofollow">"Unconquered (1947) – Notes"</a>. <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">Turner Classic Movies</a><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">January 10,</span> 2012</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=Unconquered+%281947%29+%E2%80%93+Notes&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F4415%2FUnconquered%2Fnotes.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-208"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-208">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1" id="CITEREFMiller">Miller, Frank. <a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/2332/Easter-Parade/articles.html" rel="nofollow">"Easter Parade (1948) – Articles"</a>. <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">Turner Classic Movies</a><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">July 19,</span> 2012</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=Easter+Parade+%281948%29+%E2%80%93+Articles&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft.aulast=Miller&amp;rft.aufirst=Frank&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F2332%2FEaster-Parade%2Farticles.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-209"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-209">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFStreet2002">Street, Sarah (2002). <i>Transatlantic Crossings: British Feature Films in the United States</i>. <a href="/wiki/Continuum_International_Publishing_Group" title="Continuum International Publishing Group">Continuum International Publishing Group</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=Y5cn_OiCJRkC&amp;pg=PA110" rel="nofollow">110</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-8264-1395-6" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-8264-1395-6"><bdi>978-0-8264-1395-6</bdi></a>. <q>Although both films had higher than average budgets (<i>The Red Shoes</i> cost £505,581 and <i>Hamlet</i> cost £572,530, while the average cost of the other thirty films for which Rank supplied information was £233,000), they resulted in high takings at home and abroad.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Transatlantic+Crossings%3A+British+Feature+Films+in+the+United+States&amp;rft.pages=110&amp;rft.pub=Continuum+International+Publishing+Group&amp;rft.date=2002&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-8264-1395-6&amp;rft.aulast=Street&amp;rft.aufirst=Sarah&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-210"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-210">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1" id="CITEREFOfficer2011">Officer, Lawrence H. (2011). <a class="external text" href="http://www.measuringworth.com/datasets/exchangepound/result.php?year_source=1791&amp;year_result=2011" rel="nofollow">"Dollar-Pound Exchange Rate From 1791"</a>. MeasuringWorth<span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">November 18,</span> 2012</span>. <q>1947–1948: $4.03 (per British pound)</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=Dollar-Pound+Exchange+Rate+From+1791&amp;rft.pub=MeasuringWorth&amp;rft.date=2011&amp;rft.aulast=Officer&amp;rft.aufirst=Lawrence+H.&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.measuringworth.com%2Fdatasets%2Fexchangepound%2Fresult.php%3Fyear_source%3D1791%26year_result%3D2011&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-211"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-211">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://web.archive.org/web/20111228005949/http://www.the-numbers.com/movies/1948/0SNPI.php" rel="nofollow">"The Snake Pit"</a>. <i>The Numbers</i>. Nash Information Services. LLC. Archived from <a class="external text" href="https://www.the-numbers.com/movies/1948/0SNPI.php" rel="nofollow">the original</a> on December 28, 2011.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Numbers&amp;rft.atitle=The+Snake+Pit&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.the-numbers.com%2Fmovies%2F1948%2F0SNPI.php&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-212"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-212">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1">"<span class="cs1-kern-left">'</span>Snake Pit' Seen No Problem After All". <i><a href="/wiki/Variety_(magazine)" title="Variety (magazine)">Variety</a></i>. January 19, 1949. p. <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/stream/variety173-1949-01#page/n348/mode/1up" rel="nofollow">7</a>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Variety&amp;rft.atitle=%27Snake+Pit%27+Seen+No+Problem+After+All&amp;rft.pages=7&amp;rft.date=1949-01-19&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-HallNeale136-213"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale136_213-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale136_213-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">Hall &amp; Neale 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA136" rel="nofollow">136</a>–139
<ul><li><i>Samson and Delilah</i>: "...the film became the highest grosser in the studio's history to date, with domestic rentals of $7,976,730 by 1955 and a further $6,232,520 overseas...For all their spectacle, <i>Samson</i> and <i>David</i> were quite economically produced, costing $3,097,563 and $2,170,000 respectively."</li>
<li><i>Quo Vadis</i>: "Production costs totaled a record $7,623,000...Worldwide rentals totaled $21,037,000, almost half of which came from the foreign market."</li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-214"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-214">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.the-numbers.com/movie/Cinderella-(1950)" rel="nofollow">"Cinderella (1950)"</a>. <i>The Numbers</i>. Nash Information Services<span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">January 2,</span> 2017</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Numbers&amp;rft.atitle=Cinderella+%281950%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.the-numbers.com%2Fmovie%2FCinderella-%281950%29&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-215"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-215">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFEisnerSchwartz2009">Eisner, Michael D.; Schwartz, Tony (2009). <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/workinprogress00eisn_0/page/178" rel="nofollow"><i>Work in Progress</i></a>. <a href="/wiki/Pennsylvania_State_University" title="Pennsylvania State University">Pennsylvania State University</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=73_WAAAAMAAJ&amp;q=cinderella+%22Released+in+February+1950,+it+cost+nearly+%243+million+to+make+but+earned+more+than+%2420+million+worldwide%22" rel="nofollow">178</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-7868-8507-7" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-7868-8507-7"><bdi>978-0-7868-8507-7</bdi></a>. <q><i>Cinderella</i> revived its fortunes. Re-released in February 1950, it cost nearly $3 million to make but earned more than $20 million worldwide.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Work+in+Progress&amp;rft.pages=178&amp;rft.pub=Pennsylvania+State+University&amp;rft.date=2009&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-7868-8507-7&amp;rft.aulast=Eisner&amp;rft.aufirst=Michael+D.&amp;rft.au=Schwartz%2C+Tony&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Farchive.org%2Fdetails%2Fworkinprogress00eisn_0%2Fpage%2F178&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-216"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-216">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFBarrier2003">Barrier, Michael (2003). <i>Hollywood Cartoons: American Animation in Its Golden Age</i>. <a href="/wiki/Oxford_University_Press" title="Oxford University Press">Oxford University Press</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=zDJXnzMh7bkC&amp;pg=PA401#v=snippet&amp;q=%242.2%20million%20cinderella%20gross%20rentals%20disney%20and%20rko%20%247.8%20million" rel="nofollow">401</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-19-516729-0" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-19-516729-0"><bdi>978-0-19-516729-0</bdi></a>. <q>It cost around $2.2 million, little more than each of the two package features, Melody Time and The Adventures of Ichabod and Mr. Toad (as Tluo Fabulous Characters had ultimately been named), that just preceded it, but its gross rentals—an amount shared by Disney and RKO—were $7.8 million, almost twice as much as the two package features combined.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Hollywood+Cartoons%3A+American+Animation+in+Its+Golden+Age&amp;rft.pages=401&amp;rft.pub=Oxford+University+Press&amp;rft.date=2003&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-19-516729-0&amp;rft.aulast=Barrier&amp;rft.aufirst=Michael&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Mannix-217"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-Mannix_217-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1"><i>The E. J. Mannix ledger</i>. Margaret Herrick Library, <a href="/wiki/Academy_of_Motion_Picture_Arts_and_Sciences" title="Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences">Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences</a>: Howard Strickling Collection. 1962.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+E.+J.+Mannix+ledger&amp;rft.place=Margaret+Herrick+Library%2C+Academy+of+Motion+Picture+Arts+and+Sciences&amp;rft.pub=Howard+Strickling+Collection&amp;rft.date=1962&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Lev-218"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-Lev_218-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Lev_218-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFLev2006">Lev, Peter (2006). <i>Transforming the Screen, 1950–1959</i>. Volume 7 of History of the American Cinema. <a href="/wiki/University_of_California_Press" title="University of California Press">University of California Press</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-520-24966-0" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-520-24966-0"><bdi>978-0-520-24966-0</bdi></a>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Transforming+the+Screen%2C+1950%E2%80%931959&amp;rft.pub=University+of+California+Press&amp;rft.date=2006&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-520-24966-0&amp;rft.aulast=Lev&amp;rft.aufirst=Peter&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span>
<ul><li><i>Quo Vadis</i>: p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=TEGl2Ele_XoC&amp;pg=PA15" rel="nofollow">15</a>. "MGM's most expensive film of the period, <i>Quo Vadis</i> (1951) also did extremely well. The cost was $7,623,000, earnings were an estimated $21.2 million (with foreign earnings almost 50 percent of this total), and profit was estimated at $5,562,000."</li>
<li><i>Rear Window</i>: pp. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=TEGl2Ele_XoC&amp;pg=PA203" rel="nofollow">203</a>–<a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=TEGl2Ele_XoC&amp;pg=PA204" rel="nofollow">204</a>. "Rear Window (1954) was an excellent commercial success, with a cost of $1 million and North American rentals of $5.3 million."</li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-BlockWilson335-219"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson335_219-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson335_219-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA335" rel="nofollow">335</a>.
<ul><li><i>The Robe</i>: "Domestic Rentals: $16.7; Foreign Rentals: $9.4; Production Cost: $4.1 (Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."</li>
<li><i>Quo Vadis</i>: "Domestic Rentals: $11.1; Foreign Rentals: $15.6; Production Cost: $7.5 (Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."</li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-220"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-220">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFMulligan1956">Mulligan, Hugh A. (September 23, 1956). "Cinerama Pushing Ahead As Biggest Money-Maker". <i><a href="/wiki/The_Register-Guard" title="The Register-Guard">The Register-Guard</a></i>. Eugene, Oregon. p. <a class="external text" href="https://news.google.com/newspapers?id=GPRVAAAAIBAJ&amp;sjid=YeIDAAAAIBAJ&amp;pg=6899,3861317" rel="nofollow">7B</a>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Register-Guard&amp;rft.atitle=Cinerama+Pushing+Ahead+As+Biggest+Money-Maker&amp;rft.pages=7B&amp;rft.date=1956-09-23&amp;rft.aulast=Mulligan&amp;rft.aufirst=Hugh+A.&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-221"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-221">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFZone2012">Zone, Ray (2012). <i>3-D Revolution: The History of Modern Stereoscopic Cinema</i>. <a href="/wiki/University_Press_of_Kentucky" title="University Press of Kentucky">University Press of Kentucky</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=k2Ajw-EEOEkC&amp;pg=PA71" rel="nofollow">71</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-8131-3611-0" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-8131-3611-0"><bdi>978-0-8131-3611-0</bdi></a>. <q>Produced at a cost of $1 million, <i>This is Cinerama</i> ran 122 weeks, earning $4.7 million in its initial New York run alone and eventually grossed over $32 million. It was obvious to Hollywood that the public was ready for a new form of motion picture entertainment. The first five Cinerama feature-length travelogues, though they only played in twenty-two theaters, pulled in a combined gross of $82 million.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=3-D+Revolution%3A+The+History+of+Modern+Stereoscopic+Cinema&amp;rft.pages=71&amp;rft.pub=University+Press+of+Kentucky&amp;rft.date=2012&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-8131-3611-0&amp;rft.aulast=Zone&amp;rft.aufirst=Ray&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-222"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-222">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFBurns2010">Burns, Douglas (2010), <i>The Greatest Show on Earth</i>, pp. 354–<a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA355" rel="nofollow">355</a>, <q>By May 1953, <i>Variety</i> was reporting that the Best Picture winner had amassed $18.35 million in worldwide rentals.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+Greatest+Show+on+Earth&amp;rft.pages=354-355&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Burns&amp;rft.aufirst=Douglas&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span> In: <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>.</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-223"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-223">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/4608/The-Greatest-Show-on-Earth/notes.html" rel="nofollow">"The Greatest Show on Earth (1952) – Notes"</a>. <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">Turner Classic Movies</a><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">January 8,</span> 2012</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=The+Greatest+Show+on+Earth+%281952%29+%E2%80%93+Notes&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F4608%2FThe-Greatest-Show-on-Earth%2Fnotes.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-224"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-224">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/86554/Peter-Pan/notes.html" rel="nofollow">"Peter Pan (1953) – Notes"</a>. <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">Turner Classic Movies</a><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">July 24,</span> 2011</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=Peter+Pan+%281953%29+%E2%80%93+Notes&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F86554%2FPeter-Pan%2Fnotes.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-225"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-225">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation magazine cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/stream/variety193-1954-01#page/n301/mode/2up" rel="nofollow">"Top Grossers of 1953"</a>. <i>Variety</i>. January 13, 1954. p. 10<span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">September 12,</span> 2019</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Variety&amp;rft.atitle=Top+Grossers+of+1953&amp;rft.pages=10&amp;rft.date=1954-01-13&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Farchive.org%2Fstream%2Fvariety193-1954-01%23page%2Fn301%2Fmode%2F2up&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-HallNeale147-226"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale147_226-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">Hall &amp; Neale 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA147" rel="nofollow">147</a>–<a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA148" rel="nofollow">148</a>. "To take full advantage of CinemaScope's panoramic possibilities, shooting was delayed for the sets to be redesigned and rebuilt, adding $500,000 to the eventual $4.1 million budget...It ultimately returned domestic rentals of $17.5 million and $25 million worldwide, placing it second only to <i>Gone with the Wind</i> in <i>Variety</i><span class="nowrap" style="padding-left:0.1em;">'</span>s annually updated chart."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-227"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-227">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA367&amp;q=%22foreign%20rentals%22" rel="nofollow">367</a>. "It brought in $16.7 million in domestic rentals, $9.4 million in foreign rentals, and made a net profit of $8.1 million."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-228"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-228">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://pro.boxoffice.com/movie/8393/rear-window" rel="nofollow">"Rear Window"</a>. <i><a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Boxoffice_(magazine)" title="Boxoffice (magazine)">Boxoffice</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">May 29,</span> 2016</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Boxoffice&amp;rft.atitle=Rear+Window&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fpro.boxoffice.com%2Fmovie%2F8393%2Frear-window&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-229"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-229">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://pro.boxoffice.com/movie/11167/white-christmas" rel="nofollow">"White Christmas"</a>. <i><a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Boxoffice_(magazine)" title="Boxoffice (magazine)">Boxoffice</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">May 29,</span> 2016</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Boxoffice&amp;rft.atitle=White+Christmas&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fpro.boxoffice.com%2Fmovie%2F11167%2Fwhite-christmas&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-230"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-230">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA420" rel="nofollow">420</a>. "Domestic Box Office: $19.6 million; Production Cost: $3.8 million."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-231"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-231">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">Hall &amp; Neale 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA149" rel="nofollow">149</a>. "VistaVision was first used for the musical <i>White Christmas</i> (1954), which <i>Variety</i> named the top grosser of its year with anticipated domestic rentals of $12 million."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-232"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-232">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://pro.boxoffice.com/movie/14246/20000-leagues-under-the-sea" rel="nofollow">"20000 Leagues Under The Sea"</a>. <i><a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Boxoffice_(magazine)" title="Boxoffice (magazine)">Boxoffice</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">May 29,</span> 2016</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Boxoffice&amp;rft.atitle=20000+Leagues+Under+The+Sea&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fpro.boxoffice.com%2Fmovie%2F14246%2F20000-leagues-under-the-sea&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-233"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-233">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1" id="CITEREFMiller">Miller, John M. <a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/20642/20-000-Leagues-Under-the-Sea/articles.html" rel="nofollow">"20,000 Leagues Under the Sea (1954) – Articles"</a>. <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">Turner Classic Movies</a><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">January 9,</span> 2012</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=20%2C000+Leagues+Under+the+Sea+%281954%29+%E2%80%93+Articles&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft.aulast=Miller&amp;rft.aufirst=John+M&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F20642%2F20-000-Leagues-Under-the-Sea%2Farticles.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-234"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-234">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFFinler2003">Finler 2003</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=rvVhEJmbfrsC&amp;pg=PA320" rel="nofollow">320</a>. "It was up and running in time to handle Disney's most elaborate expensive feature, <i>20,000 Leagues Under the Sea</i>, based on the book by Jules Verne, starring James Mason and Kirk Douglas and directed by Richard Fleischer at a cost of $4.5 million."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-DisneyWWVariety2003-235"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-DisneyWWVariety2003_235-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-DisneyWWVariety2003_235-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-DisneyWWVariety2003_235-2"><sup><i><b>c</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFD'Alessandro2003">D'Alessandro, Anthony (October 27, 2003). "Disney Animated Features at the Worldwide Box Office". <i><a href="/wiki/Variety_(magazine)" title="Variety (magazine)">Variety</a></i>. <q>The Jungle Book $378 million; One Hundred and One Dalmatians $303 million; Lady and the Tramp $187 million</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Variety&amp;rft.atitle=Disney+Animated+Features+at+the+Worldwide+Box+Office&amp;rft.date=2003-10-27&amp;rft.aulast=D%27Alessandro&amp;rft.aufirst=Anthony&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-236"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-236">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/80607/Lady-and-the-Tramp/notes.html" rel="nofollow">"Lady and the Tramp (1955) – Notes"</a>. <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">Turner Classic Movies</a><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">July 27,</span> 2011</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=Lady+and+the+Tramp+%281955%29+%E2%80%93+Notes&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F80607%2FLady-and-the-Tramp%2Fnotes.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-237"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-237">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFMinego1956">Minego, Pete (May 21, 1956). "Pete's Pungent Patter". <i><a href="/wiki/Portsmouth_Daily_Times" title="Portsmouth Daily Times">Portsmouth Daily Times</a></i>. Portsmouth, Ohio. p. <a class="external text" href="https://news.google.com/newspapers?id=I7dQAAAAIBAJ&amp;sjid=6M8MAAAAIBAJ&amp;pg=4059,7032150&amp;dq=cinerama-holiday+million" rel="nofollow">19</a>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Portsmouth+Daily+Times&amp;rft.atitle=Pete%27s+Pungent+Patter&amp;rft.pages=19&amp;rft.date=1956-05-21&amp;rft.aulast=Minego&amp;rft.aufirst=Pete&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-238"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-238">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/493857/Cinerama-Holiday/notes.html" rel="nofollow">"Cinerama Holiday (1955) – Notes"</a>. <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">Turner Classic Movies</a><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">July 7,</span> 2012</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=Cinerama+Holiday+%281955%29+%E2%80%93+Notes&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F493857%2FCinerama-Holiday%2Fnotes.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-239"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-239">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>
<ul><li>p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA382" rel="nofollow">382</a>. "Production Cost: $2.4 (Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)"</li>
<li><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFBurns2010">Burns, Douglas (2010), <i>Mister Roberts</i>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA383#v=onepage&amp;q=%22%249.9%20million%22" rel="nofollow">383</a>, <q><i>Mister Roberts</i> sailed onto movie screens buoyed by enthusiastic reviews and receptive audiences. For pr, Fonda, Cagney, and lemmon reenacted several scenes on ed sullivan's popular <i>Toast of the Town</i> television variety show. It returned a net profit of $4.5 million on worldwide rentals of $9.9 million, putting it in the top 5 domestic films of 1955.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Mister+Roberts&amp;rft.pages=383&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Burns&amp;rft.aufirst=Douglas&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-BlockWilson327-240"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson327_240-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA327" rel="nofollow">327</a>. "Production cost: $13.3 million; Domestic Film Rental: $31.3; Foreign Film Rental: $23.9; Worldwide Box office (estimated): $122.7 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-HallNeale159-241"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale159_241-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale159_241-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">Hall &amp; Neale 2010</a>, pp. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA159" rel="nofollow">159</a>–161
<ul><li><i>The Ten Commandments</i>: "No film did more to entrench roadshow policy than <i>The Ten Commandments</i>. While the success of <i>This Is Cinerama</i>, <i>The Robe</i>, and even <i>Eighty Days</i> could be attributed, at least in part, to their respective photographic and projection formats, that of DeMille's film (which cost a record $13,266,491) could not...General release began at normal prices in 1959 and continued until the end of the following year, when the film was temporarily withdrawn (the first of several reissues came in 1966). The worldwide rental by this time was around $60 million. In the domestic market it dislodged <i>Gone with the Wind</i> from the number one position on Variety's list of All-Time Rentals Champs. <i>GWTW</i> had hitherto maintained its lead through several reissues (and was soon to regain it through another in 1961)."</li>
<li><i>The Bridge on the River Kwai</i>: Columbia's Anglo-American war film <i>The Bridge on the River Kwai</i> (1957) opened on a roadshow basis in selected U.S. cities (including New York, Chicago, Boston, and Los Angeles) and in London. Costing only $2,840,000 to produce, it grossed $30.6 million worldwide on first release."</li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-242"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-242">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">Hall &amp; Neale 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA153" rel="nofollow">153</a>. "<i>South Pacific</i> also became for a time the most successful film ever released in the United Kingdom, where it earned a box-office gross three times its negative cost of $5,610,000. Anticipated global rentals after three years were $30 million."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-243"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-243">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFRoss2011">Ross, Steven J. (2011). <i>Hollywood Left and Right: How Movie Stars Shaped American Politics</i>. <a href="/wiki/Oxford_University_Press" title="Oxford University Press">Oxford University Press</a>. pp. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=pn1CuZUHk9MC&amp;pg=PA278#v=twopage" rel="nofollow">278–279</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-19-991143-1" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-19-991143-1"><bdi>978-0-19-991143-1</bdi></a>. <q>Costing $15 million to produce, the film earned $47 million by the end of 1961 and $90 million worldwide by January 1989.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Hollywood+Left+and+Right%3A+How+Movie+Stars+Shaped+American+Politics&amp;rft.pages=278-279&amp;rft.pub=Oxford+University+Press&amp;rft.date=2011&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-19-991143-1&amp;rft.aulast=Ross&amp;rft.aufirst=Steven+J.&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-244"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-244">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA324" rel="nofollow">324</a>. "Worldwide box office: $146.9 million; Worldwide rentals: $66.1 million; Production cost: $15.9 million. (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)"</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-245"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-245">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFReid2006">Reid, John Howard (2006). <i>America's Best, Britain's Finest: A Survey of Mixed Movies</i>. Volume 14 of Hollywood classics. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Lulu_(company)" title="Lulu (company)">Lulu</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=zCm5aNkafSYC&amp;pg=PA243" rel="nofollow">243</a>–245. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-4116-7877-4" title="Special:BookSources/978-1-4116-7877-4"><bdi>978-1-4116-7877-4</bdi></a>. <q>Negative cost: around $4 million; Worldwide film rentals gross (including 1968 American reissue) to 1970: $30 million.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=America%27s+Best%2C+Britain%27s+Finest%3A+A+Survey+of+Mixed+Movies&amp;rft.pages=243-245&amp;rft.pub=Lulu&amp;rft.date=2006&amp;rft.isbn=978-1-4116-7877-4&amp;rft.aulast=Reid&amp;rft.aufirst=John+Howard&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
