<li id="cite_note-246"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-246">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFWebster2010">Webster, Patrick (2010). <i>Love and Death in Kubrick: A Critical Study of the Films from Lolita Through Eyes Wide Shut</i>. <a href="/wiki/McFarland_%26_Company" title="McFarland &amp; Company">McFarland &amp; Company</a>. pp. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=Bz_x37RSSqIC&amp;pg=PA298" rel="nofollow">298</a> (note 2.23). <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-7864-5916-2" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-7864-5916-2"><bdi>978-0-7864-5916-2</bdi></a>. <q><i>Spartacus</i> cost $12 million and grossed some $60 million at the box office, figures Kubrick rarely again matched.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Love+and+Death+in+Kubrick%3A+A+Critical+Study+of+the+Films+from+Lolita+Through+Eyes+Wide+Shut&amp;rft.pages=298+%28note+2.23%29&amp;rft.pub=McFarland+%26+Company&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-7864-5916-2&amp;rft.aulast=Webster&amp;rft.aufirst=Patrick&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-HallNeale179-247"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale179_247-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale179_247-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">Hall &amp; Neale 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA179" rel="nofollow">179</a>.
<ul><li><i>Spartacus</i>: "In the case of <i>Spartacus</i>, overseas earnings to 1969 amounted to $12,462,044, while U.S. and Canadian rentals (even including a million-dollar TV sale) were only $10,643,181. But the film failed to show a profit on production costs of $10,284,014 because of the distribution charges and expenses amounting to an additional $15,308,083."</li>
<li><i>The Bible</i>: "<i>The Bible—In the Beginning...</i> (1966) was financed by the Italian producer Dino De Laurentiis from private investors and Swiss banks. He then sold distribution rights outside Italy jointly to Fox and Seven Arts for $15 million (70 percent of which came from Fox), thereby recouping the bulk of his $18 million investment. Although <i>The Bible</i> returned a respectable world rental of $25.3 million, Fox was still left with a net loss of just over $1.5 million. It was the last biblical epic to be released by any major Hollywood studio for nearly twenty years."</li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-248"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-248">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1" id="CITEREFNixon">Nixon, Rob. <a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.com/tcmdb/title/24944/Psycho/articles.html" rel="nofollow">"Psycho (1960) – Articles"</a>. <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">Turner Classic Movies</a><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">January 9,</span> 2012</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=Psycho+%281960%29+%E2%80%93+Articles&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft.aulast=Nixon&amp;rft.aufirst=Rob&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F24944%2FPsycho%2Farticles.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-249"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-249">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1" id="CITEREFTube.1961">Tube. (January 18, 1961). <a class="external text" href="https://www.variety.com/review/VE1117793715" rel="nofollow">"One Hundred and One Dalmatians"</a>. <i><a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Daily_Variety" title="Daily Variety">Daily Variety</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">August 23,</span> 2011</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Daily+Variety&amp;rft.atitle=One+Hundred+and+One+Dalmatians&amp;rft.date=1961-01-18&amp;rft.au=Tube.&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.variety.com%2Freview%2FVE1117793715&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-250"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-250">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFBlock2010">Block, Hayley Taylor (2010), <i>West Side Story</i>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA449" rel="nofollow">449</a>, <q>With its three rereleases, it took in over $105 million in worldwide box office ($720 million in 2005 dollars).</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=West+Side+Story&amp;rft.pages=449&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Block&amp;rft.aufirst=Hayley+Taylor&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span> In: <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>.</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-BlockWilson434-251"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson434_251-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson434_251-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson434_251-2"><sup><i><b>c</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson434_251-3"><sup><i><b>d</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson434_251-4"><sup><i><b>e</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson434_251-5"><sup><i><b>f</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson434_251-6"><sup><i><b>g</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA434" rel="nofollow">434</a>.
<ul><li><i>The Sound of Music</i>: "Domestic Rentals: $68.4; Foreign Rentals: $46.2; Production Cost: $8.0 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."</li>
<li><i>The Dirty Dozen</i>: "Domestic Rentals: $20.1; Foreign Rentals: $11.2; Production Cost: $5.4 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."</li>
<li><i>2001: A Space Odyssey</i>: "Domestic Rentals: $16.4; Foreign Rentals: $5.5; Production Cost: $10.3 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."</li>
<li><i>Cleopatra</i>: "Domestic Rentals: $22.1; Foreign Rentals: $18.2; Production Cost: $44.0 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."</li>
<li><i>West Side Story</i>: "Domestic Rentals: $16.2; Foreign Rentals: $15.6; Production Cost: $7.0 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."</li>
<li><i>The Longest Day</i>: "Domestic Rentals: $13.9; Foreign Rentals: $19.3; Production Cost: $8.6 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."</li>
<li><i>Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid</i>: "Domestic Rentals: $29.2; Foreign Rentals: $7.9; Production Cost: $6.6 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."</li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-252"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-252">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><i>Lawrence of Arabia</i>
<ul><li>1962 release: <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.the-numbers.com/movies/1962/0LWRN.php" rel="nofollow">"Lawrence of Arabia"</a>. <i>The Numbers</i>. Nash Information Services. LLC<span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">August 15,</span> 2011</span>. <q>Worldwide Box Office: $69,995,385; International Box Office: $32,500,000</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Numbers&amp;rft.atitle=Lawrence+of+Arabia&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.the-numbers.com%2Fmovies%2F1962%2F0LWRN.php&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li>
<li>U.S. total (including reissues): <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://pro.boxoffice.com/movie/5851/lawrence-of-arabia" rel="nofollow">"Lawrence of Arabia"</a>. <i><a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Boxoffice_(magazine)" title="Boxoffice (magazine)">Boxoffice</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">May 29,</span> 2016</span>. <q>$44,824,852</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Boxoffice&amp;rft.atitle=Lawrence+of+Arabia&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fpro.boxoffice.com%2Fmovie%2F5851%2Flawrence-of-arabia&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-HallNeale165-253"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale165_253-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale165_253-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale165_253-2"><sup><i><b>c</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">Hall &amp; Neale 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA165" rel="nofollow">165</a>–<a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA166" rel="nofollow">166</a>
<ul><li><i>Lawrence of Arabia</i>: Columbia released the $13.8 million <i>Lawrence of Arabia</i> (1962), filmed in Super Panavision 70, exclusively on a hard-ticket basis, but opened <i>Barabbas</i> (1962), <i>The Cardinal</i> (1963), and the $12 million Joseph Conrad adaptation <i>Lord Jim</i> (1965) as 70 mm roadshows in selected territories only."</li>
<li><i>The Longest Day</i>: "Darryl's most ambitious independent production was <i>The Longest Day</i> (1962), a three-hour reconstruction of D-Day filmed in black-and-white CinemaScope at a cost of $8 million. It grossed over $30 million worldwide as a roadshow followed by general release, thereby helping the studio regain stability during its period of reorganization."</li>
<li><i>Cleopatra</i>: "With top tickets set at an all-time high of $5.50,<i>Cleopatra</i> had amassed as much as $20 million in such guarantees from exhibitors even before its premiere. Fox claimed the film had cost in total $44 million, of which $31,115,000 represented the direct negative cost and the rest distribution, print and advertising expenses. (These figures excluded the more than $5 million spent on the production's abortive British shoot in 1960–61, prior to its relocation to Italy.) By 1966 worldwide rentals had reached $38,042,000 including $23.5 million from the United States."</li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-HallNeale164-254"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale164_254-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">Hall &amp; Neale 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA164" rel="nofollow">164</a>. "<i>West</i> cost $14,483,000; although it earned $35 million worldwide in just under three years, with ultimate domestic rentals totaling $20,932,883, high distribution costs severely limited its profitability."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Bond_grosses-255"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-Bond_grosses_255-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Bond_grosses_255-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Bond_grosses_255-2"><sup><i><b>c</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Bond_grosses_255-3"><sup><i><b>d</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, pp. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA428#v=twopage" rel="nofollow">428–429</a>
<ul><li><i>From Russia With Love</i>: "Worldwide Box Office: 78.9; Production Cost: 2.0 (in millions of $s)"</li>
<li><i>Goldfinger</i>: "Worldwide Box Office: 124.9; Production Cost: 3.0 (in millions of $s)"</li>
<li><i>Diamonds Are Forever</i>: "Worldwide Box Office: 116; Production Cost: 7.2 (in millions of $s)"</li>
<li><i>Moonraker</i>: "Worldwide Box Office: 210.3; Production Cost: 34.0 (in millions of $s)"</li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-Chapman_(2007)-256"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-Chapman_(2007)_256-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Chapman_(2007)_256-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFChapman2007">Chapman, James (2007). <i>Licence to thrill: a cultural history of the James Bond films</i>. <a href="/wiki/I.B._Tauris" title="I.B. Tauris">I.B. Tauris</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-84511-515-9" title="Special:BookSources/978-1-84511-515-9"><bdi>978-1-84511-515-9</bdi></a>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Licence+to+thrill%3A+a+cultural+history+of+the+James+Bond+films&amp;rft.pub=I.B.+Tauris&amp;rft.date=2007&amp;rft.isbn=978-1-84511-515-9&amp;rft.aulast=Chapman&amp;rft.aufirst=James&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span>
<ul><li><i>From Russia With Love</i>: "The American release of <i>From Russia With Love</i> again followed on some six months after it had been shown in Britain. North American rentals of $9.9 million were an improvement on its predecessor, helped by a slightly wider release, though they were still only half the $19.5 million of foreign rentals... (<a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=E8wqAQAAIAAJ&amp;q=%22the+american+release+of+from+russia+with+love%22" rel="nofollow">Online copy</a> at <a href="/wiki/Google_Books" title="Google Books">Google Books</a>)"</li>
<li><i>Diamonds Are Forever</i>: "<i>Diamonds Are Forever</i> marked a return to the box-office heights of the Bond films of the mid-1960s. Its worldwide rentals were $45.7 million..."<sup class="noprint Inline-Template" style="white-space:nowrap;">[<i><a href="/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources" title="Wikipedia:Citing sources"><span title="This citation requires a reference to the specific page or range of pages in which the material appears. (November 2018)">page needed</span></a></i>]</sup></li>
<li><i>Moonraker</i>: "These figures were surpassed by <i>Moonraker</i>, which earned total worldwide rentals of $87.7 million, of which $33 million came from North America. (<a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=E8wqAQAAIAAJ&amp;q=$87.7+million" rel="nofollow">Online copy</a> at <a href="/wiki/Google_Books" title="Google Books">Google Books</a>)"</li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-Balio_(2009)-257"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-Balio_(2009)_257-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Balio_(2009)_257-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFBalio2009">Balio, Tino (2009). <i>United Artists, Volume 2, 1951–1978: the Company that Changed the Film Industry</i>. <a href="/wiki/University_of_Wisconsin_Press" title="University of Wisconsin Press">University of Wisconsin Press</a>. p. 261. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-299-23014-2" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-299-23014-2"><bdi>978-0-299-23014-2</bdi></a>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=United+Artists%2C+Volume+2%2C+1951%E2%80%931978%3A+the+Company+that+Changed+the+Film+Industry&amp;rft.pages=261&amp;rft.pub=University+of+Wisconsin+Press&amp;rft.date=2009&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-299-23014-2&amp;rft.aulast=Balio&amp;rft.aufirst=Tino&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span>
<ul><li><i>From Russia With Love</i>: "The picture grossed twice as much as <i>Dr. No</i>, both domestic and foreign—$12.5 million worldwide (<a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=KKoLAQAAMAAJ&amp;q=%22%2412.5+million+worldwide%22" rel="nofollow">Online copy</a> at <a href="/wiki/Google_Books" title="Google Books">Google Books</a>)"</li>
<li><i>Goldfinger</i>: "Produced on a budget of around $3 million, <i>Goldfinger</i> grossed a phenomenal $46 million worldwide the first time around. (<a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=KKoLAQAAMAAJ&amp;q=Goldfinger+%22$46+million+worldwide%22" rel="nofollow">Online copy</a> at <a href="/wiki/Google_Books" title="Google Books">Google Books</a>)"</li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-HallNeale184-258"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale184_258-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale184_258-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">Hall &amp; Neale 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA184" rel="nofollow">184</a>
<ul><li><i>My Fair Lady</i>: "<i>My Fair Lady</i> (1964) cost Warners $17 million to make, including a record $5.5 million just for the film rights to the Alan Jay Lerner and Frederick Loewe stage show and a million-dollar fee for star Audrey Hepburn. By 1967 it was reported to have grossed $55 million from roadshowing worldwide."</li>
<li><i>Mary Poppins</i>: "<i>Mary Poppins</i> (1964), which cost $5.2 million, was neither a stage adaptation nor a roadshow. But by the end of its first release, it had grossed nearly $50 million worldwide."</li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-259"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-259">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFBurns2010">Burns, Douglas (2010), <i>Mary Poppins</i>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA469" rel="nofollow">469</a>, <q>In its initial run, <i>Poppins</i> garnered an astounding $44 million in worldwide rentals and became the company's first Best Picture Oscar contender.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Mary+Poppins&amp;rft.pages=469&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Burns&amp;rft.aufirst=Douglas&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span> In: <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>.</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-260"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-260">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://pro.boxoffice.com/movie/9495/the-sound-of-music" rel="nofollow">"The Sound of Music"</a>. <i><a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Boxoffice_(magazine)" title="Boxoffice (magazine)">Boxoffice</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">May 29,</span> 2016</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Boxoffice&amp;rft.atitle=The+Sound+of+Music&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fpro.boxoffice.com%2Fmovie%2F9495%2Fthe-sound-of-music&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-261"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-261">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFSilverman1988">Silverman, Stephen M (1988). <i>The Fox that got Away: The Last Days of the Zanuck Dynasty at Twentieth Century-Fox</i>. Secaucus, N.J.: L. Stuart. p. <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/foxthatgotawayt00silv/page/325" rel="nofollow">325</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/9780818404856" title="Special:BookSources/9780818404856"><bdi>9780818404856</bdi></a>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+Fox+that+got+Away%3A+The+Last+Days+of+the+Zanuck+Dynasty+at+Twentieth+Century-Fox&amp;rft.place=Secaucus%2C+N.J.&amp;rft.pages=325&amp;rft.pub=L.+Stuart&amp;rft.date=1988&amp;rft.isbn=9780818404856&amp;rft.aulast=Silverman&amp;rft.aufirst=Stephen+M&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-262"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-262">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.the-numbers.com/movies/1966/0HWII.php" rel="nofollow">"Hawaii"</a>. <i>The Numbers</i>. Nash Information Services. LLC<span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">August 18,</span> 2011</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Numbers&amp;rft.atitle=Hawaii&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.the-numbers.com%2Fmovies%2F1966%2F0HWII.php&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-263"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-263">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.the-numbers.com/movie/Whos-Afraid-of-Virginia-Wolf#tab=summary" rel="nofollow">"Who's Afraid of Virginia Wolf? (1966)"</a>. <i>The Numbers</i>. Nash Information Services. LLC<span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">February 3,</span> 2020</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Numbers&amp;rft.atitle=Who%27s+Afraid+of+Virginia+Wolf%3F+%281966%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.the-numbers.com%2Fmovie%2FWhos-Afraid-of-Virginia-Wolf%23tab%3Dsummary&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-HallNeale188-264"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale188_264-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">Hall &amp; Neale 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA188" rel="nofollow">188</a>. "The negative cost of Warners' adaptation of Edward Albee's play <i>Who's Afraid of Virginia Woolf?</i> (1966)—filmed in widescreen and black-and-white, largely set in domestic interiors and with a cast of only four principal actors—amounted to $7,613,000, in part because stars Elizabeth Taylor and Richard Burton received up-front fees of $1 million and $750,000 respectively, against 10 percent of the gross apiece. (Their participation was presumably added to the budget)."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-265"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-265">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1">"Animals Portray Parts in Disney's "Robin Hood<span class="cs1-kern-right">"</span>". <i><a href="/wiki/The_Blade_(Toledo,_Ohio)" title="The Blade (Toledo, Ohio)">Toledo Blade</a></i>. October 18, 1970. <a class="external text" href="https://news.google.com/newspapers?nid=1350&amp;dat=19701018&amp;id=k8hOAAAAIBAJ&amp;sjid=xQEEAAAAIBAJ&amp;pg=2133,463153" rel="nofollow">Sec. G, p. 7</a>. <q>"The Jungle Book," in it's [<i>sic</i>] initial world-wide release, has grossed $23.8 million to date...</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Toledo+Blade&amp;rft.atitle=Animals+Portray+Parts+in+Disney%27s+%22Robin+Hood%22&amp;rft.pages=Sec.+G%2C+p.+7&amp;rft.date=1970-10-18&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-266"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-266">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://variety.com/1966/film/reviews/the-jungle-book-1200421257/" rel="nofollow">"The Jungle Book"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/Variety_(magazine)" title="Variety (magazine)">Variety</a></i>. December 31, 1966<span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">March 14,</span> 2018</span>. <q>It was filmed at a declared cost of $4 million over a 42-month period.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Variety&amp;rft.atitle=The+Jungle+Book&amp;rft.date=1966-12-31&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fvariety.com%2F1966%2Ffilm%2Freviews%2Fthe-jungle-book-1200421257%2F&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Denisoff_&amp;_Romanowski_(1991)-267"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-Denisoff_&amp;_Romanowski_(1991)_267-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Denisoff_&amp;_Romanowski_(1991)_267-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFDenisoffRomanowski1991">Denisoff, R. Serge; Romanowski, William D. (1991). <i>Risky Business: Rock in Film</i>. Transaction Publishers. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-88738-843-9" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-88738-843-9"><bdi>978-0-88738-843-9</bdi></a>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Risky+Business%3A+Rock+in+Film&amp;rft.pub=Transaction+Publishers&amp;rft.date=1991&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-88738-843-9&amp;rft.aulast=Denisoff&amp;rft.aufirst=R.+Serge&amp;rft.au=Romanowski%2C+William+D.&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span>
<ul><li><i>The Graduate</i>: p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=kT0fKUCTUC4C&amp;pg=PA167" rel="nofollow">167</a>. "World net rental was estimated at more than $85 million by January 1971."</li>
<li><i>Grease</i>: p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=kT0fKUCTUC4C&amp;pg=PA236" rel="nofollow">236</a>. "The film was produced for $6 million and Paramount reportedly spent another $3 million on promotion."</li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-HallNeale191-268"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale191_268-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale191_268-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">Hall &amp; Neale 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA191" rel="nofollow">191</a>–192
<ul><li><i>The Graduate</i>: "<i>The Graduate</i> eventually earned U.S. rentals of $44,090,729 on a production cost of $3.1 million to become the most lucrative non-roadshow picture (and independent release) to date."</li>
<li><i>Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid</i>: "None of these films was roadshown in the United States; most were set in contemporary America or had a contemporary "take" on the past (the casting of genuine teenagers to play Romeo and Juliet, the urbane sophistication of the dialogue in <i>Butch Cassidy</i>, the antiauthoritarianism of <i>Bonnie and Clyde</i> and <i>MASH</i>); most were produced on modest or medium-sized budgets (as low as $450,000 for <i>Easy Rider</i> and no higher than $6,825,000 for <i>Butch Cassidy</i>); and all grossed upward of $10 million domestically."</li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-269"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-269">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><i>2001: A Space Odyssey</i>
<ul><li>Total: <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1" id="CITEREFMiller">Miller, Frank. <a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.turner.com/tcmdb/title/628/2001-A-Space-Odyssey/articles.html" rel="nofollow">"2001: A Space Odyssey (1968) – Articles"</a>. <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">Turner Classic Movies</a><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">August 24,</span> 2011</span>. <q>2001: A Space Odyssey made $15 million on its initial U.S. release, and currently shows a worldwide gross of over $190 million.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=2001%3A+A+Space+Odyssey+%281968%29+%E2%80%93+Articles&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft.aulast=Miller&amp;rft.aufirst=Frank&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.turner.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F628%2F2001-A-Space-Odyssey%2Farticles.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li>
<li>As of 2006: <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFPalmer2006">Palmer, R. Barton (2006). "2001: The Critical Reception and the Generation Gap".  In Kolker, Robert Phillip (ed.). <i>Stanley Kubrick's 2001: a Space Odyssey: New Essays</i>. <a href="/wiki/Oxford_University_Press" title="Oxford University Press">Oxford University Press</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=YpritcZXPFoC&amp;pg=PA16" rel="nofollow">16</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-19-517452-6" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-19-517452-6"><bdi>978-0-19-517452-6</bdi></a>. <q>With its initial and subsequent releases, domestic and worldwide, Kubrick's arty, intellectual film earned nearly $138 million, which was, at that time, an astounding figure.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=bookitem&amp;rft.atitle=2001%3A+The+Critical+Reception+and+the+Generation+Gap&amp;rft.btitle=Stanley+Kubrick%27s+2001%3A+a+Space+Odyssey%3A+New+Essays&amp;rft.pages=16&amp;rft.pub=Oxford+University+Press&amp;rft.date=2006&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-19-517452-6&amp;rft.aulast=Palmer&amp;rft.aufirst=R.+Barton&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li>
<li>2013 re-release: <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/movies/?page=intl&amp;id=20012013.htm" rel="nofollow">"2001: A Space Odyssey (2013 re-release)"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/Box_Office_Mojo" title="Box Office Mojo">Box Office Mojo</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">September 26,</span> 2018</span>. <q>Domestic Total Gross: $214,618; United Kingdom: $568,997</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=2001%3A+A+Space+Odyssey+%282013+re-release%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Fmovies%2F%3Fpage%3Dintl%26id%3D20012013.htm&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li>
<li>2014 re-release: <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/movies/?id=20012014.htm" rel="nofollow">"2001: A Space Odyssey (2014 re-release)"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/Box_Office_Mojo" title="Box Office Mojo">Box Office Mojo</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">September 26,</span> 2018</span>. <q>Domestic Total Gross: $135,370</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=2001%3A+A+Space+Odyssey+%282014+re-release%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Fmovies%2F%3Fid%3D20012014.htm&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li>
<li>2018 re-release: <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/movies/?page=intl&amp;id=20012018.htm" rel="nofollow">"2001: A Space Odyssey (2018 re-release)"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/Box_Office_Mojo" title="Box Office Mojo">Box Office Mojo</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">September 26,</span> 2018</span>. <q>Domestic Total Gross: $1,283,820; Australia: $192,457; Greece: $27,510; Netherlands: $159,068; New Zealand: $5,046; Russia: $155,841; United Kingdom: $296,525</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=2001%3A+A+Space+Odyssey+%282018+re-release%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Fmovies%2F%3Fpage%3Dintl%26id%3D20012018.htm&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-270"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-270">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFHaber1969">Haber, Joyces (March 27, 1969). <a class="external text" href="https://www.google.com/search?hl=en&amp;tbm=nws&amp;q=%22funny+girl%22+%22%2480+to+%24100%22+million+worldwide" rel="nofollow">"<span class="cs1-kern-left">'</span>Funny Girl' a Box Office Winner"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/Los_Angeles_Times" title="Los Angeles Times">Los Angeles Times</a></i>. Archived from <a class="external text" href="https://pqasb.pqarchiver.com/latimes/access/672252782.html?dids=672252782:672252782&amp;FMT=ABS&amp;FMTS=ABS:AI" rel="nofollow">the original</a> on March 29, 2012<span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">March 29,</span> 2012</span>. <q>..."Funny Girl" will gross an estimated $80 to $100 million worldwide.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Los+Angeles+Times&amp;rft.atitle=%27Funny+Girl%27+a+Box+Office+Winner&amp;rft.date=1969-03-27&amp;rft.aulast=Haber&amp;rft.aufirst=Joyces&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fpqasb.pqarchiver.com%2Flatimes%2Faccess%2F672252782.html%3Fdids%3D672252782%3A672252782%26FMT%3DABS%26FMTS%3DABS%3AAI&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-271"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-271">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFWelles1970">Welles, Chris (September 7, 1970). "Behind the Silence at Columbia Pictures—No Moguls, No Minions, Just Profits". <i><a href="/wiki/New_York_(magazine)" title="New York (magazine)">New York</a></i>. <b>3</b> (36). New York Media. pp. 42–<a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=8-ICAAAAMBAJ&amp;pg=PA47" rel="nofollow">47</a>. <q>While Columbia, battling Ray Stark over every dollar, did <i>Funny Girl</i> for around $8.8 million, a million or so over budget, Fox spent nearly $24 million on <i>Hello, Dolly!</i>, more than twice the initial budget, and the film will thus have to gross three times as much to break even.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=New+York&amp;rft.atitle=Behind+the+Silence+at+Columbia+Pictures%E2%80%94No+Moguls%2C+No+Minions%2C+Just+Profits&amp;rft.volume=3&amp;rft.issue=36&amp;rft.pages=42-47&amp;rft.date=1970-09-07&amp;rft.aulast=Welles&amp;rft.aufirst=Chris&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-272"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-272">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><i>Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid</i>
<ul><li>United States and Canada: <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://pro.boxoffice.com/movie/1631/butch-cassidy-and-the-sundance-kid" rel="nofollow">"Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid"</a>. <i><a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Boxoffice_(magazine)" title="Boxoffice (magazine)">Boxoffice</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">May 29,</span> 2016</span>. <q>$102,308,525</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Boxoffice&amp;rft.atitle=Butch+Cassidy+and+the+Sundance+Kid&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fpro.boxoffice.com%2Fmovie%2F1631%2Fbutch-cassidy-and-the-sundance-kid&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li>
<li>Outside North America: <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1"><a href="/wiki/Vanity_Fair_(magazine)" title="Vanity Fair (magazine)"><i>Vanity Fair</i></a>. 2008. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=VloTAQAAMAAJ&amp;q=%22by+the+spring+of+1970+it+had+taken+in+$46+million+in+North+America+and+grossed+another+$50+million+abroad%22&amp;dq=%22by+the+spring+of+1970+it+had+taken+in+$46+million+in+North+America+and+grossed+another+$50+million+abroad%22" rel="nofollow">388</a>. <q><i>Butch Cassidy</i> went on to be a huge hit—by the spring of 1970 it had taken in $46 million in North America and grossed another $50 million abroad.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Vanity+Fair&amp;rft.pages=388&amp;rft.date=2008&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-273"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-273">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1">"<span class="cs1-kern-left">'</span>Love Story' II: Ryan Redux?". <i><a href="/wiki/New_York_(magazine)" title="New York (magazine)">New York</a></i>. <b>9</b>. New York Media. 1976. p. 389. <q>Bring those handkerchiefs out of retirement. ... After all, the first movie made around $80 million worldwide.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=New+York&amp;rft.atitle=%27Love+Story%27+II%3A+Ryan+Redux%3F&amp;rft.volume=9&amp;rft.pages=389&amp;rft.date=1976&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-274"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-274">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFBlock2010">Block, Hayley Taylor (2010), <i>Love Story</i>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA545" rel="nofollow">545</a>, <q>The final cost came in at $2,260,000.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Love+Story&amp;rft.pages=545&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Block&amp;rft.aufirst=Hayley+Taylor&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span> In: <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>.</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-275"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-275">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFScott1979">Scott, Vernon (June 30, 1979). "<span class="cs1-kern-left">"</span>Airports" Flourish". <i><a href="/wiki/The_Bryan_Times" title="The Bryan Times">The Bryan Times</a></i>. <a href="/wiki/United_Press_International" title="United Press International">United Press International</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://news.google.com/newspapers?id=_L8wAAAAIBAJ&amp;sjid=x1EDAAAAIBAJ&amp;pg=5730,9236104" rel="nofollow">10</a>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Bryan+Times&amp;rft.atitle=%22Airports%22+Flourish&amp;rft.pages=10&amp;rft.date=1979-06-30&amp;rft.aulast=Scott&amp;rft.aufirst=Vernon&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-276"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-276">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA541" rel="nofollow">541</a>. "Screenwriter and director George Seaton was given a then-whopping production budget of $10 million to make what would be his last big movie after a long career as an actor in radio, a screenwriter, and a director."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-277"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-277">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA549" rel="nofollow">549</a>. "Fiddler had the highest domestic box office of 1971 (it was second in worldwide box office after <i>Diamonds Are Forever</i>), with more than $100 million in unadjusted worldwide box office on its initial release. The soundtrack album was also a huge seller. The 1979 rerelease was not as successful, with the $3.8 million print and ad costs almost as high as the $4.3 million in worldwide rentals."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-BlockWilson527-278"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson527_278-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson527_278-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson527_278-2"><sup><i><b>c</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson527_278-3"><sup><i><b>d</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson527_278-4"><sup><i><b>e</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson527_278-5"><sup><i><b>f</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA527" rel="nofollow">527</a>.
<ul><li><i>Star Wars</i>: "Domestic Rentals: $127.0; Foreign Rentals: $141.5; Production Cost: $13.0 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."</li>
<li><i>The Godfather</i>: "Domestic Rentals: $85.6; Foreign Rentals: $42.0; Production Cost: $7.2 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."</li>
<li><i>Fiddler on the Roof</i>: "Domestic Rentals: $34.0; Foreign Rentals: $11.1; Production Cost: $9.0 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."</li>
<li><i>Rocky</i>: "Domestic Rentals: $56.0; Foreign Rentals: $21.1; Production Cost: $1.6 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."</li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-279"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-279">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><i>The Godfather</i>
<ul><li>1974: <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1"><a href="/wiki/Newsweek" title="Newsweek"><i>Newsweek</i></a>. <b>84</b>. 1974. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=sCXkAAAAMAAJ&amp;q=%22%24285+million%22" rel="nofollow">74</a>. <q>The original <i>Godfather</i> has grossed a mind-boggling $285 million...</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Newsweek&amp;rft.pages=74&amp;rft.date=1974&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li>
<li>1991: <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFVon_Gunden1991">Von Gunden, Kenneth (1991). <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/postmodernauteur0000vong/page/36" rel="nofollow"><i>Postmodern auteurs: Coppola, Lucas, De Palma, Spielberg, and Scorsese</i></a>. <a href="/wiki/McFarland_%26_Company" title="McFarland &amp; Company">McFarland &amp; Company</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/postmodernauteur0000vong/page/36" rel="nofollow">36</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-89950-618-0" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-89950-618-0"><bdi>978-0-89950-618-0</bdi></a>. <q>Since <i>The Godfather</i> had earned over $85 million in U.S.-Canada rentals (the worldwide box-office gross was $285 million), a sequel, according to the usual formula, could be expected to earn approximately two-thirds of the original's box-office take (ultimately <i>Godfather II</i> had rentals of $30 million).</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Postmodern+auteurs%3A+Coppola%2C+Lucas%2C+De+Palma%2C+Spielberg%2C+and+Scorsese&amp;rft.pages=36&amp;rft.pub=McFarland+%26+Company&amp;rft.date=1991&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-89950-618-0&amp;rft.aulast=Von+Gunden&amp;rft.aufirst=Kenneth&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Farchive.org%2Fdetails%2Fpostmodernauteur0000vong%2Fpage%2F36&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li>
<li>Releases: <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/title/tt0068646/" rel="nofollow">"The Godfather (1972)"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/Box_Office_Mojo" title="Box Office Mojo">Box Office Mojo</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">January 22,</span> 2020</span>. <q>Original release: $243,862,778; 1997 re-release: $1,267,490; 2009 re-release: $121,323; 2011 re-release: $818,333; 2014 re-release: $29,349; 2018 re-release: $21,701; Budget: $6,000,000</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=The+Godfather+%281972%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Ftitle%2Ftt0068646%2F&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-280"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-280">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFJacobs1980">Jacobs, Diane (1980). <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/hollywoodrenaiss00jaco/page/115" rel="nofollow"><i>Hollywood Renaissance</i></a>. <a href="/wiki/Dell_Publishing" title="Dell Publishing">Dell Publishing</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=KP0HAQAAMAAJ&amp;q=%22million+in+worldwide+sales%22+godfather" rel="nofollow">115</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-440-53382-5" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-440-53382-5"><bdi>978-0-440-53382-5</bdi></a>. <q>The Godfather catapulted Coppola to overnight celebrity, earning three Academy Awards and a then record-breaking $142 million in worldwide sales.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Hollywood+Renaissance&amp;rft.pages=115&amp;rft.pub=Dell+Publishing&amp;rft.date=1980&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-440-53382-5&amp;rft.aulast=Jacobs&amp;rft.aufirst=Diane&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Farchive.org%2Fdetails%2Fhollywoodrenaiss00jaco%2Fpage%2F115&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-godfather-tcm-281"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-godfather-tcm_281-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://www.tcm.turner.com/tcmdb/title/443184/The-Godfather/notes.html" rel="nofollow">"The Godfather (1972) – Notes"</a>. <a href="/wiki/Turner_Classic_Movies" title="Turner Classic Movies">Turner Classic Movies</a><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">January 23,</span> 2020</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.btitle=The+Godfather+%281972%29+%E2%80%93+Notes&amp;rft.pub=Turner+Classic+Movies&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.tcm.turner.com%2Ftcmdb%2Ftitle%2F443184%2FThe-Godfather%2Fnotes.html&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-282"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-282">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://pro.boxoffice.com/movie/3253/the-exorcist" rel="nofollow">"The Exorcist"</a>. <i><a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Boxoffice_(magazine)" title="Boxoffice (magazine)">Boxoffice</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">May 29,</span> 2016</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Boxoffice&amp;rft.atitle=The+Exorcist&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fpro.boxoffice.com%2Fmovie%2F3253%2Fthe-exorcist&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-283"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-283">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFStanleySteinberg1976">Stanley, Robert Henry; Steinberg, Charles Side (1976). <i>The media environment: mass communications in American society</i>. Hastings House. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=Z30tAAAAMAAJ&amp;q=%22the+sting%22+million+worldwide+exorcist&amp;dq=%22" rel="nofollow">76</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-8038-4681-4" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-8038-4681-4"><bdi>978-0-8038-4681-4</bdi></a>. <q>...further reflected by the phenomenal successes of <i>The Sting</i>, <i>Chinatown</i> and <i>The Exorcist</i>. The latter film, which cost about $10 million to produce, has grossed over $110 million worldwide.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=The+media+environment%3A+mass+communications+in+American+society&amp;rft.pages=76&amp;rft.pub=Hastings+House&amp;rft.date=1976&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-8038-4681-4&amp;rft.aulast=Stanley&amp;rft.aufirst=Robert+Henry&amp;rft.au=Steinberg%2C+Charles+Side&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-284"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-284">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation magazine cs1">"Big Rental Films of 1974". <i><a href="/wiki/Variety_(magazine)" title="Variety (magazine)">Variety</a></i>. January 8, 1975. p. 24. <q>U.S-Canada market only $66,3000,000</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Variety&amp;rft.atitle=Big+Rental+Films+of+1974&amp;rft.pages=24&amp;rft.date=1975-01-08&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-WBos-285"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-WBos_285-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-WBos_285-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation magazine cs1" id="CITEREFPollock1979">Pollock, Dale (May 9, 1979). "WB Adds To Its Record Collection". <i><a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Daily_Variety" title="Daily Variety">Daily Variety</a></i>. p. 1. <q>"Towering Inferno" did $56,000,000 overseas in billings while "The Exorcist" toted up $46,000,000</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Daily+Variety&amp;rft.atitle=WB+Adds+To+Its+Record+Collection&amp;rft.pages=1&amp;rft.date=1979-05-09&amp;rft.aulast=Pollock&amp;rft.aufirst=Dale&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-286"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-286">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation cs2"><a href="/wiki/New_York_(magazine)" title="New York (magazine)"><i>New York</i></a>, <b>8</b>, New York Media, 1975, <q>...<i>Jaws</i> should outstrip another MCA hit, <i>The Sting</i>, which had world-wide revenues of $115 million. (<a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jW8pAQAAIAAJ&amp;q=%22The+Sting%2C+which+had+world-wide%22+revenues" rel="nofollow">Online copy</a> at <a href="/wiki/Google_Books" title="Google Books">Google Books</a>)</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=New+York&amp;rft.pub=New+York+Media&amp;rft.date=1975&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-BlockWilson560-287"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson560_287-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA560" rel="nofollow">560</a>. "Production Cost: $5.5 (Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-288"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-288">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation magazine cs1"><span class="cs1-lock-subscription" title="Paid subscription required"><a class="external text" href="https://varietyultimate.com/archive/issue/WV-06-02-1976-8" rel="nofollow">"It Towers $203,336,412 (advertisement)"</a></span>. <i><a href="/wiki/Variety_(magazine)" title="Variety (magazine)">Variety</a></i>. June 2, 1976. pp. 8–9<span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">October 31,</span> 2020</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Variety&amp;rft.atitle=It+Towers+%24203%2C336%2C412+%28advertisement%29&amp;rft.pages=8-9&amp;rft.date=1976-06-02&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fvarietyultimate.com%2Farchive%2Fissue%2FWV-06-02-1976-8&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-289"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-289">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">Hall &amp; Neale 2010</a>, pp. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=Ro0hASPfC68C&amp;pg=PA354" rel="nofollow">206</a>–208. "The most successful entry in the disaster cycle was the $15 million <i>The Towering Inferno</i> which earned over $48,650,000 in domestic rentals and about $40 million foreign."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-290"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-290">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFKlady1998">Klady, Leonard (1998). <a class="external text" href="https://web.archive.org/web/19991007042514/http://www.variety.com/numbers/video.asp" rel="nofollow">"All-Time Top Film Rentals"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/Variety_(magazine)" title="Variety (magazine)">Variety</a></i>. Archived from <a class="external text" href="https://www.variety.com:80/numbers/video.asp" rel="nofollow">the original</a> on October 7, 1999. <q>Domestic rentals: $48,838,000</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Variety&amp;rft.atitle=All-Time+Top+Film+Rentals&amp;rft.date=1998&amp;rft.aulast=Klady&amp;rft.aufirst=Leonard&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.variety.com%3A80%2Fnumbers%2Fvideo.asp&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-BlockWilson568-291"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson568_291-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, p. 568. "Production Cost: $14.3 (Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-292"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-292">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://pro.boxoffice.com/movie/5281/jaws" rel="nofollow">"Jaws"</a>. <i><a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Boxoffice_(magazine)" title="Boxoffice (magazine)">Boxoffice</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">May 29,</span> 2016</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Boxoffice&amp;rft.atitle=Jaws&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fpro.boxoffice.com%2Fmovie%2F5281%2Fjaws&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-jaws-293"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-jaws_293-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-jaws_293-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFKilday1977">Kilday, Gregg (July 5, 1977). "Director of 'Jaws II' Abandons His 'Ship<span class="cs1-kern-right">'</span>". <i><a href="/wiki/The_Victoria_Advocate" title="The Victoria Advocate">The Victoria Advocate</a></i>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://news.google.com/newspapers?id=xy4PAAAAIBAJ&amp;sjid=WYUDAAAAIBAJ&amp;pg=4243,812320" rel="nofollow">6B</a>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Victoria+Advocate&amp;rft.atitle=Director+of+%27Jaws+II%27+Abandons+His+%27Ship%27&amp;rft.pages=6B&amp;rft.date=1977-07-05&amp;rft.aulast=Kilday&amp;rft.aufirst=Gregg&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-294"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-294">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFPriggé2004">Priggé, Steven (2004). <i>Movie Moguls Speak: Interviews With Top Film Producers</i>. <a href="/wiki/McFarland_%26_Company" title="McFarland &amp; Company">McFarland &amp; Company</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=40UTI-uUHpwC&amp;pg=PA8" rel="nofollow">8</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-7864-1929-6" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-7864-1929-6"><bdi>978-0-7864-1929-6</bdi></a>. <q>The budget for the first <i>Jaws</i> was $4 million and the picture wound up costing $9 million.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Movie+Moguls+Speak%3A+Interviews+With+Top+Film+Producers&amp;rft.pages=8&amp;rft.pub=McFarland+%26+Company&amp;rft.date=2004&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-7864-1929-6&amp;rft.aulast=Prigg%C3%A9&amp;rft.aufirst=Steven&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-HallNeale214-295"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale214_295-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">Hall &amp; Neale 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA214" rel="nofollow">214</a>. "<i>Rocky</i> was the "sleeper of the decade". Produced by UA and costing just under $1 million, it went on to earn a box-office gross of $117,235,247 in the United States and $225 million worldwide."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-BlockWilson583-296"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson583_296-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation cs2" id="CITEREFBlock2010">Block, Alex Ben (2010), <i>Rocky</i>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA583" rel="nofollow">583</a>, <q>The budget was $1,075,000 plus producer's fees of $100,000.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Rocky&amp;rft.pages=583&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.aulast=Block&amp;rft.aufirst=Alex+Ben&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span> In: <a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>.</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-star-wars-se-297"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-star-wars-se_297-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/movies/?id=starwars4.htm" rel="nofollow">"Star Wars (1977)"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/Box_Office_Mojo" title="Box Office Mojo">Box Office Mojo</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">January 10,</span> 2012</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=Star+Wars+%281977%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Fmovies%2F%3Fid%3Dstarwars4.htm&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-et_first-run-298"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-et_first-run_298-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-et_first-run_298-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-et_first-run_298-2"><sup><i><b>c</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-et_first-run_298-3"><sup><i><b>d</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFWuntch1985">Wuntch, Philip (July 19, 1985). <span class="cs1-lock-subscription" title="Paid subscription required"><a class="external text" href="http://nl.newsbank.com/nl-search/we/Archives?p_product=DM&amp;p_text_direct-0=0ED3CD81CFEA1C2E&amp;p_field_direct-0=document_id" rel="nofollow">"Return of E.T."</a></span>. <i><a href="/wiki/The_Dallas_Morning_News" title="The Dallas Morning News">The Dallas Morning News</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">March 6,</span> 2012</span>. <q>Its worldwide box-office gross was $619 million, toppling the record of $530 million set by Star Wars.</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=The+Dallas+Morning+News&amp;rft.atitle=Return+of+E.T.&amp;rft.date=1985-07-19&amp;rft.aulast=Wuntch&amp;rft.aufirst=Philip&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fnl.newsbank.com%2Fnl-search%2Fwe%2FArchives%3Fp_product%3DDM%26p_text_direct-0%3D0ED3CD81CFEA1C2E%26p_field_direct-0%3Ddocument_id&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-HallNeale218-299"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-HallNeale218_299-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFHallNeale2010">Hall &amp; Neale 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=jhvrSwOOsRgC&amp;pg=PA218" rel="nofollow">218</a>. "Eventually costing $11,293,151, <i>Star Wars</i> was previewed at the Northpoint Theatre in San Francisco on May 1, 1977."</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-300"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-300">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://pro.boxoffice.com/movie/4188/grease" rel="nofollow">"Grease"</a>. <i><a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Boxoffice_(magazine)" title="Boxoffice (magazine)">Boxoffice</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">May 29,</span> 2016</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Boxoffice&amp;rft.atitle=Grease&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fpro.boxoffice.com%2Fmovie%2F4188%2Fgrease&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-301"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-301">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFHofler2010">Hofler, Robert (2010). <i>Party Animals: A Hollywood Tale of Sex, Drugs, and Rock 'N' Roll Starring the Fabulous Allan Carr</i>. ReadHowYouWant.com. p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.co.uk/books?id=iqRezpQzUcAC&amp;pg=PA145" rel="nofollow">145</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-4596-0007-2" title="Special:BookSources/978-1-4596-0007-2"><bdi>978-1-4596-0007-2</bdi></a>. <q>Despite the fact that Grease was well on its way to becoming the highest-grossing movie musical in the world, and eventually grossed over $341 million...</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=Party+Animals%3A+A+Hollywood+Tale+of+Sex%2C+Drugs%2C+and+Rock+%27N%27+Roll+Starring+the+Fabulous+Allan+Carr&amp;rft.pages=145&amp;rft.pub=ReadHowYouWant.com&amp;rft.date=2010&amp;rft.isbn=978-1-4596-0007-2&amp;rft.aulast=Hofler&amp;rft.aufirst=Robert&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-302"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-302">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/movies/intl/?page=&amp;wk=2018W34&amp;id=_fGREASE401" rel="nofollow">"Grease (40th Anniversary)"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/Box_Office_Mojo" title="Box Office Mojo">Box Office Mojo</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">August 30,</span> 2018</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=Grease+%2840th+Anniversary%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Fmovies%2Fintl%2F%3Fpage%3D%26wk%3D2018W34%26id%3D_fGREASE401&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-kramer-303"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-kramer_303-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-kramer_303-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><i>Kramer vs. Kramer</i>
<ul><li><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1"><i>United &amp; Babson Investment Report</i>. <b>72</b>. <a href="/wiki/Babson-United,_Inc." title="Babson-United, Inc.">Babson-United, Inc.</a> 1980. p. 262. <q>Columbia Pictures Industries is continuing to rake in the box office dollars from its Oscar-winning <i>Kramer vs. Kramer</i>, which has topped $100 million in domestic grosses and $70 million overseas. <i>Kramer</i>, which cost less than $8 million to make, is now the second...</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=United+%26+Babson+Investment+Report&amp;rft.pages=262&amp;rft.pub=Babson-United%2C+Inc.&amp;rft.date=1980&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li>
<li><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation book cs1" id="CITEREFPrince2002">Prince, Stephen (2002). <i>A New Pot of Gold: Hollywood Under the Electronic Rainbow, 1980–1989</i>. <a href="/wiki/University_of_California_Press" title="University of California Press">University of California Press</a>. p. <a class="external text" href="https://archive.org/details/newpotofgold00step/page/7" rel="nofollow">7</a>. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/ISBN_(identifier)" title="ISBN (identifier)">ISBN</a> <a href="/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-520-23266-2" title="Special:BookSources/978-0-520-23266-2"><bdi>978-0-520-23266-2</bdi></a>. <q>Much of this was attributable to the performance of its hit film, <i>Kramer vs. Kramer</i> ($94 million worldwide and the number two film in the domestic market).</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Abook&amp;rft.genre=book&amp;rft.btitle=A+New+Pot+of+Gold%3A+Hollywood+Under+the+Electronic+Rainbow%2C+1980%E2%80%931989&amp;rft.pages=7&amp;rft.pub=University+of+California+Press&amp;rft.date=2002&amp;rft.isbn=978-0-520-23266-2&amp;rft.aulast=Prince&amp;rft.aufirst=Stephen&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-304"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-304">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="http://pro.boxoffice.com/movie/8653/rocky-ii" rel="nofollow">"Rocky II"</a>. <i><a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Boxoffice_(magazine)" title="Boxoffice (magazine)">Boxoffice</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">May 29,</span> 2016</span>.</cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Boxoffice&amp;rft.atitle=Rocky+II&amp;rft_id=http%3A%2F%2Fpro.boxoffice.com%2Fmovie%2F8653%2Frocky-ii&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-305"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-305">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation news cs1" id="CITEREFKilday1992">Kilday, Greg (May 22, 1992). <a class="external text" href="https://www.ew.com/ew/article/0,,310543,00.html" rel="nofollow">"Rules of the Game"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/Entertainment_Weekly" title="Entertainment Weekly">Entertainment Weekly</a></i> (119)<span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">January 10,</span> 2012</span>. <q>North America: $67,597,694; Overseas: $57,214,766</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=The+Empire+Strikes+Back+%28Special+Edition%29&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Fmovies%2F%3Fid%3Dstarwars5se.htm&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li>
<li>2015 re-release: <link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/movies/?page=intl&amp;country=00&amp;id=starwars5.htm" rel="nofollow">"The Empire Strikes Back"</a>. <i>Box Office Mojo</i><span class="reference-accessdate">. Retrieved <span class="nowrap">October 8,</span> 2015</span>. <q>United Kingdom: $9,593,937</q></cite><span class="Z3988" title="ctx_ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft_val_fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=unknown&amp;rft.jtitle=Box+Office+Mojo&amp;rft.atitle=The+Empire+Strikes+Back&amp;rft_id=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.boxofficemojo.com%2Fmovies%2F%3Fpage%3Dintl%26country%3D00%26id%3Dstarwars5.htm&amp;rfr_id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3AList+of+highest-grossing+films"></span></li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-BlockWilson519-307"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson519_307-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson519_307-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-BlockWilson519_307-2"><sup><i><b>c</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA519" rel="nofollow">519</a>.

<li><a href="#CITEREFBlockWilson2010">Block &amp; Wilson 2010</a>, p. <a class="external text" href="https://books.google.com/books?id=vpbuSXSSqdkC&amp;pg=PA625" rel="nofollow">625</a>. Domestic Box office: $180.1; "Production Cost: $22.8 (Initial Release – Unadjusted $s in Millions of $s)."</li></ul>
</span></li>
<li id="cite_note-ET-309"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-ET_309-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r999302996" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style"/><cite class="citation web cs1"><a class="external text" href="https://www.boxofficemojo.com/movies/?id=et.htm" rel="nofollow">"E.T.: The Extra-Terrestrial (1982)"</a>. <i><a href="/wiki/Box_Office_Mojo" title="Box Office Mojo">Box Office Mojo</a></i><span class="reference-accessdate">.
