</li>
<li class="toclevel-2 tocsection-14"><a href="#Critical_response"><span class="tocnumber">7.2</span> <span class="toctext">Critical response</span></a></li>
<li class="toclevel-2 tocsection-15"><a href="#Accolades"><span class="tocnumber">7.3</span> <span class="toctext">Accolades</span></a></li>
</ul>
</li>
<li class="toclevel-1 tocsection-16"><a href="#Future"><span class="tocnumber">8</span> <span class="toctext">Future</span></a></li>
<li class="toclevel-1 tocsection-17"><a href="#Notes"><span class="tocnumber">9</span> <span class="toctext">Notes</span></a></li>
<li class="toclevel-1 tocsection-18"><a href="#References"><span class="tocnumber">10</span> <span class="toctext">References</span></a></li>
<li class="toclevel-1 tocsection-19"><a href="#External_links"><span class="tocnumber">11</span> <span class="toctext">External links</span></a></li>
</ul>
</div>
</div>
<h2><span class="mw-headline" id="Plot">Plot</span></h2>
<p>Twenty-three days after <a href="/wiki/Thanos_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Thanos</a> killed half of all life in the universe,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-Timeline_4-0"><a href="#cite_note-Timeline-4">[N 1]</a></sup> <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Carol_Danvers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Carol Danvers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Carol Danvers</a> rescues <a href="/wiki/Tony_Stark_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Tony Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Tony Stark</a> and <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Nebula_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Nebula (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Nebula</a> from deep space and they reunite with the remaining <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Avengers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Avengers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Avengers</a>—<a href="/wiki/Bruce_Banner_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Bruce Banner (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Bruce Banner</a>, <a href="/wiki/Steve_Rogers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Steve Rogers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Steve Rogers</a>, <a href="/wiki/Thor_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Thor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Thor</a>, <a href="/wiki/Natasha_Romanoff_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Natasha Romanoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Natasha Romanoff</a>, and <a href="/wiki/James_Rhodes_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="James Rhodes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">James Rhodes</a>—and <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Rocket_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Rocket (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Rocket</a> on Earth. Locating Thanos on an uninhabited planet, they plan to use the <a href="/wiki/Infinity_Stones" title="Infinity Stones">Infinity Stones</a> to reverse his actions, but Thanos already destroyed the Stones to prevent further use. Enraged, Thor decapitates Thanos.
</p><p>Five years later, in 2023, <a href="/wiki/Scott_Lang_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Scott Lang (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Scott Lang</a> escapes from the <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Quantum_realm_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Quantum realm (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">quantum realm</a>.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-Timeline2_5-0"><a href="#cite_note-Timeline2-5">[N 2]</a></sup> Reaching the Avengers' compound, he explains that he experienced only five hours while trapped. Theorizing the quantum realm could allow <a href="/wiki/Time_travel" title="Time travel">time travel</a>, they ask Stark to help them get the Stones from the past to reverse Thanos' actions in the present. Stark, Rocket, and Banner, who has since merged his intelligence with the Hulk's strength, build a time machine. Banner notes that changing the past does not affect their present; any changes create <a href="/wiki/Parallel_universes_in_fiction" title="Parallel universes in fiction">alternate realities</a>. Visiting the Asgardian refugees' settlement New Asgard, in Norway, Banner and Rocket recruit an overweight and despondent Thor. In Tokyo, Romanoff recruits <a href="/wiki/Clint_Barton_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Clint Barton (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Clint Barton</a>, who had become a vigilante after the death of his family.
</p><p>Banner, Lang, Rogers, and Stark travel to New York City during <a href="/wiki/Loki_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Loki (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Loki</a>'s attack in 2012.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-Timeline3_6-0"><a href="#cite_note-Timeline3-6">[N 3]</a></sup> At the <a href="/wiki/Sanctum_Sanctorum" title="Sanctum Sanctorum">Sanctum Sanctorum</a>, Banner convinces the <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Ancient_One_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Ancient One (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Ancient One</a> to give him the Time Stone by promising to return the various Stones to their proper points in time. At <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Stark_Tower_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Stark Tower (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Stark Tower</a>, Rogers retrieves the Mind Stone from <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/HYDRA_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="HYDRA (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Hydra</a> sleeper agents, but Stark and Lang's attempt to steal the Space Stone fails, allowing 2012-Loki to escape with it. Rogers and Stark travel to the <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/S.H.I.E.L.D._(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="S.H.I.E.L.D. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">S.H.I.E.L.D.</a> headquarters in 1970, where Stark obtains an earlier version of the Space Stone and encounters his father, <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Howard_Stark_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Howard Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Howard</a>. Rogers steals Pym Particles from <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Hank_Pym_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Hank Pym (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Hank Pym</a> to return to the present and spies his lost love, <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Peggy_Carter_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Peggy Carter (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Peggy Carter</a>.
</p><p>Meanwhile, Rocket and Thor travel to <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Asgard_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Asgard (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Asgard</a> in 2013;<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-Timeline4_7-0"><a href="#cite_note-Timeline4-7">[N 4]</a></sup> the former extracts the Reality Stone from <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Jane_Foster_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Jane Foster (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Jane Foster</a>, while the latter gets encouragement from his mother, <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Frigga_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Frigga (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Frigga</a>, and retrieves his old hammer, <a href="/wiki/Mjolnir_(comics)" title="Mjolnir (comics)">Mjolnir</a>. Barton, Romanoff, Nebula, and Rhodes travel to 2014; Nebula and Rhodes go to Morag and steal the Power Stone before <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Peter_Quill_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Peter Quill (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Peter Quill</a> can,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-Timeline5_8-0"><a href="#cite_note-Timeline5-8">[N 5]</a></sup> while Barton and Romanoff travel to Vormir. The Soul Stone's keeper, <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Red_Skull_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Red Skull (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Red Skull</a>, reveals it can only be acquired by sacrificing someone a loved one. Romanoff sacrifices herself, allowing Barton to get the Stone. Rhodes and Nebula attempt to return to their own time, but Nebula is incapacitated when her cybernetic implants link with her past self, allowing 2014-Thanos to learn his future self's success and the Avengers' attempt to undo it. 2014-Thanos sends 2014-Nebula forward in time to prepare for his arrival.
</p><p>Reuniting in the present, the Avengers place the Stones into a gauntlet that Stark, Banner, and Rocket built. Banner, having the most resistance to their gamma radiation, wields the gauntlet and reverses Thanos' disintegrations. Meanwhile, 2014-Nebula, impersonating her future self, uses the time machine to transport 2014-Thanos and his warship to the present, destroying the Avengers' compound in the process. Present-day Nebula convinces 2014-<a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Gamora_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Gamora (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Gamora</a> to betray Thanos, but is unable to convince 2014-Nebula and kills her. Thanos overpowers Stark, Thor, and a Mjolnir-wielding Rogers and summons his army from his warship to retrieve the Stones, intent on using them to destroy the universe and create a new one. A restored <a href="/wiki/Stephen_Strange_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Stephen Strange (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Stephen Strange</a> arrives with other sorcerers, the restored Avengers and <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Guardians of the Galaxy (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Guardians of the Galaxy</a>, the Ravagers, and the armies of <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Wakanda_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Wakanda (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Wakanda</a> and Asgard to fight Thanos' army. Danvers also shows up and destroys Thanos' warship, but Thanos overpowers her and seizes the gauntlet. Stark grabs the Stones and uses them to disintegrate Thanos and his army, but the strain of using the Stones kills him.
</p><p>Following Stark's funeral, Thor appoints <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Valkyrie_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Valkyrie (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Valkyrie</a> as the new ruler of New Asgard and joins the Guardians. Rogers returns the Stones and Mjolnir to their proper timelines and remains in the past to live with Carter. In the present, an elderly Rogers passes <a href="/wiki/Captain_America%27s_shield" title="Captain America's shield">his shield</a> to <a href="/wiki/Sam_Wilson_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Sam Wilson (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Sam Wilson</a>.
</p>
<h2><span class="mw-headline" id="Cast">Cast</span></h2>
<ul><li><a href="/wiki/Robert_Downey_Jr." title="Robert Downey Jr.">Robert Downey Jr.</a> as <a href="/wiki/Tony_Stark_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Tony Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Tony Stark / Iron Man</a>:<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-DowneyClarify_9-0"><a href="#cite_note-DowneyClarify-9">[4]</a></sup><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-RuddAvengers4_10-0"><a href="#cite_note-RuddAvengers4-10">[5]</a></sup> <br/>According to directors <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Joe_and_Anthony_Russo" title="Joe and Anthony Russo">Joe and Anthony Russo</a>, Downey was the only actor to receive the entire screenplay for the film.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-ScreenRantDowney_11-0"><a href="#cite_note-ScreenRantDowney-11">[6]</a></sup> Screenwriters <a href="/wiki/Christopher_Markus_and_Stephen_McFeely" title="Christopher Markus and Stephen McFeely">Christopher Markus and Stephen McFeely</a> knew that Stark's death was inevitable both as a "[move] to selflessness" and as an end to the "chapter" Stark started. They felt that his death was earned after granting him "the perfect retirement life," adding, "That's the life he's been striving for [...] They got married, they had a kid, it was great. It's a good death. It doesn't feel like a tragedy. It feels like a heroic, finished life."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-NYMarkusMcFeely_12-0"><a href="#cite_note-NYMarkusMcFeely-12">[7]</a></sup> Joe Russo explained that Stark "always knew he was going to die because he could never reconcile that notion in himself of not protecting the universe," and added that Stark was the most defiant among the Avengers since "Stark is the most formidable of all of them [...] because of his heart."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-IronManClose_13-0"><a href="#cite_note-IronManClose-13">[8]</a></sup> The Russos sought Downey's approval for Stark's arc, which they had developed since <i><a href="/wiki/Captain_America:_Civil_War" title="Captain America: Civil War">Captain America: Civil War</a></i> (2016).<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-DowneyArcApproval_14-0"><a href="#cite_note-DowneyArcApproval-14">[9]</a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Chris_Evans_(actor)" title="Chris Evans (actor)">Chris Evans</a> as <a href="/wiki/Steve_Rogers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Steve Rogers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Steve Rogers / Captain America</a>:<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-RuddAvengers4_10-1"><a href="#cite_note-RuddAvengers4-10">[5]</a></sup> <br/>Markus described Rogers as someone who is "moving toward some sort of <a href="/wiki/Enlightened_self-interest" title="Enlightened self-interest">enlightened self-interest</a>". McFeely knew Rogers "was going to get his dance" he promised <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Peggy_Carter_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Peggy Carter (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Peggy Carter</a> in <i><a href="/wiki/Captain_America:_The_First_Avenger" title="Captain America: The First Avenger">Captain America: The First Avenger</a></i> (2011), with McFeely said that Rogers "postponed a life" to "fulfill his duty", and McFeely "didn't think we were ever going to kill him", which is "not the [Rogers' story] arc." Instead, Rogers' arc is "finally get to put [his] shield down".<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-NYMarkusMcFeely_12-1"><a href="#cite_note-NYMarkusMcFeely-12">[7]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Patrick_Gorman_(American_actor)" title="Patrick Gorman (American actor)">Patrick Gorman</a> provided on-set reference for an elderly Steve Rogers.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-PatrickGorman_15-0"><a href="#cite_note-PatrickGorman-15">[10]</a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Mark_Ruffalo" title="Mark Ruffalo">Mark Ruffalo</a> as <a href="/wiki/Bruce_Banner_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Bruce Banner (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Bruce Banner / Hulk</a>:<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-RuffaloAvengers4_16-0"><a href="#cite_note-RuffaloAvengers4-16">[11]</a></sup> <br/>In this film, Banner has managed to balance his two sides with gamma experimentation, enabling him to combine his intelligence with the Hulk's strength and physical stature,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-ProfessorHulk2_17-0"><a href="#cite_note-ProfessorHulk2-17">[12]</a></sup> based on the "Professor Hulk" comic book identity.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-ProfessorHulk_18-0"><a href="#cite_note-ProfessorHulk-18">[13]</a></sup> Compared to other heroes, who were demotivated by their loss against Thanos, Banner is the only character to remain hopeful, with Anthony Russo explaining, "Banner is the sole character who is actually forging into a bright new future, trying to build something totally new and find something completely new [...] Banner is the one who is most heroic in a sense that he maintains his will to keep trying."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-WarriorThanos_19-0"><a href="#cite_note-WarriorThanos-19">[14]</a></sup> This concludes a character arc that was established in <i><a href="/wiki/Thor:_Ragnarok" title="Thor: Ragnarok">Thor: Ragnarok</a></i> (2017) and continued in <i>Avengers: Infinity War</i>.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-RuffaloHulkArc_20-0"><a href="#cite_note-RuffaloHulkArc-20">[15]</a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Chris_Hemsworth" title="Chris Hemsworth">Chris Hemsworth</a> as <a href="/wiki/Thor_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Thor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Thor</a>:<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-HemsworthEvans_21-0"><a href="#cite_note-HemsworthEvans-21">[16]</a></sup> <br/>Thor now wields a mystical axe known as Stormbreaker, after the destruction of his hammer, <a href="/wiki/Mjolnir_(comics)" title="Mjolnir (comics)">Mjolnir</a>, in <i>Thor: Ragnarok</i>.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-EWIWBTS_22-0"><a href="#cite_note-EWIWBTS-22">[17]</a></sup> In the film, Thor has become an overweight, drunken ruler of Asgard's refugees in <a href="/wiki/T%C3%B8nsberg" title="Tønsberg">Tønsberg</a>, Norway. Referencing this drastic character change, Hemsworth said, "I just had an opinion. I wanted to do something different this time. Each film I've wanted to, in particular, the last couple, and they were on board," and added, "We shot for many hours and days and discussed how far could we push (Thor) and what we could do different."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-DrunkThor_23-0"><a href="#cite_note-DrunkThor-23">[18]</a></sup> Anthony Russo added, "Even though there's a lot of fun to be had in the movie with his physical condition, it's not a gag. It's a manifestation of where he is on a character level, and we think it's one of the most relatable aspects of him. I mean, it's a very common sort of response to depression and pain."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-OverweightThor_24-0"><a href="#cite_note-OverweightThor-24">[19]</a></sup> Thor's story was his favorite arc, saying, "Part of Chris' magic as a comedic actor is his dedication to the depth of the character on a very earnest level [...] It's so devious and subversive when comedy is coming from a place of complete commitment and emotional complexity."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-ThorArc_25-0"><a href="#cite_note-ThorArc-25">[20]</a></sup> Hemsworth underwent around three hours of hair and makeup for the transformation, which also required him to wear a large silicone prosthetic suit; he called himself "<a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Lebowski" title="Lebowski">Lebowski</a> Thor" on set.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-LebowsChris_26-0"><a href="#cite_note-LebowsChris-26">[21]</a></sup> Initially, Thor was supposed to revert to his "old chiseled self" in the middle of <i>Endgame</i>, but Hemsworth successfully argued in favor of retaining Thor's new physique.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-LebowsChris_26-1"><a href="#cite_note-LebowsChris-26">[21]</a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Scarlett_Johansson" title="Scarlett Johansson">Scarlett Johansson</a> as <a href="/wiki/Natasha_Romanoff_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Natasha Romanoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Natasha Romanoff / Black Widow</a>:<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-JohanssonAvengers4_27-0"><a href="#cite_note-JohanssonAvengers4-27">[22]</a></sup><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-RuddAvengers4_10-2"><a href="#cite_note-RuddAvengers4-10">[5]</a></sup> <br/>At the beginning of the film, Romanoff continues to command several teams from around the galaxy in the Avengers headquarters, which Joe Russo explained stemmed from her inability to move on from their failure to stop Thanos, saying, "she's doing everything she can to try and hold the community together [...] She's the watcher on the wall still."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-ControWidow_28-0"><a href="#cite_note-ControWidow-28">[23]</a></sup> On the decision for Romanoff to sacrifice herself for Barton to acquire the Soul Stone to bring back everyone, Joe Russo stated that it was part of a larger theme exploring the desire to sacrifice, compared to the desire to protect in <i>Infinity War</i>; he says, "When she gets to that [Soul Stone] scene, I think she understands that the only way to bring the community back is for her to sacrifice herself."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-ControWidow_28-1"><a href="#cite_note-ControWidow-28">[23]</a></sup> McFeely stated, "Her journey, in our minds, had come to an end if she could get the Avengers back. She comes from such an abusive, terrible, mind-control background, so when she gets to Vormir and she has a chance to get the family back, that's a thing she would trade for."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-NYMarkusMcFeely_12-2"><a href="#cite_note-NYMarkusMcFeely-12">[7]</a></sup> To prepare for the film, Johansson underwent a high-intensity workout regimen, which included <a href="/wiki/Plyometrics" title="Plyometrics">plyometrics</a>, <a href="/wiki/Olympic_weightlifting" title="Olympic weightlifting">Olympic weightlifting</a>, and gymnastics, as well as a time-restricted eating diet, all under the supervision of her longtime trainer, Eric Johnson, with whom she had worked since <i><a href="/wiki/Iron_Man_2" title="Iron Man 2">Iron Man 2</a></i> (2010), the film which introduced her character.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-JohanssonPrepare_29-0"><a href="#cite_note-JohanssonPrepare-29">[24]</a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Jeremy_Renner" title="Jeremy Renner">Jeremy Renner</a> as <a href="/wiki/Clint_Barton_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Clint Barton (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Clint Barton / Hawkeye</a>:<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-RoninTrailer_30-0"><a href="#cite_note-RoninTrailer-30">[25]</a></sup> <br/> McFeely described Barton's dark turn as "a good example of people who had much stronger stories after the Snap."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-NYMarkusMcFeely_12-3"><a href="#cite_note-NYMarkusMcFeely-12">[7]</a></sup> The film's cold open, which features the disintegration of Barton's family, was initially supposed to be in <i>Infinity War</i> following Thanos' snap; however, it was moved to <i>Endgame</i> instead, with Markus explaining that it was "going to blunt the brutality of what [Thanos] did."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-NYMarkusMcFeely_12-4"><a href="#cite_note-NYMarkusMcFeely-12">[7]</a></sup> Joe Russo felt it was "a very tragic scene to open the movie with. It's one of the few scenes in the movie that actually makes me tear up when I watch it, because I think about my own family [...] And then you think about what would happen to you, as a father. You'd become very self-destructive."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-WarriorThanos_19-1"><a href="#cite_note-WarriorThanos-19">[14]</a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Don_Cheadle" title="Don Cheadle">Don Cheadle</a> as <a href="/wiki/James_Rhodes_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="James Rhodes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">James "Rhodey" Rhodes / War Machine</a>:<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-CheadleAvengers4_31-0"><a href="#cite_note-CheadleAvengers4-31">[26]</a></sup> <br/>Cheadle described Rhodes' newfound belonging as an Avenger as "not so much straddling one foot in the military. He's much more on the side of The Avengers than he was prior."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-CheadleInterview_32-0"><a href="#cite_note-CheadleInterview-32">[27]</a></sup> This is reflected on Rhodes' more instinctive and realist worldview in the midst of encountering the fantastic, with Cheadle explaining, "He's definitely got some 'what-the-eff-is-happening' [attitude,] more than maybe the rest of them do, given his background. But it's a trial by fire, and he's quickly adapted to what [the threat] is, rather than what he wishes it were."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-CheadleInterview_32-1"><a href="#cite_note-CheadleInterview-32">[27]</a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Paul_Rudd" title="Paul Rudd">Paul Rudd</a> as <a href="/wiki/Scott_Lang_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Scott Lang (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Scott Lang / Ant-Man</a>:<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-RuddAvengers4_10-3"><a href="#cite_note-RuddAvengers4-10">[5]</a></sup> <br/>Lang is portrayed by twins Bazlo and Loen LeClair as a baby, by Jackson A. Dunn at age 12, and by Lee Moore at age 93.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-TVGuideCast_33-0"><a href="#cite_note-TVGuideCast-33">[28]</a></sup> This was Moore's final film before his death in August 2018.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-MooreDeath_34-0"><a href="#cite_note-MooreDeath-34">[29]</a></sup> Markus explained that adding Lang helped with implementing time travel into the film, saying, "we had access to him in the second movie, and the fact that he was bringing a whole subset of technology that did have something to do with a different concept of time was like a birthday present."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-MarkusAntManTimeTravel_35-0"><a href="#cite_note-MarkusAntManTimeTravel-35">[30]</a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Brie_Larson" title="Brie Larson">Brie Larson</a> as <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Carol_Danvers_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Carol Danvers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Carol Danvers / Captain Marvel</a>:<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-LarsonAvengers4_36-0"><a href="#cite_note-LarsonAvengers4-36">[31]</a></sup><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-LarsonAvengers4EW_37-0"><a href="#cite_note-LarsonAvengers4EW-37">[32]</a></sup> <br/>Markus stated that Danvers' powers are on a scale that has not previously existed in the MCU and likened her personality to Rogers', "which is sort of a person who's right and knows they're right and doesn't really want to hear it when you tell them they're wrong".<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-MarkusMar2018_38-0"><a href="#cite_note-MarkusMar2018-38">[33]</a></sup> Danvers has little screen time in the film, which McFeely reasoned as "not the story we're trying to tell—it's the original Avengers dealing with loss and coming to a conclusion, and she's the new, fresh blood."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-NYMarkusMcFeely_12-5"><a href="#cite_note-NYMarkusMcFeely-12">[7]</a></sup> Larson filmed her scenes for <i>Endgame</i> before beginning work on her solo film <i><a href="/wiki/Captain_Marvel_(film)" title="Captain Marvel (film)">Captain Marvel</a></i> (2019), which was released first. <i>Captain Marvel</i> directors <a href="/wiki/Anna_Boden_and_Ryan_Fleck" title="Anna Boden and Ryan Fleck">Anna Boden and Ryan Fleck</a> were present for the filming of her scenes in <i>Endgame</i> and gave Danvers' characterization in the film their blessing.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-LarsonEndgameFirst_39-0"><a href="#cite_note-LarsonEndgameFirst-39">[34]</a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Karen_Gillan" title="Karen Gillan">Karen Gillan</a> as <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Nebula_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Nebula (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Nebula</a>:<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-Gillan_40-0"><a href="#cite_note-Gillan-40">[35]</a></sup> <br/>After being previously featured as an antagonist or an anti-hero in previous MCU films, Nebula undergoes a redemption arc in the film where she makes amends for her past actions, including an encounter with a past version of herself, with Gillan adding that she is "staring her former self in the face and it's really clear how far she's come from that angry, bitter and twisted person. She's starting to connect with other people and find some level of forgiveness."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-NebulaEnd_41-0"><a href="#cite_note-NebulaEnd-41">[36]</a></sup> Gillan guessed that Nebula would play a prominent role in the film when she realized that <i>Infinity War</i> and <i>Endgame</i> would be adapted from <i><a href="/wiki/The_Infinity_Gauntlet" title="The Infinity Gauntlet">The Infinity Gauntlet</a></i>, which she had previously read when she was initially cast as Nebula in <i><a href="/wiki/Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_(film)" title="Guardians of the Galaxy (film)">Guardians of the Galaxy</a></i> (2014).<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-NebulaEnd_41-1"><a href="#cite_note-NebulaEnd-41">[36]</a></sup> Gillan shared several scenes with Downey in the film's opening, and the two improvised most of their scenes together.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-NebulaEnd_41-2"><a href="#cite_note-NebulaEnd-41">[36]</a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Danai_Gurira" title="Danai Gurira">Danai Gurira</a> as <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Okoye_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Okoye (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Okoye</a><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-GuriraTopBillingPolygon_42-0"><a href="#cite_note-GuriraTopBillingPolygon-42">[37]</a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Benedict_Wong" title="Benedict Wong">Benedict Wong</a> as <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Wong_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Wong (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Wong</a><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-Wong_43-0"><a href="#cite_note-Wong-43">[38]</a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Jon_Favreau" title="Jon Favreau">Jon Favreau</a> as <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Happy_Hogan_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Happy Hogan (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Happy Hogan</a><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-FavreauPaltrowAvengers4_44-0"><a href="#cite_note-FavreauPaltrowAvengers4-44">[39]</a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Bradley_Cooper" title="Bradley Cooper">Bradley Cooper</a> as <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Rocket_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Rocket (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Rocket</a>:<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-ScreenRantCast_45-0"><a href="#cite_note-ScreenRantCast-45">[40]</a></sup> <br/><a href="/wiki/Sean_Gunn" title="Sean Gunn">Sean Gunn</a> was again the stand-in for Rocket during filming, with his acting and expressions serving as motion reference for the character.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-SeanGunnAvengers4_46-0"><a href="#cite_note-SeanGunnAvengers4-46">[41]</a></sup> Rocket's appearance in the film continues a story arc that was established by <i>Guardians of the Galaxy</i> writer-director and <i>Endgame</i> executive producer <a href="/wiki/James_Gunn" title="James Gunn">James Gunn</a> in the first two <i>Guardians of the Galaxy</i> films, was continued in <i>Infinity War</i> and <i>Endgame</i>, and will conclude in <i><a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_Vol._3" title="Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3">Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3</a></i> (2023).<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-GunnRocketArc_47-0"><a href="#cite_note-GunnRocketArc-47">[42]</a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Gwyneth_Paltrow" title="Gwyneth Paltrow">Gwyneth Paltrow</a> as <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Pepper_Potts_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Pepper Potts (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Pepper Potts</a>:<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-FavreauPaltrowAvengers4_44-1"><a href="#cite_note-FavreauPaltrowAvengers4-44">[39]</a></sup> Potts wears a weaponized <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Powered_exosuit" title="Powered exosuit">powered exosuit</a> of armor made for her by Stark, based on the Rescue armor.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-DeadlineNov2018_48-0"><a href="#cite_note-DeadlineNov2018-48">[43]</a></sup><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-Rescue_49-0"><a href="#cite_note-Rescue-49">[44]</a></sup> Paltrow said this would be her final major appearance in the MCU.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-PaltrowLast_50-0"><a href="#cite_note-PaltrowLast-50">[45]</a></sup></li>
<li><a href="/wiki/Josh_Brolin" title="Josh Brolin">Josh Brolin</a> as <a href="/wiki/Thanos_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Thanos</a>:<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-BrolinAvengers4_51-0"><a href="#cite_note-BrolinAvengers4-51">[46]</a></sup><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-IWSynopsis_52-0"><a href="#cite_note-IWSynopsis-52">[47]</a></sup> <br/>Joe Russo said that after Thanos was successful in <i>Avengers: Infinity War</i>, he is now "done. He did it. He's retired."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-ThanosDec2018_53-0"><a href="#cite_note-ThanosDec2018-53">[48]</a></sup> Markus and McFeely had difficulty in factoring the older, post-<i>Infinity War</i>, Thanos into the film due to the character already possessing the Infinity Stones, until executive producer Trinh Tran suggested that they kill Thanos in the film's first act.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-NYMarkusMcFeely_12-6"><a href="#cite_note-NYMarkusMcFeely-12">[7]</a></sup> Markus explained that the character's early death "reinforced Thanos' agenda. He was done [...] it was like, 'If I've got to die, I can die now.'"<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-NYMarkusMcFeely_12-7"><a href="#cite_note-NYMarkusMcFeely-12">[7]</a></sup> Thanos has less screen time in <i>Endgame</i> than in <i>Infinity War</i>, where he was considered the main character, as explained by McFeely: "We had to give ourselves permission to backseat the villain a little bit. I don't think anyone in the first half of the movie is going, 'Oh I wish there was a villain'. You're rolling around in the loss and the time heist, and you think it's sort of Avengers against nature." The younger version of Thanos was nicknamed "Warrior Thanos" by the filmmakers.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-WarriorThanos_19-2"><a href="#cite_note-WarriorThanos-19">[14]</a></sup> In addition to providing the voice for the character, Brolin performed <a href="/wiki/Motion_capture" title="Motion capture">motion capture</a> on set.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-BrolinMoCap_54-0"><a href="#cite_note-BrolinMoCap-54">[49]</a></sup> Joe Russo was a <a href="/wiki/Stand-in" title="Stand-in">stand-in</a> for Thanos for some scenes opposite Nebula.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-RussoThanosStandIn_55-0"><a href="#cite_note-RussoThanosStandIn-55">[50]</a></sup></li></ul>
<p>Several actors from <i>Infinity War</i> reprise their roles in <i>Endgame</i>, including <a href="/wiki/Benedict_Cumberbatch" title="Benedict Cumberbatch">Benedict Cumberbatch</a> as Dr. <a href="/wiki/Stephen_Strange_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Stephen Strange (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Stephen Strange</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-Cumberbatch_56-0"><a href="#cite_note-Cumberbatch-56">[51]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Chadwick_Boseman" title="Chadwick Boseman">Chadwick Boseman</a> as <a href="/wiki/T%27Challa_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="T'Challa (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">T'Challa / Black Panther</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-Boseman_57-0"><a href="#cite_note-Boseman-57">[52]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Tom_Holland" title="Tom Holland">Tom Holland</a> as <a href="/wiki/Peter_Parker_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Peter Parker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Peter Parker / Spider-Man</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-ScreenRantCast_45-1"><a href="#cite_note-ScreenRantCast-45">[40]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Zoe_Saldana" title="Zoe Saldana">Zoe Saldana</a> as <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Gamora_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Gamora (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Gamora</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-ScreenRantCast_45-2"><a href="#cite_note-ScreenRantCast-45">[40]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Elizabeth_Olsen" title="Elizabeth Olsen">Elizabeth Olsen</a> as <a href="/wiki/Wanda_Maximoff_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Wanda Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Wanda Maximoff / Scarlet Witch</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-PrattOlsen_58-0"><a href="#cite_note-PrattOlsen-58">[53]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Anthony_Mackie" title="Anthony Mackie">Anthony Mackie</a> as <a href="/wiki/Sam_Wilson_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Sam Wilson (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Sam Wilson / Falcon</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-MackieConfirm_59-0"><a href="#cite_note-MackieConfirm-59">[54]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Sebastian_Stan" title="Sebastian Stan">Sebastian Stan</a> as <a href="/wiki/Bucky_Barnes_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Bucky Barnes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Bucky Barnes / Winter Soldier</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-Stan_60-0"><a href="#cite_note-Stan-60">[55]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Tom_Hiddleston" title="Tom Hiddleston">Tom Hiddleston</a> as <a href="/wiki/Loki_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Loki (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Loki</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-HiddlestonInfinity_61-0"><a href="#cite_note-HiddlestonInfinity-61">[56]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Pom_Klementieff" title="Pom Klementieff">Pom Klementieff</a> as <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Mantis_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Mantis (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Mantis</a>,<sup class="reference" href="#cite_note-ScreenRantCast-45">[40]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Letitia_Wright" title="Letitia Wright">Letitia Wright</a> as <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Shuri_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Shuri (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Shuri</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-Wright_63-0"><a href="#cite_note-Wright-63">[58]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/William_Hurt" title="William Hurt">William Hurt</a> as <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Thaddeus_Ross_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Thaddeus Ross (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Thaddeus Ross</a>,<sup class="reference" title="M'Baku (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">M'Baku</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-Duke_64-0"><a href="#cite_note-Duke-64">[59]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Tom_Vaughan-Lawlor" title="Tom Vaughan-Lawlor">Tom Vaughan-Lawlor</a> as <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Ebony_Maw_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Ebony Maw (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Ebony Maw</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-ScreenRantCast_45-6"><a href="#cite_note-ScreenRantCast-45">[40]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Jacob_Batalon" title="Jacob Batalon">Jacob Batalon</a> as <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Ned_Leeds_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Ned Leeds (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Ned</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-RadioTimesCameos_65-0"><a href="#cite_note-RadioTimesCameos-65">[60]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Vin_Diesel" title="Vin Diesel">Vin Diesel</a> as <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Groot_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Groot (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Groot</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-ScreenRantCast_45-7"><a href="#cite_note-ScreenRantCast-45">[40]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Chris_Pratt" title="Chris Pratt">Chris Pratt</a> as <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Peter_Quill_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Peter Quill (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Peter Quill / Star-Lord</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-PrattOlsen_58-1"><a href="#cite_note-PrattOlsen-58">[53]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Samuel_L._Jackson" title="Samuel L. Jackson">Samuel L. Jackson</a> as <a href="/wiki/Nick_Fury_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Nick Fury (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Nick Fury</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-ScreenRantCast_45-8"><a href="#cite_note-ScreenRantCast-45">[40]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Ross_Marquand" title="Ross Marquand">Ross Marquand</a> as <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Red_Skull_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Red Skull (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Red Skull / Stonekeeper</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-ScreenRantCast_45-9"><a href="#cite_note-ScreenRantCast-45">[40]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Michael_James_Shaw" title="Michael James Shaw">Michael James Shaw</a> as <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Corvus_Glaive_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Corvus Glaive (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Corvus Glaive</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-ScreenRantCast_45-10"><a href="#cite_note-ScreenRantCast-45">[40]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Terry_Notary" title="Terry Notary">Terry Notary</a> as <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Cull_Obsidian" title="Cull Obsidian">Cull Obsidian</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-ScreenRantCast_45-11"><a href="#cite_note-ScreenRantCast-45">[40]</a></sup> and <a href="/wiki/Kerry_Condon" title="Kerry Condon">Kerry Condon</a> as the voice of Stark's suit A.I. <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/F.R.I.D.A.Y._(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="F.R.I.D.A.Y. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">F.R.I.D.A.Y.</a><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-Condon_66-0"><a href="#cite_note-Condon-66">[61]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Monique_Ganderton" title="Monique Ganderton">Monique Ganderton</a> again provides motion capture for <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Proxima_Midnight_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Proxima Midnight (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Proxima Midnight</a>.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-ScreenRantCast_45-12"><a href="#cite_note-ScreenRantCast-45">[40]</a></sup>
</p><p>Also reprising their roles from previous MCU films are <a href="/wiki/Evangeline_Lilly" title="Evangeline Lilly">Evangeline Lilly</a> as <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Hope_van_Dyne_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Hope van Dyne (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Hope van Dyne / Wasp</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-LillyConfirm_67-0"><a href="#cite_note-LillyConfirm-67">[62]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Tessa_Thompson" title="Tessa Thompson">Tessa Thompson</a> as <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Valkyrie_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Valkyrie (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Valkyrie</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-RadioTimesCameos_65-1"><a href="#cite_note-RadioTimesCameos-65">[60]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Rene_Russo" href="/wiki/Howard_Stark_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Howard Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Howard Stark</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-RadioTimesCameos_65-3"><a href="#cite_note-RadioTimesCameos-65">[60]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Tilda_Swinton" title="Tilda Swinton">Tilda Swinton</a> as the <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Ancient_One_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Ancient One (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Ancient One</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-RadioTimesCameos_65-4"><a href="#cite_note-RadioTimesCameos-65">[60]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Hayley_Atwell" title="Hayley Atwell">Hayley Atwell</a> as <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Peggy_Carter_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)" title="Peggy Carter (Marvel Cinematic Universe)">Peggy Carter</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-RadioTimesCameos_65-5"><a href="#cite_note-RadioTimesCameos-65">[60]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Marisa_Tomei" title="Marisa Tomei">Marisa Tomei</a> as <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/May_Parker_(Marvel_Cinematic_Universe)"
