style="width:94px;max-width:94px"><div class="thumbimage" style="height:110px;overflow:hidden"><a class="image" href="/wiki/File:Mark_Ruffalo_in_2017_by_Gage_Skidmore.jpg"><img alt="" data-file-height="2183" data-file-width="1858" decoding="async" height="108" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8a/Mark_Ruffalo_in_2017_by_Gage_Skidmore.jpg/92px-Mark_Ruffalo_in_2017_by_Gage_Skidmore.jpg" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8a/Mark_Ruffalo_in_2017_by_Gage_Skidmore.jpg/138px-Mark_Ruffalo_in_2017_by_Gage_Skidmore.jpg 1.5x, //upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8a/Mark_Ruffalo_in_2017_by_Gage_Skidmore.jpg/184px-Mark_Ruffalo_in_2017_by_Gage_Skidmore.jpg 2x" width="92"/></a></div></div></div><div class="trow"><div class="tsingle" style="width:94px;max-width:94px"><div class="thumbimage" style="height:110px;overflow:hidden"><a class="image" href="/wiki/File:Chris_Hemsworth_by_Gage_Skidmore_2_(cropped).jpg"><img alt="" data-file-height="2605" data-file-width="1910" decoding="async" height="125" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e8/Chris_Hemsworth_by_Gage_Skidmore_2_%28cropped%29.jpg/92px-Chris_Hemsworth_by_Gage_Skidmore_2_%28cropped%29.jpg" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e8/Chris_Hemsworth_by_Gage_Skidmore_2_%28cropped%29.jpg/138px-Chris_Hemsworth_by_Gage_Skidmore_2_%28cropped%29.jpg 1.5x, //upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e8/Chris_Hemsworth_by_Gage_Skidmore_2_%28cropped%29.jpg/184px-Chris_Hemsworth_by_Gage_Skidmore_2_%28cropped%29.jpg 2x" width="92"/></a></div></div><div class="tsingle" style="width:97px;max-width:97px"><div class="thumbimage" style="height:110px;overflow:hidden"><a class="image" href="/wiki/File:Scarlett_Johansson_by_Gage_Skidmore_2_(cropped).jpg"><img alt="" data-file-height="2928" data-file-width="1937" decoding="async" height="144" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/60/Scarlett_Johansson_by_Gage_Skidmore_2_%28cropped%29.jpg/95px-Scarlett_Johansson_by_Gage_Skidmore_2_%28cropped%29.jpg" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/60/Scarlett_Johansson_by_Gage_Skidmore_2_%28cropped%29.jpg/143px-Scarlett_Johansson_by_Gage_Skidmore_2_%28cropped%29.jpg 1.5x, //upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/60/Scarlett_Johansson_by_Gage_Skidmore_2_%28cropped%29.jpg/190px-Scarlett_Johansson_by_Gage_Skidmore_2_%28cropped%29.jpg 2x" width="95"/></a></div></div><div class="tsingle" style="width:94px;max-width:94px"><div class="thumbimage" style="height:110px;overflow:hidden"><a class="image" href="/wiki/File:Jeremy_Renner_by_Gage_Skidmore.jpg"><img alt="" data-file-height="2752" data-file-width="2094" decoding="async" height="121" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/32/Jeremy_Renner_by_Gage_Skidmore.jpg/92px-Jeremy_Renner_by_Gage_Skidmore.jpg" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/32/Jeremy_Renner_by_Gage_Skidmore.jpg/138px-Jeremy_Renner_by_Gage_Skidmore.jpg 1.5x, //upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/32/Jeremy_Renner_by_Gage_Skidmore.jpg/184px-Jeremy_Renner_by_Gage_Skidmore.jpg 2x" width="92"/></a></div></div></div><div class="trow" style="display:flex"><div class="thumbcaption">The performances of (top, L to R) <a href="/wiki/Robert_Downey_Jr." title="Robert Downey Jr.">Robert Downey Jr.</a>, <a href="/wiki/Chris_Evans_(actor)" title="Chris Evans (actor)">Chris Evans</a>, <a href="/wiki/Mark_Ruffalo" title="Mark Ruffalo">Mark Ruffalo</a>, (bottom, L to R) <a href="/wiki/Chris_Hemsworth" title="Chris Hemsworth">Chris Hemsworth</a>, <a href="/wiki/Scarlett_Johansson" title="Scarlett Johansson">Scarlett Johansson</a>, and <a href="/wiki/Jeremy_Renner" title="Jeremy Renner">Jeremy Renner</a> as the original six Avengers were widely praised by critics<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-CBRReviewRoundup_188-0"><a href="#cite_note-CBRReviewRoundup-188">[183]</a></sup></div></div></div></div>
<p>The <a href="/wiki/Review_aggregator" title="Review aggregator">review aggregator</a> website <a href="/wiki/Rotten_Tomatoes" title="Rotten Tomatoes">Rotten Tomatoes</a> reported an approval rating of 94% with an average score of 8.20/10, based on 538 reviews. The website's critical consensus reads, "Exciting, entertaining, and emotionally impactful, <i>Avengers: Endgame</i> does whatever it takes to deliver a satisfying finale to Marvel's epic Infinity Saga."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-RottenTomatoes_189-0"><a href="#cite_note-RottenTomatoes-189">[184]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Metacritic" title="Metacritic">Metacritic</a>, which uses a weighted average, assigned the film a score of 78 out of 100 based on 57 critics, indicating "generally favorable reviews".<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-Metacritic_190-0"><a href="#cite_note-Metacritic-190">[185]</a></sup> Audiences polled by <a href="/wiki/CinemaScore" title="CinemaScore">CinemaScore</a> gave the film a rare "A+" grade on an A+ to F scale, the third Marvel film to earn the score after <i><a href="/wiki/The_Avengers_(2012_film)" title="The Avengers (2012 film)">The Avengers</a></i> and <i><a href="/wiki/Black_Panther_(film)" title="Black Panther (film)">Black Panther</a></i> (2018), and those at <a href="/wiki/PostTrak" title="PostTrak">PostTrak</a> gave the film 5 out of 5 stars and an 85% "definite recommend".<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-DEADopening_171-1"><a href="#cite_note-DEADopening-171">[166]</a></sup>
</p><p>Writing for <a href="/wiki/NPR" title="NPR">NPR</a>, <a href="/wiki/Glen_Weldon" title="Glen Weldon">Glen Weldon</a> gave the film a positive review and found the film to be a worthy sequel to its predecessor, stating, "The Russos' decision to stick close to the experiences of the remaining Avengers proves a rewarding one, as they've expressly constructed the film as an extended victory lap for the Marvel Cinematic Universe writ large. Got a favorite character from any Marvel movie over the past decade, no matter how obscure? Prepare to get serviced, fan."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-NPRWeldon_191-0"><a href="#cite_note-NPRWeldon-191">[186]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Peter_Travers" title="Peter Travers">Peter Travers</a> in his review for <i><a href="/wiki/Rolling_Stone" title="Rolling Stone">Rolling Stone</a></i> gave the film 4 out of 5 stars, saying, "You don't have to make jokes about the clichéd time travel plot – the film is ready, willing and able to make its own, with <i><a href="/wiki/Back_to_the_Future" title="Back to the Future">Back to the Future</a></i> coming in for a serious ribbing."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-RollingStoneTravers_192-0"><a href="#cite_note-RollingStoneTravers-192">[187]</a></sup>
</p><p>Peter Debruge of <i><a href="/wiki/Variety_(magazine)" title="Variety (magazine)">Variety</a></i> wrote, "After the must-see showdown that was <i>Infinity War</i>, the Russo brothers deliver a more fan-facing three-hour follow-up, rewarding loyalty to Marvel Cinematic Universe."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-VarietyReview_193-0"><a href="#cite_note-VarietyReview-193">[188]</a></sup> J. R. Kinnard of <i><a href="/wiki/PopMatters" title="PopMatters">PopMatters</a></i> wrote, "Big budget action filmmaking doesn't get much better than this."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-PM_194-0"><a href="#cite_note-PM-194">[189]</a></sup> Todd McCarthy of <i><a href="/wiki/The_Hollywood_Reporter" title="The Hollywood Reporter">The Hollywood Reporter</a></i> said, "[W]hat comes across most strongly here, oddly enough for an effects-driven comic-book-derived film, is the character acting, especially from Downey, Ruffalo, Evans, Hemsworth, Brolin, and Paul Rudd".<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-THRReview_195-0"><a href="#cite_note-THRReview-195">[190]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Richard_Roeper" title="Richard Roeper">Richard Roeper</a>, writing for the <i><a href="/wiki/Chicago_Sun-Times" title="Chicago Sun-Times">Chicago Sun-Times</a></i>, gave the film four stars and praised its "emotional punch", as well as the "funny, well-paced, smart, expertly rendered screenplay by Christopher Markus and Stephen McFeely, crisp direction from Anthony Russo and Joe Russo, [...] and the universally stellar performances".<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-SunTimesReview_196-0"><a href="#cite_note-SunTimesReview-196">[191]</a></sup>
</p><p><i><a href="/wiki/The_New_York_Times" title="The New York Times">The New York Times</a></i> reviewer <a href="/wiki/A._O._Scott" title="A. O. Scott">A. O. Scott</a> gave the film a positive though guarded review, stating, "<i>Endgame</i> is a monument to adequacy, a fitting capstone to an enterprise that figured out how to be good enough for enough people enough of the time. Not that it's really over, of course: Disney and Marvel are still working out new wrinkles in the time-money continuum. But the Russos do provide the sense of an ending, a chance to appreciate what has been done before the timelines reset and we all get back to work."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-NewYorkTimesScott_197-0"><a href="#cite_note-NewYorkTimesScott-197">[192]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Justin_Chang" title="Justin Chang">Justin Chang</a> of the <i><a href="/wiki/Los_Angeles_Times" title="Los Angeles Times">Los Angeles Times</a></i> wrote that "<i>Avengers: Endgame</i> achieves and earns its climactic surge of feeling, even as it falls just short of real catharsis".<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-LATReview_198-0"><a href="#cite_note-LATReview-198">[193]</a></sup> Some have noted the film as a notable improvement over its predecessor, <i>Avengers: Infinity War</i>, such as Brian Tallerico of <i><a href="/wiki/RogerEbert.com" title="RogerEbert.com">RogerEbert.com</a></i>, who stated that <i>Endgame</i> is "a more patient, focused film [than <i>Infinity War</i>], even as its plot draws in elements of a dozen other movies."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-RogerEbertTallerico_199-0"><a href="#cite_note-RogerEbertTallerico-199">[194]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Matt_Zoller_Seitz" title="Matt Zoller Seitz">Matt Zoller Seitz</a>, also of <i>RogerEbert.com</i>, gave the film a positive assessment as compared with <i>Infinity War</i>, which he considered "too crowded, too rushed and yet too long". Seitz stated that <i>Endgame</i> is "a heartfelt and satisfying experience", along with being a "surprisingly relaxed, character-driven, self-aware yet sincere comedy [for] two-thirds of [the film]. Much of the script suggests a laid-back <a href="/wiki/Richard_Linklater" title="Richard Linklater">Richard Linklater</a> movie with superheroes".<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-RogerEbertSeitz_200-0"><a href="#cite_note-RogerEbertSeitz-200">[195]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Joe_Morgenstern" title="Joe Morgenstern">Joe Morgenstern</a> of <i><a href="/wiki/The_Wall_Street_Journal" title="The Wall Street Journal">The Wall Street Journal</a></i> acknowledged the unique achievement that <i>Avengers: Endgame</i> accomplished as the conclusion of the Infinity Saga, calling the final battle "inevitably unwieldy [...] but thrilling all the same, and followed by a delicate <a class="extiw" href="https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/coda" title="wikt:coda">coda</a>. So many stories. So many adventures. So much to sort out before the next cycle starts."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-MorgensternEndgame_201-0"><a href="#cite_note-MorgensternEndgame-201">[196]</a></sup>
</p><p><a href="/wiki/Richard_Brody" title="Richard Brody">Richard Brody</a>, writing for <i><a href="/wiki/The_New_Yorker" title="The New Yorker">The New Yorker</a></i>, was more critical of the film, opining that the good acting was not matched by comparable skill from the directors. He said, "The Russos have peculiarly little sense of visual pleasure, little sense of beauty, little sense of metaphor, little aptitude for texture or composition; their spectacular conceit is purely one of scale, which is why their finest moments are quiet and dramatic ones".<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-NewYorkerBrody_202-0"><a href="#cite_note-NewYorkerBrody-202">[197]</a></sup> <a href="/wiki/Anthony_Lane" title="Anthony Lane">Anthony Lane</a> of <i>The New Yorker</i> gave the film a compromising review, finding it to be overdeveloped and overwrought, stating, "The one thing you do need to know about <i>Avengers: Endgame</i> is that it runs for a little over three hours, and that you can easily duck out during the middle hour, do some shopping, and slip back into your seat for the climax. You won't have missed a thing."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-NewYorkerLane_203-0"><a href="#cite_note-NewYorkerLane-203">[198]</a></sup>
</p>
<h3><span class="mw-headline" id="Accolades">Accolades</span></h3>
<div class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" role="note">Main article: <a href="/wiki/List_of_accolades_received_by_Avengers:_Endgame" title="List of accolades received by Avengers: Endgame">List of accolades received by Avengers: Endgame</a></div>
<p><i>Avengers: Endgame</i> was nominated for one <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Academy_Award" title="Academy Award">Academy Award</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-Oscars_204-0"><a href="#cite_note-Oscars-204">[199]</a></sup> one <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Annie_Award" title="Annie Award">Annie Award</a> (won),<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-AnnieAwards_205-0"><a href="#cite_note-AnnieAwards-205">[200]</a></sup> one <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/British_Academy_Film_Award" title="British Academy Film Award">British Academy Film Award</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-BAFTA_206-0"><a href="#cite_note-BAFTA-206">[201]</a></sup> three <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Critics%27_Choice_Awards" title="Critics' Choice Awards">Critics' Choice Awards</a> (winning two),<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-CriticsChoice_207-0"><a href="#cite_note-CriticsChoice-207">[202]</a></sup> one <a href="/wiki/Grammy_Award" title="Grammy Award">Grammy Award</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-Grammys_208-0"><a href="#cite_note-Grammys-208">[203]</a></sup> one <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Hollywood_Film_Award" title="Hollywood Film Award">Hollywood Film Award</a> (won),<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-HollywoodFilmAwards_209-0"><a href="#cite_note-HollywoodFilmAwards-209">[204]</a></sup> four <a href="/wiki/MTV_Movie_%26_TV_Awards" title="MTV Movie &amp; TV Awards">MTV Movie &amp; TV Awards</a> (winning three),<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-MTVAwards_210-0"><a href="#cite_note-MTVAwards-210">[205]</a></sup> seven <a href="/wiki/People%27s_Choice_Awards" title="People's Choice Awards">People's Choice Awards</a> (winning three),<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-PeoplesChoice_211-0"><a href="#cite_note-PeoplesChoice-211">[206]</a></sup> two <a href="/wiki/Satellite_Awards" title="Satellite Awards">Satellite Awards</a>,<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-SatelliteAwards_212-0"><a href="#cite_note-SatelliteAwards-212">[207]</a></sup> fourteen <a href="/wiki/Saturn_Awards" title="Saturn Awards">Saturn Awards</a> (winning six),<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-SaturnAwardsNom_213-0"><a href="#cite_note-SaturnAwardsNom-213">[208]</a></sup><sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-SaturnAwardsWon_214-0"><a href="#cite_note-SaturnAwardsWon-214">[209]</a></sup> one <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Screen_Actors_Guild_Award" title="Screen Actors Guild Award">Screen Actors Guild Award</a> (won),<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-SAGAwards_215-0"><a href="#cite_note-SAGAwards-215">[210]</a></sup> and nine <a href="/wiki/Teen_Choice_Awards" title="Teen Choice Awards">Teen Choice Awards</a> (winning four),<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-TeenChoice_216-0"><a href="#cite_note-TeenChoice-216">[211]</a></sup> among others. The film was <a href="/wiki/Wikipedia:2019_Top_50_Report" title="Wikipedia:2019 Top 50 Report">the most-viewed Wikipedia article of 2019</a>, garnering 44.2 million page views.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-TopWikipediaArticle2019_217-0"><a href="#cite_note-TopWikipediaArticle2019-217">[212]</a></sup>
</p>
<h2><span class="mw-headline" id="Future">Future</span></h2>
<div class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" role="note">Further information: <a href="/wiki/List_of_Marvel_Cinematic_Universe_films#Future" title="List of Marvel Cinematic Universe films">List of Marvel Cinematic Universe films § Future</a></div>
<p>In May 2018, <a class="mw-redirect" href="/wiki/Disney" title="Disney">Disney</a> CEO <a href="/wiki/Bob_Iger" title="Bob Iger">Bob Iger</a> said of Marvel's plans beyond <i>Endgame</i>, "I'm guessing we will try our hand at what I'll call a new franchise beyond <i>Avengers</i>, but that doesn't necessarily mean you won't see more <i>Avengers</i> down the road. We just haven't made any announcements about that." Iger added, "Given the popularity of the characters and given the popularity of the franchise, I don't think people should conclude there will never be another <i>Avengers</i> movie."<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-IgerTHR_218-0"><a href="#cite_note-IgerTHR-218">[213]</a></sup> Shortly after the film's premiere, the Russo brothers said they were not opposed to returning to the MCU in the future due to their positive relationship with Marvel Studios, but did not plan to do so at that time.<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-RussosFuture_219-0"><a href="#cite_note-RussosFuture-219">[214]</a></sup>
</p>
<h2><span class="mw-headline" id="Notes">Notes</span></h2>
<style data-mw-deduplicate="TemplateStyles:r1011085734">.mw-parser-output .reflist{font-size:90%;margin-bottom:0.5em;list-style-type:decimal}.mw-parser-output .reflist .references{font-size:100%;margin-bottom:0;list-style-type:inherit}.mw-parser-output .reflist-columns-2{column-width:30em}.mw-parser-output .reflist-columns-3{column-width:25em}.mw-parser-output .reflist-columns{margin-top:0.3em}.mw-parser-output .reflist-columns ol{margin-top:0}.mw-parser-output .reflist-columns li{page-break-inside:avoid;break-inside:avoid-column}.mw-parser-output .reflist-upper-alpha{list-style-type:upper-alpha}.mw-parser-output .reflist-upper-roman{list-style-type:upper-roman}.mw-parser-output .reflist-lower-alpha{list-style-type:lower-alpha}.mw-parser-output .reflist-lower-greek{list-style-type:lower-greek}.mw-parser-output .reflist-lower-roman{list-style-type:lower-roman}</style><div class="reflist">
<div class="mw-references-wrap"><ol class="references">
<li id="cite_note-Timeline-4"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-Timeline_4-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text">As depicted in the 2018 film <i><a href="/wiki/Avengers:_Infinity_War" title="Avengers: Infinity War">Avengers: Infinity War</a></i>.</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Timeline2-5"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-Timeline2_5-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text">In which he was trapped at the end of the 2018 film <i><a href="/wiki/Ant-Man_and_the_Wasp" title="Ant-Man and the Wasp">Ant-Man and the Wasp</a></i>.</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Timeline3-6"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-Timeline3_6-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text">During the events of the 2012 film <i><a href="/wiki/The_Avengers_(2012_film)" title="The Avengers (2012 film)">The Avengers</a></i>.</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Timeline4-7"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-Timeline4_7-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text">During the events of the 2013 film <i><a href="/wiki/Thor:_The_Dark_World" title="Thor: The Dark World">Thor: The Dark World</a></i>.</span>
</li>
<li id="cite_note-Timeline5-8"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-Timeline5_8-0">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text">As depicted in the 2014 film <i><a href="/wiki/Guardians_of_the_Galaxy_(film)" title="Guardians of the Galaxy (film)">Guardians of the Galaxy</a></i>.</span>
</li>
</ol></div></div>

</div></td></tr></tbody></table></div>

<li><i><a href="/wiki/Mulan_(2020_film)" title="Mulan (2020 film)">Mulan</a></i> (2020)</li></ul>

## Identify a tag you could use to target the info

## Eff that, use pd.read_html

APPENDIX#

Other HTML/CSS Use Cases#

Adding/controlling images and alignment of text#

  • Add an image hosted on github by grabbing grabbing the raw link.

  • Let’s add an image hosted on github to our notebook (great example for making blog posts).

  1. Go to the repo’s website, click on the image file.

  2. Click download and copy the raw.githubsercontent.link.

6. ipywidget layouts (e.g. fsds.ihelp_menu)#

## 4. ipywidgets Example
try: 
    import fsds as fs
except:
    !pip install -U fsds 
    import fsds as fs

fs.ihelp_menu(fs.ihelp_menu)

Other Text-Related Tips#

Sidebar: Using RegularExpressions to sift through the content.#

import re
regexp = re.compile(r"(\$\d{1,})\.(\d{2})")
regexp

found_text = regexp.findall(all_text)
found_text

tag0=tags[0]
target = tag0.contents
target = ' '.join(target)
target

  • Best Hands On Tester for Regex:

    • https://regex101.com/

    • Select “Python” on the left side of the page.

    • Paste the text you want to sift through in the large center window.

    • Type your expression in the top center window.

    • It will highlight the text that matches your regular expression in the big center panel.

  • Cheatsheet for Regex Symbols:

    • https://www.debuggex.com/cheatsheet/regex/python

import re
price =  re.compile("(\$\d\,\d*\.\d{2})")
price.findall(target)

Text Formatting with f-strings#

import requests
url = 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stock_market'

response = requests.get(url, timeout=3)
print('Status code: ',response.status_code)
if response.status_code==200:
    print('Connection successfull.\n\n')
else: 
    print('Error. Check status code table.\n\n')    

    
# Print out the contents of a request's response
print(f"{'---'*20}\n\tContents of Response.items():\n{'---'*20}")

for k,v in response.headers.items():
    print(f"{k:{25}}: {v:{40}}") # Note: add :{number} inside of a    

for k,v in response.headers.items():
    print(f"{k:{30}}:{v:{20}}") # Note: add :{number} inside of a

Sidebar Notes - Explaining The Above Text Printing/Formatting:**#

  • You can repeat strings by using multiplication

    • '---'*20 will repeat the dashed lines 20 times

  • You can determine how much space is alloted for a variable when using f-strings

    • Add a :{##} after the variable to specify the allocated width

    • Add a > before the {##} to force alignment

    • Add another symbol (like ‘.’’ or ‘-’) before > to add guiding-line/placeholder (like in a table of contents)

print(f"Status code: {response.status_code}")
print(f"Status code: {response.status_code:>{20}}")
print(f"Status code: {response.status_code:->{20}}")

# Returns:
Status code: 200
Status code:                  200
Status code: -----------------200

Recommended packages/tools to use#

  1. fake_useragent

    • pip-installable module that conveniently supplies fake user agent information to use in your request headers.

    • recommended by udemy course

  2. lxml

    • popular pip installable html parser (recommended by Udemy course)

    • using 'html.parser' in requests.get() did not work for me, I had to install lxml

# !pip install fake_useragent
# !pip install lxml

# import fake_useragent
# import lxml
